findings.title: Android top bar always renders a back chevron (ChevronLeft); design is tab-root with no back button
findings.designSays: TopBar in scheduling-hub-frames.jsx line 89: left slot is a 36×36 inert spacer div with no icon or click handler. Hub is a tab-root destination — no back navigation.
findings.implementationSays: HubTopBar (SchedulingHubScreen.kt lines 160–168) always renders a 36dp Box with .clickable(onClickLabel='Back') containing PantopusIcon.ChevronLeft, regardless of canEdit state. iOS correctly passes leading: .none to SetupTopBar (SchedulingHubScreen.swift line 30) and hides the back button with .navigationBarBackButtonHidden(true).
findings.evidence: scheduling-hub-frames.jsx:89 (empty spacer div) vs SchedulingHubScreen.kt:164 (.clickable onClickLabel='Back') and :168 (PantopusIcon.ChevronLeft). iOS contrast: SchedulingHubScreen.swift:30 (leading: .none).

findings.title: SchedulingSummaryCard (A5 stats card) is shown first in loaded state on all three platforms but is absent from design frames entirely
findings.designSays: All 6 frames in scheduling-hub-frames.jsx (FrameDefault lines 526–563, FramePaused 707–746, FrameHome 752–788, FramePermission 794–832, FrameEmpty 569–643, FrameSkeleton 649–701) contain no summary/stats card. The loaded body order in FrameDefault is: BookingLinkCard → PauseRow → SectionHeader 'Today & upcoming' → agenda rows → SectionHeader 'Manage' → QuickRows → FooterCTA.
findings.implementationSays: All three platforms render a summary/stats card as the first element in the loaded body, above the BookingLinkCard. iOS: summaryCard computed var (SchedulingHubScreen.swift:116–129) called at loadedBody line 84, before HubLinkCard at line 89. Android: SummaryCard() (SchedulingHubScreen.kt:258–264) rendered before BookingLinkCard at line 271. Web: <SchedulingSummaryCard> (SchedulingHub.tsx:254–258) rendered before <BookingLinkCard> at line 259.
findings.evidence: scheduling-hub-frames.jsx:526–563 (no summary card in FrameDefault) vs SchedulingHubScreen.swift:84+116–129, SchedulingHubScreen.kt:252–264, SchedulingHub.tsx:254–258.

findings.title: Web: missing pinned footer CTA ('Share booking link' / 'Resume bookings')
findings.designSays: FooterCTA component (scheduling-hub-frames.jsx:497–513) is a position:absolute pinned-bottom element rendered in FrameDefault (line 560), FramePaused (line 743), and FrameHome (line 785). It shows the pillar-accented primary CTA ('Share booking link' when active, 'Resume bookings' when paused).
findings.implementationSays: SchedulingHub.tsx loaded body (lines 253–281) renders SchedulingSummaryCard, BookingLinkCard, AcceptingBookingsCard, AgendaSection, and ManageRows in a plain div with no sticky/fixed footer. No pinned CTA exists anywhere in the loaded state. The share action is only accessible via SchedulingSummaryCard's onShare callback (line 257), not a standalone footer button. iOS and Android both implement the footer: iOS HubFooterCTA (SchedulingHubScreen.swift:110, gated by canEdit), Android HubFooterCta (SchedulingHubScreen.kt:135, gated by showFooter).
findings.evidence: scheduling-hub-frames.jsx:497–513 (FooterCTA), :560 (used in FrameDefault) vs SchedulingHub.tsx:253–281 (no footer element). iOS match: SchedulingHubScreen.swift:109–111. Android match: SchedulingHubScreen.kt:132–135.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: All three platforms share the undocumented summary card (A5 stats card) at the top of the loaded state, which is absent from the design. Android alone has the spurious back chevron in the top bar — iOS correctly uses leading: .none and Android has no equivalent gate. Web alone is missing the pinned footer CTA — iOS and Android both implement it and gate it on canEdit. iOS is the closest to design on top-bar chrome; Android is closest overall minus the back-button regression and summary card; web diverges most with both the summary card addition and the missing footer CTA.
screens.verifierSummary: All three major/high-confidence findings were independently confirmed against source. (1) CONFIRMED: Android HubTopBar unconditionally renders PantopusIcon.ChevronLeft at line 168 with a clickable Back handler at line 164; the design's TopBar has an inert 36×36 spacer at line 89. iOS correctly passes leading: .none at line 30. (2) CONFIRMED: No summary/stats card exists in any of the 6 design frames; all three platforms render it first in the loaded body (iOS:84+116–129, Android:258–264, Web:254). (3) CONFIRMED: The web loaded path (SchedulingHub.tsx:253–281) has no fixed/sticky/pinned footer CTA; iOS has HubFooterCTA at line 110 and Android has HubFooterCta at line 135, both matching the design's FooterCTA. Per-platform status recomputed from major to 'major' for all three platforms (the draft rated iOS and web 'minor' but failed to account for the confirmed summary-card major finding that affects all platforms). No findings were refuted or downgraded. No additional findings were added (the other 14 minor/nit findings from the full draft were not re-audited and are carried forward as-is per auditor instructions).

findings.title: Android success hero uses green accent instead of personal (sky) pillar accent
findings.designSays: Success hero outer circle = radial-gradient(primary50 → primary100); inner filled circle = primary600 (#0284c7 sky) with box-shadow rgba(2,132,199,0.32); check icon color = #fff. Design uses P.primary600 throughout (scheduling-setup-frames.jsx:402–410).
findings.implementationSays: SuccessHeroBlock.kt (a shared wizard block, not pillar-aware) hardcodes PantopusColors.successBg (green #F0FDF4) as the outer circle background and PantopusColors.success (green #059669) as the check icon tint. No pillar parameter is accepted or passed; the sky identity is entirely ignored.
findings.evidence: design: scheduling-setup-frames.jsx:403 radial-gradient(primary50, primary100), :406 background:P.primary600 | android: SuccessHeroBlock.kt:55 .background(PantopusColors.successBg), SuccessHeroBlock.kt:62 tint = PantopusColors.success | FirstRunWizardScreen.kt:78 calls SuccessHeroBlock with no pillar arg

findings.title: Android Sunday weekday key is 7 instead of backend ISO 0, mismatching iOS, web, and backend
findings.designSays: n/a — no design; functional correctness: backend ISO weekday 0 = Sunday (confirmed by web weeklyHours.ts:2 comment and weeklyHours.ts:37 weekday: 0 for Sunday entry)
findings.implementationSays: Android DAY_LABELS maps 7 to 'Sunday' and DEFAULT_HOURS maps 7 to false; toggling Sunday on Android sends weekday key 7 to the backend, which does not match the ISO contract (0 = Sunday) used by iOS (hoursEnabled: [0: false]) and web (weekday: 0). This means Android Sunday toggles are silently lost or corrupt backend state.
findings.evidence: android: WizardSteps.kt:59 `7 to "Sunday"`, FirstRunWizardViewModel.kt:53 `7 to false` | ios: FirstRunWizardModel.swift:38 `0: false` | web: weeklyHours.ts:2 `// weekday is backend ISO (0 = Sunday … 6 = Saturday)`, weeklyHours.ts:37 `{ weekday: 0, label: "Sunday", … }`

findings.title: iOS and Android missing resume banner on step 3 re-entry
findings.designSays: Frame 6 (resume) shows a ResumeBanner at the top of step 3 content when the user re-enters the wizard with steps 1–2 already done: RotateCcw icon, 'Pick up where you left off' title, 'Steps 1–2 are done. Set your hours to finish.' subtitle (scheduling-setup-frames.jsx:371–390, 550–567).
findings.implementationSays: Neither FirstRunWizardScreen.swift nor FirstRunWizardScreen.kt tracks a resume flag or renders any resume banner. The iOS model (FirstRunWizardModel.swift) and Android VM (FirstRunWizardViewModel.kt) have no resume state at all. Web SetupWizard.tsx does implement the banner (lines 382–395), conditioned on a `resume` boolean derived from page state.
findings.evidence: design: scheduling-setup-frames.jsx:371–390 ResumeBanner component, :550–567 FrameResume renders it in step 3 | ios: FirstRunWizardScreen.swift (full file, 120 lines) — no resume | android: FirstRunWizardScreen.kt (full file, 92 lines) — no resume | web: SetupWizard.tsx:382–395 resume banner present

findings.title: Web step 4 success primary CTA label is 'Go to scheduling' instead of design's 'Share link'
findings.designSays: Step 4 CTAs: ghost 'Add type' (plus icon) + primary 'Share link' (share-2 icon) (scheduling-setup-frames.jsx:539–540).
findings.implementationSays: Web SuccessStep renders ghost 'Add type' with Plus icon (correct) and primary 'Go to scheduling' with Share2 icon (wrong label). The onClick routes to the hub page (router.push(BASE)), not a share action — so both the label and the action diverge from design.
findings.evidence: design: scheduling-setup-frames.jsx:539 `<GhostBtn flex={1} icon='plus'>Add type</GhostBtn>`, :540 `<PrimaryBtn icon='share-2' flex={1.5}>Share link</PrimaryBtn>` | web: SetupWizard.tsx:565–580 'Add type' correct, line 579 'Go to scheduling' wrong label; onDone calls router.push(BASE) not a share sheet

screens.crossPlatformNotes: All four draft blocker/major findings independently confirmed at cited lines. iOS is closest to design on identity accent (WizardSuccessHero receives pillar accent/accentBg props from the model theme, correctly using sky color), but shares the missing-resume-banner gap with Android. Android has the additional critical Sunday-key mismatch (7 vs ISO 0) that iOS and web both get right. Web is closest on weekday contract and success hero colors but diverges on the step-4 primary CTA label. No new findings added — the three files reviewed during verification did not surface additional clear discrepancies beyond those already documented.
screens.verifierSummary: All four draft findings survived independent line-by-line verification with zero refutations or downgrades. (1) Android SuccessHeroBlock.kt confirmed at lines 55/62 using green successBg/success tokens with no pillar parameter — sky accent never applied. (2) Android Sunday key confirmed as 7 in WizardSteps.kt:59 and FirstRunWizardViewModel.kt:53, vs iOS FirstRunWizardModel.swift:38 using 0 and web weeklyHours.ts:37 using 0 with an explicit ISO comment. (3) Neither iOS FirstRunWizardScreen.swift (120 lines, fully read) nor Android FirstRunWizardScreen.kt (92 lines, fully read) contains any resume flag or banner; web SetupWizard.tsx:382–395 has it. (4) Web SuccessStep (SetupWizard.tsx:578–580) confirmed to render 'Go to scheduling' label with router.push(BASE) action instead of design's 'Share link' with a share-sheet action. iOS status upgraded from minor to major because the missing resume banner on step 3 re-entry is a clearly designed feature frame (Frame 6) that is entirely absent on iOS.

findings.title: Android: Automation and Scheduling-defaults groups omit pillar accent on overline
findings.designSays: Every AccentOverline is colored with the active pillar accent (Personal=sky #0284c7, Home=#15803d, Business=#6d28d9). AutomationGroup and DefaultsGroup both call AccentOverline with accent={p.accent}. scheduling-settings-frames.jsx lines 135, 178.
findings.implementationSays: Android calls SettingsGroup(title = "Automation") at line 115 and SettingsGroup(title = "Scheduling defaults") at line 151 with no accent argument, so both fall back to the default PantopusColors.primary600 (sky, SettingsComponents.kt:88). The ViewModel hardcodes SchedulingOwner.Personal (SchedulingSettingsRootViewModel.kt:60), so the bug does not currently surface for users, but the screen already has isBusiness branching and a Team group — the moment Home/Business routing is wired, Automation and Scheduling-defaults overlines will remain sky instead of green/violet.
findings.evidence: Design: scheduling-settings-frames.jsx:135, 178. Android: SchedulingSettingsRootScreen.kt:115, 151 (no accent= arg); SettingsComponents.kt:88 (default accent = PantopusColors.primary600); SchedulingSettingsRootViewModel.kt:60 (owner hardcoded Personal).

findings.title: Android: TzRight and ConnectPill hard-code sky (primary600) instead of pillar accent
findings.designSays: TzRight lock tile and ConnectPill both receive accent={p.accent}, tinting with the active pillar. scheduling-settings-frames.jsx lines 185, 200 (in DefaultsGroup and PaymentsGroup).
findings.implementationSays: SchedulingSettingsRootScreen.kt line 156: SettingsTzRight(accent = PantopusColors.primary600, ...) and line 180: SettingsConnectPill(accent = PantopusColors.primary600, ...). Both are hardcoded sky. Same caveat as Finding 1: SchedulingOwner.Personal is the only current owner, so no live regression yet, but the structure is plainly wrong and will break on multi-pillar expansion.
findings.evidence: Design: scheduling-settings-frames.jsx:185, 200. Android: SchedulingSettingsRootScreen.kt:156 (TzRight hardcoded), :180 (ConnectPill hardcoded); SchedulingSettingsRootViewModel.kt:60.

findings.title: Android: Per-row saving shimmer and SavedChip states absent
findings.designSays: Design specifies two in-flight write states: (a) 'saving' — replaces timezone sub and cancellation-policy trailing with a Shimmer placeholder; (b) 'saved' — shows a green SavedChip + chevron in the cancellation row trailing. scheduling-settings-frames.jsx:174–175, 182–184.
findings.implementationSays: A grep across all files under .../scheduling/settings/ returns zero hits for 'Shimmer', 'shimmer', 'savedChip', 'SavedChip', 'savingRow', 'justSaved'. SettingsData (SchedulingSettingsRootViewModel.kt:40-49) carries no per-row saving/saved fields. SchedulingSettingsRootScreen.kt SettingsBody (lines 103-192) always renders live data; the only write feedback is a screen-level toast. iOS fully implements savingRow / justSavedRow / .savingShimmer / .savedChip (SchedulingSettingsScreen.swift:117, 139–148).
findings.evidence: Design: scheduling-settings-frames.jsx:174–175, 182–184. Android: no Shimmer/SavedChip references in scheduling/settings/ directory. iOS: SchedulingSettingsScreen.swift:117–148 (fully implemented).

findings.title: Web: Payments row always shows Chevron — ConnectPill and 'Connected' chip absent
findings.designSays: Payments row shows ConnectPill when Stripe is not connected (fresh), or a success chip 'Connected' + chevron when loaded. scheduling-settings-frames.jsx lines 199–208 (PaymentsGroup). ConnectPill component also tints with p.accent.
findings.implementationSays: BookingSettings.tsx lines 383–391: sub is hardcoded to 'Take payment at booking' and right={<Chevron />} unconditionally. The web never fetches payments connection status; the ConnectPill component does not exist anywhere in web/src/components/scheduling/hub/ (confirmed by grep). The Chip/ChipChevron imports exist in the file but are unused for the payments section.
findings.evidence: Design: scheduling-settings-frames.jsx:199–208. Web: BookingSettings.tsx:383–391 (Chevron only, no state check, no ConnectPill); hub/ui.tsx (no ConnectPill defined).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the closest to design: it correctly threads `accent` through all SettingsGroup/TzRight/ConnectPill calls using the owner's theme, and fully implements per-row saving/saved states (savingShimmer + SavedChip). Android matches iOS structurally but has two systematic gaps: (1) accent is not threaded into the Automation and Scheduling-defaults SettingsGroup calls (hardcoded sky) and TzRight/ConnectPill (also hardcoded sky), and (2) per-row shimmer/SavedChip states are entirely absent — only a screen-level toast exists. Web correctly uses AccentOverline with the resolved pillar and pillarTokens for the TzLock cluster, so its accent handling is fine; its only deviation is the payments row, which hard-codes a Chevron instead of showing ConnectPill vs. Connected chip. All three platforms render the Danger zone without a per-row accent (uses error color directly), which matches the design.
screens.verifierSummary: All four draft findings confirmed by direct code inspection. Finding 1 (Automation/Scheduling-defaults groups missing accent): verified — SchedulingSettingsRootScreen.kt lines 115 and 151 omit the accent= argument; SettingsComponents.kt line 88 confirms the default falls back to PantopusColors.primary600 (sky). Finding 2 (TzRight and ConnectPill hardcoded sky): verified — lines 156 and 180 explicitly pass PantopusColors.primary600. A clarifying note was added to both findings: the ViewModel currently hardcodes SchedulingOwner.Personal (line 60), so no live regression exists yet, but the screen's isBusiness branching makes multi-pillar expansion imminent. Finding 3 (saving shimmer and SavedChip absent on Android): verified — exhaustive grep across the scheduling/settings folder finds zero references to Shimmer, savedChip, savingRow, or justSaved; SettingsData struct has no such fields; confirmed against iOS which fully implements .savingShimmer and .savedChip (SchedulingSettingsScreen.swift:117–148). Finding 4 (Web payments row hardcodes Chevron): verified — BookingSettings.tsx lines 383–391 hardcode sub text and right={<Chevron />} with no payments-connected state check; ConnectPill does not exist in the web hub UI component library. No findings were refuted or downgraded. No additional clear omissions were found during verification (web accent handling via pillarTokens/AccentOverline is correct and was not flagged as a bug by the draft auditor either).

findings.title: Android: identity pillar accent hardcoded to sky (Personal) — Home/Business show wrong color
findings.designSays: Accent color comes from the active owner pillar: Personal=sky #0284c7, Home=green #16a34a, Business=violet #7c3aed. The ACCENT constant in the design comment is sky only because the loaded frame uses Personal.
findings.implementationSays: NotifComponents.kt:48-49 declares `private val ACCENT = PantopusColors.primary600` and `private val ACCENT_BG = PantopusColors.primary50` as file-level private vals. These are not composable-scoped and never receive a pillar parameter. NotifCategoryCard, NotifMatrixRow, reminder chips, and all header tinting unconditionally use this sky-only ACCENT. NotificationPrefsScreen.kt:133 additionally hardcodes `SchedulingPillar.Personal.accent` in the error retry button. The correct per-pillar mapping exists in OwnerPillarChrome.kt:46-57 but is never consulted by the notif screen.
findings.evidence: NotifComponents.kt:48-49 (hardcoded ACCENT/ACCENT_BG); NotifComponents.kt:106-165 (NotifCategoryCard uses ACCENT with no pillar param); NotificationPrefsScreen.kt:133 (hardcoded Personal.accent in error state); OwnerPillarChrome.kt:46-57 (correct per-pillar definitions ignored); contrast with iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:25-26 (owner-derived accent via model.theme) and Web pillarTokens.ts (MAP keyed by pillar).

findings.title: Web: P chip for Notify-attendees rows renders as 'off' (interactive) instead of 'disabled'
findings.designSays: For all Notify-attendees rows, the P (push) chip is always 'disabled' — grayed out and non-interactive. Only email applies for attendees.
findings.implementationSays: MatrixRow in NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:139-143 renders `state={channels.push ? 'on' : 'off'}` with `onClick={() => onToggle('push')}` for every row regardless of group. All NOTIFY_ATTENDEES rows have def.push:false so P shows 'off' (white bg, interactive) and remains clickable — users can enable push for attendee rows.
findings.evidence: NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:139-143 (P chip state and onClick for all rows, no attendee guard); notificationPrefs.ts:50-73 (NOTIFY_ATTENDEES all have def.push:false but no disabled flag); contrast with iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:314-321 (case .notifyAttendees: return .disabled) and Android NotifComponents.kt:185 (paused || isAttendee -> NotifChipState.Disabled).

findings.title: Web: S channel chips in rows use state='locked' (adds lock badge) instead of state='disabled' (gray, no badge)
findings.designSays: Row-level S chips are state='disabled' (gray bg, no lock badge). The lock icon appears only in the column header text label, not on individual row chips. Design scheduling-notif-frames.jsx:51 shows `<ChannelChip letter='S' state='disabled'/>`.
findings.implementationSays: NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:151 passes `state='locked'` to every S ChannelChip. 'locked' shares gray bg/text with 'disabled' (both fall into the clsx else branch at line 66) but additionally renders an absolute-positioned circular lock-badge overlay (lines 73-81), resulting in a spurious lock badge on every S chip in every row.
findings.evidence: NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:151 (`<ChannelChip letter='S' state='locked' ...>`); NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:73-81 (lock badge rendered when state==='locked'); design scheduling-notif-frames.jsx:51 (S state='disabled'); contrast with iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:302 (state:.disabled for S) and Android NotifComponents.kt:212 (NotifChipState.Disabled for S).

findings.title: Web: booking-confirmation E chip renders state='on' (no lock badge) instead of state='locked' (accent fill + badge)
findings.designSays: The 'Booking confirmation' row is locked (lockedEmail:true). The E chip should render as state='locked': filled accent background + lock-badge overlay indicating the channel is mandatory.
findings.implementationSays: MatrixRow NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:145-150: `state={row.lockedEmail ? 'on' : channels.email ? 'on' : 'off'}`. When lockedEmail=true the E chip gets state='on' (filled accent, no lock badge). Clicking is correctly blocked but the visual distinction is absent.
findings.evidence: NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:145-150 (lockedEmail maps to 'on' not 'locked'); notificationPrefs.ts:56 (booking_confirmation has lockedEmail:true); contrast with iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:325-328 (row.locked -> .locked eState) and Android NotifComponents.kt:192-193 (row.locked -> NotifChipState.Locked for eState).

findings.title: Web: paused and push-off states are not implemented
findings.designSays: Design has four frames: Loaded, Paused (amber master-pause banner with Resume CTA, rows at 0.55 opacity), SMS-locked, and Push-off (red push-permission notice with Settings CTA).
findings.implementationSays: NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:265-282 handles only loading and error. No paused field is read from the API response and no OS push-permission check exists. No PauseBanner or PushOffNotice component exists in the web implementation.
findings.evidence: NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:265-282 (loading/error only, no paused/pushOff branch); NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:218-229 (load() never extracts paused flag); contrast with iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:53-54 (if model.paused pauseBanner; if model.pushOff pushOffNotice) and Android NotificationPrefsScreen.kt:74-75 (NotifPauseBanner / NotifPushOffNotice).

findings.title: iOS: pause banner missing 'Resume' button
findings.designSays: PauseBanner has a 'Resume' pill button (white bg, #fdba74 border, #9a3412 text) on the right side. Design scheduling-notif-frames.jsx:201-204.
findings.implementationSays: iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:161-181 pauseBanner: the HStack contains only the icon ZStack, a VStack with two Text labels, and a Spacer. No Button or Resume text anywhere in the view.
findings.evidence: iOS SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:161-181 (no Button in pauseBanner; HStack ends with Spacer); design scheduling-notif-frames.jsx:201-204 (Resume button); Android NotifComponents.kt:322-331 (NotifPauseBanner correctly includes Box with Text('Resume') and warningLight border).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the closest platform to the design: it correctly uses owner-derived pillar accent/accentBg (model.theme), correctly disables P for attendee rows, uses .disabled for S chips (not locked), uses .locked for the booking-confirmation E chip, and implements both paused and push-off banners. The only iOS gap is the missing Resume button in the pause banner. Android mirrors iOS well for chip state logic and paused/push-off banners (including the Resume button), but breaks pillar theming by hardcoding sky/primary600 across all three pillars. Web has the most divergence: pillar accent is handled correctly, but P attendee chips are interactive instead of disabled, S chips show a spurious lock badge, the booking-confirmation E chip does not show a locked state visually, and neither the paused nor push-off banners are implemented at all.
screens.verifierSummary:
All 6 draft findings independently confirmed against source code. No findings dropped or downgraded.

CONFIRMED:
1. Android hardcoded ACCENT — NotifComponents.kt:48-49 has file-level `private val ACCENT = PantopusColors.primary600` with no pillar param in any downstream composable. OwnerPillarChrome.kt:46-57 defines correct per-pillar mapping but is never consulted. NotificationPrefsScreen.kt:133 also hardcodes Personal.accent in the error retry button.

2. Web P chip attendee not disabled — MatrixRow (NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:139-143) passes push state and onClick to ALL rows with no group guard. iOS NotifMatrixRow.pState line 320 returns .disabled for .notifyAttendees; Android NotifComponents.kt:185 returns Disabled when isAttendee.

3. Web S chip 'locked' vs 'disabled' — NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:151 uses state='locked'. ChannelChip lines 73-81 render a lock-badge overlay for locked state only. Design scheduling-notif-frames.jsx:51 specifies state='disabled' for row S chips. iOS and Android both use .disabled/Disabled for row S chips.

4. Web booking-confirmation E chip 'on' not 'locked' — NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:147 maps lockedEmail to 'on'. iOS line 327 and Android line 193 both return the Locked chip state when row.locked is true, adding the badge.

5. Web missing paused/push-off states — NotificationPrefsForm.tsx:265-282 has no paused or pushOff branch. iOS lines 53-54 and Android lines 74-75 both implement both banners.

6. iOS pause banner missing Resume — SchedulingNotificationPrefsScreen.swift:161-181 confirmed exhaustively: icon + VStack(text, text) + Spacer only. No Button. Android NotifComponents.kt:322-331 includes Resume button correctly.

No new clear discrepancies found beyond the 6 confirmed findings. Per-platform status: iOS=minor, Android=major, Web=major.

findings.title: Web: delta stat cell missing — 'Pending' added, 'No-shows' replaced by 'No-show rate%'
findings.designSays: 4 stat cells: Bookings | +N% vs last month (StatCell with delta='up', arrow icon, green color) | Upcoming | No-shows — summary-card-frames.jsx:232-238. StatCell component at line 141 supports a delta prop that drives arrow icon and success/error coloring.
findings.implementationSays: 4 cells rendered as Bookings | Upcoming | Pending | No-show rate% (SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:207-213). Web StatCell (lines 32-43) has no delta prop, no arrow icon, and uses plain appText color for all values.
findings.evidence: Design: summary-card-frames.jsx:232-238 (StatCell value='+24%' label='vs last month' delta='up', StatCell value='1' label='No-shows'). Web: SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:207-213 (StatCell label='Upcoming', StatCell label='Pending', StatCell value=`${noShowPct}%` label='No-show rate'). Web StatCell at lines 32-43 lacks any delta/arrow logic.

findings.title: Web: sparkline entirely absent in loaded state
findings.designSays: 40px tall SVG sparkline (pillar-accent polyline, 8% opacity fill area, terminal circle dot) rendered below stat cells in the data state — summary-card-frames.jsx:163-181, used at lines 241 and 275.
findings.implementationSays: No Sparkline component, SVG, or canvas element exists in the loaded state of SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx (lines 196-228). The component jumps directly from stat cells to the See insights link.
findings.evidence: Design: summary-card-frames.jsx:241 (<Sparkline accent={acc.accent} data={[3,5,...]}/>). Web: SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:215-226 — only mt-3 flex justify-end div with Link, no sparkline between stat row and insights link.

findings.title: Web: period toggle ('This week' / 'This month') absent in loaded state
findings.designSays: CardHeader renders a sunken segmented pill toggle ('This week' | 'This month') in the top-right of the card header when showPeriod=true (the default for the data state) — summary-card-frames.jsx:119-138. The active chip uses the pillar accent as background with white text.
findings.implementationSays:
Web loaded state (SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:197-205) renders only a single

'This month' in uppercase tracking style. No toggle widget, no 'This week' option, no interactive period switching.
findings.evidence:
Design: summary-card-frames.jsx:124-135 (period picker div with 'This week'/'This month' buttons). Web: SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:198-205 — only

This month

, no enclosing toggle Row.

findings.title: Web: event-type breakdown chips absent in loaded state
findings.designSays: Up to 3 pill chips per event type (6px accent-colored dot + name + count, sunken background) rendered between the sparkline and the See insights link when breakdown=true (the default) — summary-card-frames.jsx:243-255.
findings.implementationSays: No breakdown section, no per-event-type chips, and no corresponding data field (data.byEventType or similar) consumed in the loaded state of SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx.
findings.evidence: Design: summary-card-frames.jsx:243-255 (breakdown chips with acc.accent dot). Web: SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:194-228 — only noShowPct derived from data, no breakdown chips rendered.

findings.title: Android: loading skeleton omits header shimmer for period toggle area
findings.designSays: CardLoading skeleton shows a shimmer placeholder for the overline label (w=88, h=11) AND a shimmer pill for the period toggle (w=120, h=26) side by side — summary-card-frames.jsx:315-318.
findings.implementationSays: SummaryCard.kt SummaryLoading() (lines 323-344) renders only the 4 stat-cell column skeletons, sparkline placeholder, and omits the top header row entirely. iOS HubSummaryCard loadingBody (lines 217-241) correctly renders both shimmer elements in an HStack.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the closest platform to design: it renders all four correct stat cells (with conditional delta arrow cell), sparkline, period toggle, and event-type breakdown chips. Android matches iOS on all those elements but lacks the loading header shimmer and card drop shadow. Web diverges most severely: the stat cell set is wrong (delta column replaced by Pending, No-shows replaced by No-show rate%), sparkline is absent, the period toggle is absent, and event-type breakdown chips are absent — all four blocker/major web-only issues stem from a shallower data model (BookingsSummary lacks deltaPct, sparkline, and byEventType fields) and no StatCell delta prop.
screens.verifierSummary: All four draft blocker/major findings independently confirmed against both the design frame and web source. Finding 1 (stat cells): design summary-card-frames.jsx:232-238 shows 4 cells including a delta arrow cell; SchedulingSummaryCard.tsx:207-213 shows a different 4-cell layout with no delta prop on StatCell. Finding 2 (sparkline): design line 241 uses Sparkline; web loaded state lines 196-228 has none. Finding 3 (period toggle): design CardHeader:119-138 shows a segmented pill picker in the data state; web only shows a static p element. Finding 4 (breakdown chips): design lines 243-255 show up to 3 event-type chips; web has no breakdown rendering. No findings were refuted or downgraded. Two new findings were added: Android omits the loading-skeleton header shimmer (confirmed by comparing SummaryCard.kt:323-344 vs iOS HubSummaryCard.swift:217-241), and Android has no card drop shadow (confirmed by absence of any shadow/elevation in SummaryCard.kt:67-73 vs design boxShadow and iOS setupCard). Per-platform status held at ios=minor, android=minor, web=major.

Choose how their availability turns into one set of bookable times.' Web uses a briefer 'Choose how members' availability turns into one set of bookable times.' screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: correct 3-step flow, 'Share link' primary CTA, full sub-copy on success, dynamic step 2 sub. Android matches iOS on CTA and success sub-copy but hard-codes 'Three members' in step 2 sub. Web matches on step structure (3 discrete states via step===1/2/3 in WizardFrame) but diverges on the primary CTA label ('Go to scheduling' vs 'Share link') and truncates the success sub-copy. The auditor's 'major' finding that web 'collapses to 2 steps' was a misread — web correctly implements three step states (step 1, 2, 3) inside WizardFrame with the WizardFrame chrome updating to show Step 3 of 3; the design's Step 3 IS the success hero frame, identical to web's implementation.
screens.verifierSummary: REFUTED (downgraded): Finding 1 ('Web collapses Home onboarding to 2 steps') was wrong. The web code has three distinct conditional blocks (step===1, step===2, step===3) inside a WizardFrame with total=3; finish() sets setStep(3) which transitions to a genuine third step frame. The design's HomeSuccess IS Step 3 — so the architecture is correct, not collapsed. The only real gap surfaced by this finding is that web's step 3 success sub-copy is truncated (missing 'Bookings show up on the family schedule.'), which is a minor copy defect, not a structural one. CONFIRMED (preserved as major): Finding 2 ('Primary CTA label is Go to scheduling instead of Share link') is verified — web OnboardingWizard.tsx:460-461 uses primaryLabel='Go to scheduling' with router.push navigation, whereas design specifies 'Share link' and both iOS and Android correctly implement 'Share link' that triggers a share sheet. The Share2 icon is present in the button but mislabeled. ADDED: Android step 2 sub-copy hard-codes 'Three members are scheduled' — a count that may be inaccurate if the user deselects members; rated minor with medium confidence because the design frame uses the same phrase but as illustrative copy. Web status downgraded from major to minor since the only confirmed gap is a CTA label mismatch plus sub-copy truncation.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it has all 4 team members including Dana W. (Front desk), correct ApproveExplainer with UserCheck icon and full body, dedicated TeamList with role chips and seat counter. Android diverges on two points: missing Dana W. (4th team member) and both Android and web share the same paid-flag gate on the Price field. Web has the most divergences: wrong component (ComposedNote instead of ApproveExplainer) with truncated body, generic MemberPicker instead of TeamList with role chips/counter, and the wrong primary CTA label on the success screen ('Go to scheduling' vs 'Share link').
screens.verifierSummary: Confirmed 5 of the 6 submitted findings; 1 was correctly self-retracted by the auditor and dropped; 1 finding was extended to cover an additional platform. (1) ApproveExplainer / ComposedNote mismatch: CONFIRMED — web:817-821 uses CheckCircle2 via ComposedNote; design and Android both use UserCheck in a dedicated explainer card; body is also truncated on web (missing '24 hours'). (2) Price field behind paid flag: CONFIRMED and EXTENDED — the auditor flagged web only, but Android has the identical gate via SchedulingFeatureFlags.paidSchedulingEnabled; finding now covers both web and android. (3) Android missing Dana W.: CONFIRMED — BUSINESS_TEAM has only 3 entries (lines 51-56); iOS has 4 (line 488-493) with an explicit comment citing the design spec. (4) Success screen StepRail missing: CORRECTLY RETRACTED — code at lines 605-610 does render StepRail with done=[1,2,3,4]; dropped. (5) Success CTA primary label 'Go to scheduling' vs design's 'Share link': CONFIRMED — Share2 icon is present on the right button (matching position) but the label and onClick routing are wrong. (6) MemberPicker vs dedicated TeamList: CONFIRMED — web reuses the generic MemberPicker for business Step 3, losing role chips, seat counter, gradient avatars, and 'Seated · bookable' sub-labels that the design specifies and Android implements correctly. No additional major findings were discovered during verification beyond the Android price-field extension.

findings.title: Android FilterHeader replaces single-pillar identity pill with a 3-way switcher row
findings.designSays: FilterHeader shows ONE static pill (e.g. 'Personal' or 'Business') in pillar accent color on pillar accent bg, with a single icon. The design has no switcher — the user arrives scoped to one owner. (event-types-frames.jsx lines 104-137)
findings.implementationSays: Android renders an IdentitySwitcherPillRow with all three options (Personal/Home/Business) as a full-width segmented pill row. The active segment fills solid with option.accent and renders icon+label in appTextInverse (white). Inactive options show appTextStrong. The component is interactive — selecting a segment switches the active pillar. iOS and Web both implement a static single-pillar pill as the design specifies.
findings.evidence: Design: event-types-frames.jsx:104-137 (single static pill, no switcher). Android: EventTypeListScreen.kt:209-233 (IdentitySwitcherPillRow with 3 options). IdentitySwitcherPillRow.kt:77 (background accent on active), 91,97 (appTextInverse/appTextStrong). iOS correct: EventTypeListView.swift:113-124 (single static pill in theme.accent/theme.accentBg). Web correct: fields.tsx:32-54 (PillarPill single static pill).

findings.title: Android FilterHeader uses vertical accent gradient background instead of plain surface
findings.designSays: FilterHeader has background: P.surface (#ffffff) — plain white, no gradient. (event-types-frames.jsx:110-112)
findings.implementationSays: Android wraps the filter header column in a Brush.verticalGradient(listOf(pillar.accentBg, PantopusColors.appSurface)) background, producing a tinted-to-white fade behind the identity row and segment filter. iOS and Web use plain appSurface/bg-app-surface.
findings.evidence: Design: event-types-frames.jsx:110 (background:P.surface). Android: EventTypeListScreen.kt:206-207 (Brush.verticalGradient with pillar.accentBg). iOS: EventTypeListView.swift:134 (.background(Theme.Color.appSurface)). Web: page.tsx header has no gradient.

findings.implementationSays: Web AllHidden (filter=active) renders both a 'View hidden' button and a separate 'New' primary CTA button side by side. The 'Nothing hidden' (filter=hidden) state is correct.
findings.evidence: Design: event-types-frames.jsx does not show a secondary 'New' CTA in the all-hidden frame. Web: page.tsx:410-427 (two buttons: View hidden + New).

findings.title: iOS overflow menu uses system action sheet instead of custom in-list popover
findings.designSays: Design shows a custom floating popover menu anchored near the row (OverflowMenu, event-types-frames.jsx:231-257) with icon+label rows, blue highlight on first item, rounded card with shadow.
findings.implementationSays: iOS uses a system confirmationDialog (.confirmationDialog modifier) which renders as a native action sheet. The item order (Copy booking link, Duplicate, Share, Hide/Make active, Delete) matches the design, but the visual presentation is the platform's native sheet, not the custom popover. Android uses a DropdownMenu (closer to the design's popover). This is a reasonable native adaptation but diverges from the design visual.
findings.evidence: Design: event-types-frames.jsx:231-257 (custom popover with specific styling). iOS: EventTypeListView.swift:43-48, 223-234 (.confirmationDialog presenting:). Android: EventTypeListScreen.kt:376-399 (DropdownMenu with matching items).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: static single-pillar identity pill, correct filter header, correct row cards. Android diverges on the identity area: a 3-way interactive switcher row replaces the static single-pillar pill, and the filter header adds an accent-to-surface vertical gradient not in the design. Web is generally correct on the identity pill and filter but adds a secondary 'New' CTA in the all-hidden state. Android and iOS agree on: segmented Active/Hidden filter with sunken track and white selected segment, section overline in pillar accent, row card geometry (dot/name/meta/toggle/overflow). iOS uses a native action sheet for the overflow menu while Android uses a DropdownMenu closer to the design's floating popover.
screens.verifierSummary: CONFIRMED: The major finding (Android 3-way switcher row replacing single static identity pill) is correct and independently verified. Design frame (event-types-frames.jsx:104-137) unambiguously shows a single static pillar pill with no switching functionality. Android (EventTypeListScreen.kt:209-233 + IdentitySwitcherPillRow.kt) implements an interactive full-width segmented row across all three pillars. iOS and Web correctly implement the static single-pillar pill. All line citations in the original finding are accurate. ADDED: A related minor finding — Android also applies a vertical accent-to-surface gradient on the filter header background, while the design specifies plain P.surface (white). ADDED: Minor iOS finding — system action sheet is used instead of the design's custom floating popover (reasonable native adaptation, but a visual divergence). ADDED: Nit web finding — AllHidden state shows an extra 'New' CTA not in the design. No findings were refuted or downgraded; all evidence supports the original verdict. Per-platform status unchanged: ios=minor, android=major, web=minor.

findings.title: Web Assignment card renders a single LinkRow (links out) instead of inline Segmented control with blurb
findings.designSays: AssignmentCard contains a Segmented control with options 'Anyone/Specific/Collective', a caption blurb, and for Collective mode: a Required-hosts stepper and member avatars — event-editor-frames.jsx lines 115-136
findings.implementationSays: Web renders a single LinkRow icon=Users, label='Who can be booked', href='/app/scheduling/business' with no Segmented, no blurb, and no collective controls (EventTypeForm.tsx lines 490-501). iOS renders a full Picker.segmented with 3 modes, caption blurb, and conditional collectiveControls (EventTypeEditorView.swift lines 257-296). Android renders EtSegmented with 3 options and a blurb Text but is also missing the collective-mode required-hosts stepper and member avatars (EventTypeEditorScreen.kt lines 233-248). The first-pass finding correctly identifies web as the sole platform with a LinkRow substitution; the Android evidence overstated Android as having 'the full inline' experience — Android has Segmented+blurb only.
findings.evidence: Design: event-editor-frames.jsx:115-136; Web: EventTypeForm.tsx:490-501; iOS: EventTypeEditorView.swift:257-296; Android: EventTypeEditorScreen.kt:233-248

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Web diverges most severely from design on B2: it substitutes the entire Assignment inline Segmented+blurb+collective controls with a single link-out row, and omits two of three link-out rows in the More card (Booking limits, Reminders). iOS is closest to design — it renders the full Segmented picker, collective Required-hosts stepper, all three More rows, correct 16pt card radius with 1px border overlay, Pricing Collect segmented, and sticky SaveBar — with only minor collective member-avatars absence. Android sits between the two: it has the Assignment Segmented+blurb and all three More rows (matching design) but is missing the collective-mode Required-hosts stepper and member avatars, putting it at minor parity for those business-pillar frames. Several items flagged in the underlying parity-audit document are stale and have been fixed since that document was written: Android bottomActionLabel/SaveBar is present (EventTypeEditorScreen.kt:130), Android title is 'Event type' (line 125), there is no extra 'Manage hosts' LinkRow on Android, iOS has no extra slug TextField in BasicsCard (comment at EventTypeEditorView.swift:124-126 confirms intentional omission), and Pricing Collect segmented (Full amount/Deposit) is present on all three platforms.
screens.verifierSummary: Verified 3 flagged blocker/major/low-confidence findings by reading design frames (event-editor-frames.jsx) and all three platform implementations. Finding 1 (Web Assignment single LinkRow): CONFIRMED for web — EventTypeForm.tsx:490-501 is conclusively a single LinkRow vs design's Segmented+blurb+collective controls. The first-pass evidence overstated Android as having 'the full' experience; Android has Segmented+blurb (EventTypeEditorScreen.kt:233-248) but lacks collective stepper and avatars, which is captured as a separate major Android finding. Finding 2 (Web missing Booking limits + Reminders): CONFIRMED — imports and render tree at EventTypeForm.tsx:1-21 and 544-555 confirm only Intake questions is present; Bell and Gauge icons are not imported; iOS (EventTypeEditorView.swift:428-449) and Android (EventTypeEditorScreen.kt:349-366) both have all three rows. Finding 3 (Web Advanced missing Per-day cap): REFUTED and DROPPED — per-day cap Stepper is present at EventTypeForm.tsx:473-487. Additionally, several prior-audit items were verified as stale and are not carried forward: Android SaveBar now exists (bottomActionLabel at EventTypeEditorScreen.kt:130), Android top-bar title is 'Event type' (line 125), no 'Manage hosts' extra LinkRow exists on Android, iOS BasicsCard has no slug TextField (intentional, per comment at EventTypeEditorView.swift:124-126), and Pricing Collect segmented is implemented on all three platforms (iOS EventTypeEditorView.swift:409-420, Android EventTypeEditorScreen.kt:407-413, Web PricingFields.tsx:129-138). One new finding added: Android collective mode lacks the required-hosts stepper entirely (not just avatars), which the first-pass finding obscured by crediting Android with 'full' Assignment implementation.

findings.evidence: intake-frames.jsx: no bottom save button in FrameDefaults/FrameCustom/FrameEditing/FrameReorder; IntakeQuestionsEditor.tsx:231-240 `Save questions` button

findings.title: Web page: uses grey 'for {name}' subtext and PillarPill badge instead of pillar-accent overline
findings.designSays: Sheet header has a 9.5px bold uppercase pillar-accent overline 'Personal · Intro call' immediately above the 'Intake questions' title (intake-frames.jsx lines 65-67, accent colour from accentOf(pillar)).
findings.implementationSays: questions/page.tsx renders `for {name}` at 14px in text-app-text-secondary below the EditorHeader title (questions/page.tsx line 122). EditorHeader shows a PillarPill badge indented below the title (EventTypeForm.tsx lines 632-634), not a pillar-accent text overline above it.
findings.evidence: intake-frames.jsx:65-67 accent overline; questions/page.tsx:121-123 'for {name}' in text-app-text-secondary; EventTypeForm.tsx:632-634 PillarPill badge

findings.title: iOS: Checkbox type does not show the options editor; Android correctly does
findings.designSays: isSelectType includes Checkbox, so OptionsList renders when Checkbox is selected (intake-frames.jsx line 191: `['Dropdown','Multi-select','Checkbox']`; line 231: `{isSelectType(type) && <OptionsList />}`).
findings.implementationSays: iOS QuestionFieldType.needsOptions returns true only for .select and .multiselect, excluding .checkbox (IntakeQuestionsEditorViewModel.swift line 38). IntakeQuestionEditGroup shows optionsEditor only when needsOptions is true (IntakeQuestionCard.swift line 128). Android correctly sets hasOptions=true for Checkbox (IntakeQuestionsModels.kt line 18) and shows the options editor (IntakeQuestionsEditorScreen.kt line 338).
findings.evidence: intake-frames.jsx:191 isSelectType includes Checkbox; IntakeQuestionsEditorViewModel.swift:38 `self == .select || self == .multiselect`; IntakeQuestionsModels.kt:18 `Checkbox("Checkbox", "checkbox", true)` hasOptions=true

findings.title: iOS/Android: FormShell top-bar 'Done' button label matches design sheet header; no discrepancy
findings.designSays: Sheet header top-right button is 'Done' (intake-frames.jsx line 73).
findings.implementationSays: iOS FormShell rightActionLabel='Done' (IntakeQuestionsEditorView.swift line 65); Android FormShell rightActionLabel='Done' (IntakeQuestionsEditorScreen.kt line 123). Both match design exactly.
findings.evidence: intake-frames.jsx:73; IntakeQuestionsEditorView.swift:65; IntakeQuestionsEditorScreen.kt:123

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and Android are largely aligned: both use FormShell with a 'Done' header action, pillar-accent overlines, locked Name/Email rows, and 3-column TypeSelector. The one iOS-specific bug is Checkbox not showing the options editor (needsOptions excludes .checkbox), while Android correctly sets hasOptions=true for Checkbox. Web diverges most severely: EditGroup CTA reads 'Done' instead of 'Save question', a non-design 'Save questions' bottom button is the actual commit action, and the pillar-accent overline is replaced with a grey 'for {name}' subtext plus a PillarPill badge. Android is closest to design on all four inspected points.
screens.verifierSummary: All four major/high-confidence draft findings were independently confirmed against the source files and design frame. (1) Web EditGroup 'Done' vs 'Save question': CONFIRMED — intake-frames.jsx:243 says 'Save question', IntakeQuestionsEditor.tsx:473 renders 'Done'. (2) Web extra 'Save questions' bottom button: CONFIRMED — design has no such button in any of its four frames; web renders it at lines 231-240. (3) Web identity overline: CONFIRMED — design specifies a pillar-accent uppercase overline above the title; web renders 'for {name}' in secondary grey below the EditorHeader title, with a PillarPill badge instead of an accent text overline. (4) iOS Checkbox options omission: CONFIRMED — needsOptions at IntakeQuestionsEditorViewModel.swift:38 excludes .checkbox while the design's isSelectType at intake-frames.jsx:191 includes it; Android correctly marks hasOptions=true for Checkbox. One note was added confirming iOS/Android top-bar 'Done' label matches the design's sheet header button, so no discrepancy there. No draft findings were refuted or downgraded; all evidence in the original citations was accurate with only minor line-number precision differences (EditGroup button text sits at line 473, not 471). Per-platform status is unchanged: ios=minor (one major functional gap, no chrome issues), android=minor (parity-correct; Android adds native drag-to-reorder on the grip handle, a minor divergence from the design's grab cursor idiom), web=major (three significant design-parity gaps).

findings.title: iOS connected-calendar status chip uses wrong labels and no leading icon
findings.designSays: Connected-calendar chip shows leading icon (check for synced, triangle-alert for re-auth) + label 'Synced' or 'Action needed', rendered as a filled pill with success100 bg (#D1FAE5) + success700 fg
findings.implementationSays: iOS reuses SchedulingStatusPill(.active) — label 'Active', no leading icon, successBg (#F0FDF4/success50) background — and .pending — label 'Pending', warningBg. Labels are wrong and the shared pill renders text only (no icon, SchedulingStatusPill.swift body is Text(label) only at line 156).
findings.evidence: connected-calendars-frames.jsx:110-123 (StatusPill map: kind='synced' icon='check' label='Synced', kind='attention' icon='triangle-alert' label='Action needed'). ConnectedCalendarsView.swift:273 (status: .active), :344 (status: .pending). SchedulingStatusPill.swift:59-76 (labels 'Active'/'Pending'), :155-169 (body is Text(label) only, no icon). Android's local CalendarStatusPill correctly renders label 'Synced'/'Action needed' + leading icon (ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:566-582).

findings.title: Android Error state unreachable — network failures silently swallowed as Loaded(emptyList())
findings.designSays: Error state with retry should be reachable on network failure
findings.implementationSays: ConnectedCalendarsViewModel.kt:51-55: the Failure branch sets _state to Loaded(emptyList()) rather than Error, so the Error branch in ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:129 is dead code for real network failures. The coming-soon hero (shown on emptyList) is an acceptable v1 UX fallback, but the Error state with retry cannot be exercised by a real network failure. All row variants (CONNECTED, REAUTH, DENIED, CONNECTING) are correctly implemented and dispatched via rowKindOf() at ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:265-275.
findings.evidence: ConnectedCalendarsViewModel.kt:46-55 (Failure -> Loaded(emptyList())). ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:129 (Error branch exists but unreachable). ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:265-275 (all row variants present: CONNECTED/REAUTH/DENIED/CONNECTING/CONNECT).

findings.title: Web ConnectedRow missing re-auth, denied, and connecting row variants
findings.designSays: Frame 3 (ConnectingRow with external-link icon + shimmer in place of Connect button), Frame 5 (ReAuthRow: attention chip + warningBg banner with triangle-alert + Reconnect button), and Frame 6 (DeniedRow: lock banner + Open Settings link) must render based on calendar status.
findings.implementationSays: ConnectComingSoon.tsx:213-249 — ConnectedRow handles only synced/non-synced via a boolean check on status; it always shows a Check icon and either 'Synced' or the raw status string. No ReAuthRow, no DeniedRow, no ConnectingRow (the connecting state is inlined into ConnectRow as text 'Opening…' without a shimmer). No warning banner, no Reconnect button, no Open Settings link.
findings.evidence: ConnectComingSoon.tsx:213-249 (ConnectedRow, single status boolean). connected-calendars-frames.jsx:150-168 (ConnectingRow with shimmer), :217-245 (ReAuthRow with warningBg banner + Reconnect), :250-275 (DeniedRow with lock + Open Settings).

findings.title: Web ConnectedRow missing conflict/booking toggle rows and Disconnect button
findings.designSays: A connected (synced) row shows two ToggleRows ('Check for conflicts' / search-check icon; 'Add bookings to this calendar' / calendar-plus icon) and a trailing 'Disconnect' text button below the sync timestamp.
findings.implementationSays: Web ConnectedRow (ConnectComingSoon.tsx:213-249) shows only: ProviderTile + provider name + account email + status chip + last-synced timestamp. No toggle rows, no Disconnect button.
findings.evidence: ConnectComingSoon.tsx:213-249. connected-calendars-frames.jsx:172-178 (AccountToggles with search-check and calendar-plus ToggleRows), :206-211 (Disconnect button), :197-213 (full ConnectedRow structure).

findings.title: iOS and Android both use plain Search icon instead of search-check for conflicts toggle
findings.designSays: ToggleRow for 'Check for conflicts' uses icon 'search-check' (connected-calendars-frames.jsx:175)
findings.implementationSays: iOS uses PantopusIcon.Search (ConnectedCalendarsView.swift:275). Android uses PantopusIcon.Search (ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:370). Both are plain search magnifier without the checkmark variant.
findings.evidence: connected-calendars-frames.jsx:175 (icon='search-check'). ConnectedCalendarsView.swift:275 (.search). ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt:370 (PantopusIcon.Search).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Android is the closest to design: it correctly implements all row variants (CONNECTED, REAUTH, DENIED, CONNECTING), uses correct labels ('Synced'/'Action needed'), and has the leading icon in CalendarStatusPill. Its only issues are a dead Error state on network failure (minor) and using Search instead of search-check. iOS is major-divergent on the status chip (wrong labels, no leading icon in the shared SchedulingStatusPill, and slightly lighter successBg vs design's success100). Web is the furthest from design: ConnectedRow is a stub missing re-auth/denied/connecting variants, toggle rows, and Disconnect. The 3-way divergence is: Android has its own local CalendarStatusPill that matches design labels+icon; iOS reuses the shared SchedulingStatusPill which lacks that capability; web uses an inline chip that only handles synced/non-synced.
screens.verifierSummary: REFUTED (finding 2): Draft claimed Android was missing re-auth/denied/connecting row variants — this is incorrect. ConnectedCalendarsScreen.kt lines 265-275 clearly implement and dispatch all five CalendarRowKind variants (CONNECTED, REAUTH, DENIED, CONNECTING, CONNECT) via rowKindOf(). Downgraded to minor and retitled: the real issue is the ViewModel's Failure branch emitting Loaded(emptyList()) instead of Error, making the Error state unreachable, not missing row variants. CONFIRMED (finding 1): iOS status chip labels are wrong ('Active' not 'Synced', 'Pending' not 'Action needed') and SchedulingStatusPill renders no leading icon (body is Text-only). Also caught that iOS uses successBg (#F0FDF4) while design specifies success100 (#D1FAE5). CONFIRMED (finding 3 + 4): Web ConnectedRow is a partial stub with no re-auth/denied/connecting variants, no toggle rows, no Disconnect button. ADDED (new nit): Both iOS and Android use plain Search icon for the conflicts toggle; design specifies search-check. CORRECTED status: iOS elevated from minor to major (status pill labels + missing icon are functionally wrong); Android corrected from major to minor (row variants are present, only error-state reachability issue); web stays major.

findings.title: iOS — PersonalHeaderPill absent on ScheduleList screen (all four states)
findings.designSays: FrameSingle/FrameMultiple/FrameEmpty/FrameLoading all render <HeaderPill pillar="personal" /> immediately below SchedTopBar (availability-frames.jsx lines 206, 224, 243, 259). E.personal = E.blue600 = #0284C7.
findings.implementationSays: AvailabilityScheduleListView.swift never calls AvailabilityHeaderPill. The component exists at AvailabilitySheetComponents.swift:23 and is used in WeeklyHoursEditorView.swift:100 (editor screens) but is absent from the list screen across all display phases (loading, loaded, empty, error). The code comment 'Spec omits the helper line on the empty frame' is also inaccurate — the design omits the HelperLine on empty but keeps the HeaderPill.
findings.evidence: Design: availability-frames.jsx:206,224,243,259. iOS: AvailabilityScheduleListView.swift — zero AvailabilityHeaderPill references. AvailabilitySheetComponents.swift:23 declares the component; WeeklyHoursEditorView.swift:100 is the only call site.

findings.title: Android — AvailabilityTopBar: 52dp height (design 46px), 16sp title (design 15px), no bottom border
findings.designSays: SchedTopBar: height 46, title fontSize 15 / fontWeight 600, borderBottom 1px solid E.border (availability-frames.jsx lines 19-20, 28).
findings.implementationSays: AvailabilityComponents.kt:47 declares TOP_BAR_HEIGHT = 52.dp. Title at line 90-91 is fontSize=16.sp, FontWeight.SemiBold. The Row composable (lines 62-103) has no HorizontalDivider or border Box appended, so no bottom hairline exists. The shared SchedulingTopBar in _shared/SchedulingChrome.kt (which does have a 1dp bottom hairline) uses 56dp and is not used by any availability screen — all five availability screens (AvailabilityListScreen, WeeklyHoursEditorScreen, BlockOffTimeScreen, BookingLimitsScreen, DateOverridesScreen) call AvailabilityTopBar instead.
findings.evidence: Design: availability-frames.jsx:19,20,28. Android: AvailabilityComponents.kt:47 (52dp), :90 (16sp), :62-103 (no border). SchedulingChrome.kt:49 (56dp), :153-160 (bottom hairline) — not used by availability screens. AvailabilityListScreen.kt:80 calls AvailabilityTopBar.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Android correctly renders PersonalHeaderPill() on the list screen (AvailabilityListScreen.kt:87), while iOS omits it entirely. For the top bar, iOS delegates to the system NavigationStack (navigationTitle + .inline display mode), so no custom top bar height divergence applies on iOS; the Android bespoke AvailabilityTopBar at 52dp/16sp/no-border diverges from both design (46px/15sp/1px-border) and the project's own shared SchedulingTopBar (56dp/16sp/1px-border). Web uses a page-header pattern appropriate for desktop layout and is not expected to replicate the mobile-style HeaderPill or custom top bar. Android is closest to design on the HeaderPill (has it); iOS is absent on that element. Neither mobile platform matches design top bar spec, with Android's bespoke composable being the root cause.
screens.verifierSummary: Both draft findings were independently confirmed against source. (1) iOS HeaderPill absence: verified by grep across AvailabilityScheduleListView.swift (zero matches for AvailabilityHeaderPill) and cross-checked that the component exists in AvailabilitySheetComponents.swift:23 and is only called from WeeklyHoursEditorView.swift:100 — not the list screen. All four design frames require it. Finding survives unchanged. (2) Android top-bar dimensions: confirmed TOP_BAR_HEIGHT=52.dp at AvailabilityComponents.kt:47, fontSize=16.sp at line 90, and no bottom border anywhere in the Row or surrounding Column. Verified the shared SchedulingTopBar in _shared/SchedulingChrome.kt:154-160 does have a 1dp bottom hairline but is not used by any availability screen. Finding survives unchanged. No findings were refuted or downgraded. One corroborating observation added: all five Android availability screens (not just the list) use the legacy AvailabilityTopBar, making the top-bar gap systemic across the entire availability feature area. The per-platform status was recomputed: iOS=major (HeaderPill missing), Android=major (top bar), Web=minor (no regressions from original audit's minor assessment confirmed by reading page.tsx — appropriate desktop header pattern used).

findings.title: Android: Empty/unset hero state missing — no CompositionGapCard, EmptyHero, or isUnset guard
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (FrameEmpty): when schedule is unset, show CompositionGapCard (layers icon + 'Start with your hours') then EmptyHero (calendar-clock icon, 'Set your hours', QuickDefaultButton). Top bar title changes to 'Set hours'. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:340-352.
findings.implementationSays: WeeklyHoursEditorViewModel has allDaysOff and hasNoRules but no isUnset property (no check for 'never had rules and not dirty'). WeeklyHoursEditorScreen hardcodes title to 'Edit schedule' (line 80) and only branches on form.allDaysOff for the warning card; no CompositionGapCard or EmptyHero branch exists. A3InfoCard exists in AvailabilityComponents.kt (lines 742-769) but is never called from WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:340-352. Android: WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:80 (hardcoded title), WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:100-111 (only allDaysOff branch), WeeklyHoursEditorViewModel.kt:55-56 (allDaysOff/hasNoRules; no isUnset). iOS counterpart at WeeklyHoursEditorViewModel.swift:97-99 has isUnset = allOff && !isDirty and view branches on it at WeeklyHoursEditorView.swift:101-105.

findings.title: Web: Missing HeaderPill / personal identity accent chip below top bar
findings.designSays: Every frame (FrameDefault, FrameMultiBlock, FrameWarning, FrameEmpty, FrameSaving) renders <HeaderPill pillar='personal' /> immediately below the TopBar — a sky-tinted capsule chip with user icon and 'PERSONAL' label. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:282, 307, 328, 345, 362.
findings.implementationSays: Web AvailabilityEditorPage renders BackLink + h1 + p subtitle + tab bar but no HeaderPill or personal identity chip at any point in the layout. Sky color tokens appear only on tab underlines, buttons, and focus rings — not as a standalone identity pill component.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:282, 307, 328, 345, 362 (HeaderPill in every frame). Web: frontend/apps/web/src/app/(app)/app/scheduling/availability/[id]/page.tsx:225-251 — no pill rendered. iOS: AvailabilitySheetComponents.swift:23-41 (AvailabilityHeaderPill). Android: AvailabilityComponents.kt:152-176 (PersonalHeaderPill), called at WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:99.

findings.title: Web: Tab-bar navigation (Weekly hours / Date overrides / Limits & notice) not in design
findings.designSays: All content (Schedule name card, Timezone card, WeekGrid card, LinksCard) is on one scrollable page. LinksCard rows are link-out affordances that navigate to separate views/sheets — no in-page tab switcher shown in any frame. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:280-292 (FrameDefault: single Body, no TABS).
findings.implementationSays: Web wraps the editor in a three-tab bar (Weekly hours / Date overrides / Limits & notice) at page.tsx:57-61, 234-251. The two link rows in the hours tab switch tabs rather than push/sheet-navigate. DateOverrideEditor and BookingLimitsForm render as tab panels (page.tsx:385-393). iOS and Android both push/present sub-editors as sheets or navigation destinations.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:260-267 (LinksCard: two LinkRows, no tab container). Web: frontend/apps/web/src/app/(app)/app/scheduling/availability/[id]/page.tsx:57-61 (TABS array), 234-251 (tab button bar), 338-381 (link rows that call setTab), 385-393 (tab panels).

findings.title: Web: Empty/unset state (CompositionGapCard + EmptyHero) not implemented
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (FrameEmpty): unset schedule shows CompositionGapCard + EmptyHero (calendar-clock icon, 'Set your hours', QuickDefaultButton) instead of the name/timezone/week-grid cards. Top bar title changes to 'Set hours'. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:340-352.
findings.implementationSays: Web has no distinction between 'never had rules' (isUnset) and 'all days turned off by user' (allOff). After load, if days is empty the same form (name + timezone + noHours warning + empty grid) is shown. Page title is hardcoded 'Edit schedule' (page.tsx:228). No CompositionGapCard or EmptyHero component exists in the web codebase.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:340-352. Web: page.tsx:228 (hardcoded 'Edit schedule'), page.tsx:302-328 (noHours warning only, no hero state), no CompositionGapCard or EmptyHero import or render anywhere in page.tsx.

findings.title: iOS: Copy-to-other-days uses preset native Menu instead of per-day checkbox popover
findings.designSays: CopyMenu shows an individual-day checkmark list (all 6 other weekdays as independent toggles) and a 'Copy to N days' confirm button. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:97-134.
findings.implementationSays: iOS WeekdayHoursRow.copyMenu (WeeklyHoursComponents.swift:77-87) uses a SwiftUI native Menu with three preset actions: 'Copy to all days', 'Copy to weekdays', 'Copy to weekend'. No per-day checkbox picker is presented. Android (AvailabilityComponents implied, CopyToDaysSheet in WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:153-161) and Web (WeeklyHoursGrid.tsx:70-156) both implement the individual-day checkmark popover.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:97-134 (CopyMenu: per-day checkbox list). iOS: WeeklyHoursComponents.swift:77-87 (SwiftUI Menu with 3 preset options). Android: WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:152-161 (CopyToDaysSheet bottom sheet). Web: WeeklyHoursGrid.tsx:70-156 (CopyMenu with individual day toggles).

findings.title: Web: Native HTML time inputs instead of labeled time-range buttons
findings.designSays: Time blocks are a single labeled time-range button (clock icon + '9:00 AM – 5:00 PM' + chevron-down) that opens a picker on tap. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:57-81 (TimeRangeButton).
findings.implementationSays: Web WeeklyHoursGrid.tsx (TimeBlockRow, lines 15-68) renders two separate inline `<input type='time'>` fields side by side within a bordered container. There is no single labeled button showing the formatted range; instead the browser's native time picker UI is used directly. iOS and Android both implement the correct labeled-button + sheet-picker pattern.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:57-81. Web: WeeklyHoursGrid.tsx:38-54 (two type='time' inputs). iOS: WeeklyHoursComponents.swift:51-55 (AvailabilityTimeRangeButton) + WeeklyHoursComponents.swift:234-285 (TimeRangePickerSheet). Android: AvailabilityComponents.kt:492-537 (A3TimeRangeButton) + WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:141-151 (TimeRangeEditorSheet).

findings.title: Android: LinksCard has extra 'Block off time' row and different subtitle copy from design
findings.designSays: LinksCard contains exactly two rows: 'Date overrides & holidays' (value 'None set') and 'Booking limits & notice rules' (value 'Defaults'). weekly-hours-frames.jsx:260-267.
findings.implementationSays: Android LinksCard (WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:302-324) has three rows: 'Date overrides & holidays' (value 'Set days off & custom hours'), 'Booking limits & notice rules' (value 'Notice, caps & start times'), and an extra 'Block off time' (value 'Add a one-off busy hold') row not present in the design.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:260-267 (two LinkRows, no block-off). Android: WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:308-323 (three A3LinkRow calls including PantopusIcon.Clock / 'Block off time').

findings.title: iOS: Overline color in AvailabilityCard correctly uses personal accent per design spec
findings.designSays: Design comment (weekly-hours-frames.jsx:11): 'accent ONLY on the pill + overline'. Pillar accent (personal sky) is explicitly applied to overline text inside cards.
findings.implementationSays: iOS AvailabilityCard renders overline in Theme.Color.personal (sky) at AvailabilitySheetComponents.swift:64. Android SectionOverline also defaults to PantopusColors.personal (AvailabilityComponents.kt:183). Both match the design intent. This was flagged as a potential issue but the design explicitly confirms accent on overline.
findings.evidence: Design: weekly-hours-frames.jsx:11 ('accent ONLY on the pill + overline'). iOS: AvailabilitySheetComponents.swift:64. Android: AvailabilityComponents.kt:183. All consistent with design.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design (has empty/unset hero state via isUnset, CompositionGapCard, WeeklyHoursEmptyHero, and PersonalHeaderPill). iOS diverges only on the copy-to-other-days UX (native Menu preset vs. per-day checkbox popover) and has a minor block-off time navigation difference. Android is missing the isUnset/EmptyHero branch entirely (top bar hardcoded to 'Edit schedule', no CompositionGapCard) and adds an extra 'Block off time' link row with non-design subtitle copy. Web has the most divergence: no HeaderPill, tab-bar navigation instead of single-page layout, no isUnset/empty hero state, and native HTML time inputs instead of labeled-button + picker idiom.
screens.verifierSummary: Confirmed 5 of the 6 draft findings and dropped/reclassified 1. The low-confidence iOS overline color finding (F5) was refuted: the design explicitly states 'accent ONLY on the pill + overline' (weekly-hours-frames.jsx:11), making the personal sky overline color correct on both iOS and Android — the finding was demoted to a nit/documentation note rather than dropped entirely, to acknowledge it was reviewed. All four major findings (Android empty state, Web HeaderPill, Web tab layout, Web empty state) were independently confirmed against source. The cross-platform Web time-input finding was confirmed (two separate type='time' inputs in WeeklyHoursGrid.tsx:38-54 vs. the design's single labeled-button + picker idiom). Two new findings were added: (1) iOS copy-to-other-days uses a 3-preset native SwiftUI Menu instead of the design's per-day checkbox popover (confirmed in WeeklyHoursComponents.swift:77-87 vs. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:97-134); (2) Android LinksCard has an extra 'Block off time' row and non-design subtitle text on both existing rows (WeeklyHoursEditorScreen.kt:308-323 vs. weekly-hours-frames.jsx:260-267). Platform status is unchanged: ios=minor (copy menu deviation), android=major (missing empty/unset state), web=major (tab layout + missing pill + empty state + time inputs).

findings.title: iOS uses native DatePicker(.graphical) instead of custom month-calendar widget
findings.designSays: MonthCalendar: custom grid with weekday headers S/M/T/W/T/F/S, 28dp day circles (borderRadius 50%), chevron prev/next buttons, override dots (4dp circle at bottom of cell), 1px border card at 16px radius (date-overrides-frames.jsx lines 90–133)
findings.implementationSays: Uses SwiftUI DatePicker with .graphical style (system calendar widget) — the system widget does not render override dots, uses Apple's own layout/chrome, and does not match the compact custom-grid spec. Android (MonthCalendar.kt:42–155) and Web (DateOverrideEditor.tsx:49–155) both implement a correct custom grid.
findings.evidence: DateOverridesView.swift:108–124 (.datePickerStyle(.graphical)); MonthCalendar.kt:42–155 (custom Compose grid, 36dp day cells, dot at marked dates); DateOverrideEditor.tsx:49–155 (custom React grid, 32px day cells, dot at marked/holiday dates); date-overrides-frames.jsx:90–133

findings.title: Web missing 'From US public holidays' section, HolidayRow list, and explainer footnote when holidaySetOn=true
findings.designSays: When holidaySetOn=true: SectionLabel 'From US public holidays' + OverridesCard listing each HolidayRow (date, name, HOLIDAY badge) + explainer footnote 'Holidays are blocked as a set. Turn the set off to remove them all at once.' (date-overrides-frames.jsx lines 352–370 in FrameHolidays)
findings.implementationSays: Web computes holidaySetOn (DateOverrideEditor.tsx:185–187) and toggles holidays into the overrides array, and renders the Holiday sets toggle card (lines 396–409), but never conditionally renders the 'From US public holidays' section label, the HolidayRow list, or the explainer footnote. iOS (DateOverridesView.swift:438–462) and Android (DateOverridesScreen.kt:180–188) both implement this correctly.
findings.evidence: DateOverrideEditor.tsx:395–409 (toggle card only — no conditional holiday list block anywhere in file); DateOverridesView.swift:438–462 (holidayImportSection with sectionLabel + list + footnote); DateOverridesScreen.kt:180–188 (if form.holidaySetOn: SectionLabel + OverridesCard with HolidayRow items); date-overrides-frames.jsx:352–370

findings.title: Web CTA label 'Save custom hours' diverges from design and both native platforms
findings.designSays: CTA label when custom hours mode: 'Add custom hours for this day' (date-overrides-frames.jsx line 182)
findings.implementationSays: Web renders 'Save custom hours' (DateOverrideEditor.tsx:327); iOS renders 'Add custom hours for this day' (DateOverridesView.swift:189); Android renders 'Add custom hours for this day' (DateOverridesScreen.kt:214)
findings.evidence: DateOverrideEditor.tsx:327; DateOverridesView.swift:189; DateOverridesScreen.kt:214; date-overrides-frames.jsx:182

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Android is closest to design: it implements a custom MonthCalendar grid (MonthCalendar.kt), the holiday import section with HolidayRow list, and the correct CTA label. iOS departs on the calendar widget (system .graphical picker instead of custom grid, missing override dots). Web departs on two fronts: the holiday import section is entirely absent when the set is on, and the custom-hours CTA uses a different label ('Save custom hours' vs 'Add custom hours for this day'). The personal pillar accent (#0284C7) equals product sky primary-600, so web's use of bg-app-personal for selected-day circles is actually color-correct — not a discrepancy.
screens.verifierSummary: Both major findings from the draft were independently confirmed and survive unchanged. Finding 1 (iOS .graphical DatePicker): verified in DateOverridesView.swift:108–124 — the system widget is used and cannot render override dots or match the compact design grid; Android and Web both implement correct custom grids. Finding 2 (Web missing holiday import section): verified in DateOverrideEditor.tsx — holidaySetOn is computed and the toggle renders, but there is zero conditional rendering of the 'From US public holidays' section label, HolidayRow list, or explainer footnote anywhere in the file; iOS and Android both implement it correctly. One additional clear discrepancy was found during verification: Web CTA label is 'Save custom hours' (minor) while design and both native platforms use 'Add custom hours for this day'. A potential false positive was examined and refuted: web's use of bg-app-personal for the selected day circle looks like a token mismatch but is actually color-identical to the design's E.blue600 (both resolve to #0284C7 per colors_and_type.css), so no finding is warranted there. Per-platform status was upgraded: iOS is 'major' (not 'minor') due to the calendar widget divergence.

findings.title: Web missing conflict-warning card entirely
findings.designSays: block-time-frames.jsx Frame 4 (FrameConflict): ConflictCard (lines 115–138) renders a warningBg card with 'Booking overlap' chip, conflict body copy, and 'View booking' link whenever the chosen window overlaps a confirmed booking
findings.implementationSays: BlockOffForm.tsx has no conflict state or ConflictCard component at all; blocks/page.tsx performs no conflict detection. The only conflict-handling in the web scheduling layer is for 409 SlotConflict on booking (decodeError.ts) — unrelated to this proactive overlay-detection requirement.
findings.evidence: block-time-frames.jsx:115-138 (ConflictCard); BlockOffForm.tsx:1-224 (no conflict state, no ConflictCard); blocks/page.tsx:1-155 (no conflict detection). iOS has conflictCard() at BlockOffTimeView.swift:220-262; Android has A3ConflictCard at BlockOffTimeScreen.kt:85.

findings.title: Web uses native <input type='date'> / <input type='time'> and <select> instead of designed FieldButton fields
findings.designSays: block-time-frames.jsx FieldButton (lines 99–111) and RepeatCard (line 172): icon + value text + chevron-down styled bordered button for Date, Starts, Ends, and Repeats — opens platform-appropriate pickers; not browser-native inputs
findings.implementationSays: BlockOffForm.tsx uses <input type='date'> (line 107), <input type='time'> (lines 138, 155) with absolute-positioned icons — these still render browser-native date/time chrome. Repeats uses a native <select> (line 177), not a styled FieldButton with chevron-down. iOS uses AvailabilityFieldButton + custom picker sheets; Android uses A3FieldButton + A3DatePickerDialog/TimePickerSheet. Original finding cited only Date/Starts/Ends; Repeats control is equally non-conformant.
findings.evidence: block-time-frames.jsx:99-111, 172; BlockOffForm.tsx:102-116 (date), 130-146 (start time), 148-164 (end time), 170-195 (repeats <select>); iOS BlockOffTimeView.swift:92-140 (AvailabilityFieldButton + picker sheets); Android BlockOffTimeScreen.kt:145, 158, 162, 178 (A3FieldButton).

findings.title: Web has no pillar overline ('Personal · Availability') above the screen title
findings.designSays: block-time-frames.jsx Sheet header (line 73): uppercase overline 'Personal · Availability' at fontSize 9.5, fontWeight 700, letterSpacing 0.08em, textTransform uppercase, color E.personal (sky #0284c7), placed above the 'Block off time' title
findings.implementationSays: blocks/page.tsx lines 97–101 render a plain <h1> 'Block off time' followed by a description paragraph. No 'Personal · Availability' overline exists anywhere in the web route. BlockOffForm.tsx also has no overline.
findings.evidence: block-time-frames.jsx:70-75; blocks/page.tsx:97-101 (h1 only, no overline). iOS BlockOffTimeView.swift:66-74 (sheetOverline with PERSONAL · AVAILABILITY in Theme.Color.personal). Android BlockOffTimeScreen.kt:75 (SectionOverline('Personal · Availability')).

findings.title: iOS missing 'Monthly' repeat option — cross-platform divergence with Android and Web
findings.designSays: n/a — no design; design frames show only 'Does not repeat' and 'Weekly' examples without exhaustively listing all options
