findings.implementationSays: iOS BlockRepeat enum has only .never/.daily/.weekly (BlockOffTimeViewModel.swift:16-52). Android has None/Daily/Weekly/Monthly (AvailabilityFormat.kt:243-257). Web has none/daily/weekly/monthly (BlockOffForm.tsx:22-28). iOS is the outlier missing the Monthly option.
findings.evidence: BlockOffTimeViewModel.swift:16-52 (no Monthly case); AvailabilityFormat.kt:247 (Monthly('Monthly')); BlockOffForm.tsx:27 ({ value: 'monthly', label: 'Monthly', rrule: 'FREQ=MONTHLY' }).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Android is closest to design parity: it has the SectionOverline, A3FieldButton pickers with native date/time dialogs, A3ConflictCard with backend-driven conflict detection, and all four repeat options. iOS matches on the overline, FieldButton-style pickers, and conflict card, but is missing the Monthly repeat option. Web is the most divergent: missing the pillar overline, uses native browser inputs instead of styled FieldButton components for all date/time/repeat fields, and entirely lacks conflict-warning functionality. All three audit findings against web were independently confirmed with direct code citations. One new cross-platform issue was found: iOS omits the Monthly repeat option that Android and Web both offer.
screens.verifierSummary: All three draft blocker/major findings were independently confirmed as accurate and are retained at their original severity. Finding 1 (web missing conflict card): confirmed — no conflict state, no ConflictCard, no overlap detection anywhere in BlockOffForm.tsx or blocks/page.tsx. Finding 2 (web native date/time inputs): confirmed and scope extended — web uses native <input type='date'>, <input type='time'>, AND <select> for Repeats, where the design specifies styled FieldButton components with chevron-down for all four fields. Finding 3 (web missing pillar overline): confirmed — blocks/page.tsx has only an <h1> with no 'Personal · Availability' overline in sky color; iOS and Android both have this element. No findings were refuted or downgraded. One new minor cross-platform finding was added: iOS BlockRepeat enum is missing the Monthly option that both Android and Web include.

findings.title: "Max per week" row missing on all three platforms
findings.designSays: All three frames (FrameDefaults, FrameCustom, FrameError) render a StepperRow label="Max per week" — value="20" in FrameDefaults (line 151), value="12" in FrameCustom (line 173) and FrameError (line 201).
findings.implementationSays: iOS: form body lists noticeRow, windowRow, dailyCapRow, perPersonRow, startTimesRow — no weekly cap (BookingLimitsView.swift lines 62-66). VM comment at lines 20-22 explicitly documents the omission as a backend gap (no weekly_cap field). Android: LimitsBody has Minimum notice, Book up to, Max per day, Per-person limit, Start times — no Max per week (BookingLimitsScreen.kt lines 110-151). VM KDoc at line 78 confirms deliberate omission. Web: DEFAULTS array has no weekly-cap entry (BookingLimitsForm.tsx lines 14-40).
findings.evidence: Design: booking-limits-frames.jsx:151,173,201. iOS: BookingLimitsView.swift:62-66, BookingLimitsViewModel.swift:20-22. Android: BookingLimitsScreen.kt:110-151, BookingLimitsViewModel.kt:78. Web: BookingLimitsForm.tsx:14-40.

findings.title: Web: BookingLimitsForm is entirely read-only (no stepper or segmented controls)
findings.designSays: All rows are interactive StepperRow or SegmentRow controls with +/- steppers and segmented pickers that persist edits (booking-limits-frames.jsx lines 107-135).
findings.implementationSays: BookingLimitsForm.tsx renders static ValueRow components (defined as a read-only definition row in primitives.tsx:194) showing hardcoded default strings from the DEFAULTS array. File comment at lines 3-8 explicitly documents this surface as intentionally read-only, deferring to the event-type editor. No stepper, no segmented control, no save mutation.
findings.evidence: Design: booking-limits-frames.jsx:107-135. Web: BookingLimitsForm.tsx:1-8,42-76; primitives.tsx:194-221.

findings.title: Web: Extra info banner explaining limits are per event type — not in design
findings.designSays: Sheet contains only the Helper subtitle ("Sensible defaults are set…") and six rows. No info banner (booking-limits-frames.jsx lines 87-93).
findings.implementationSays: BookingLimitsForm.tsx renders a blue rounded info card with Info icon, "Limits are set per event type" heading, explanatory body copy, and an ArrowUpRight "Manage on event types" link before any ValueRow (lines 44-64).
findings.evidence: Design: booking-limits-frames.jsx:87-93. Web: BookingLimitsForm.tsx:44-64.

findings.title: Web: "Minimum notice" shows hardcoded "None" instead of design default value
findings.designSays: Minimum notice StepperRow shows value="4" (unit="hours") in FrameDefaults, value="12" in FrameCustom (booking-limits-frames.jsx:148,170).
findings.implementationSays: BookingLimitsForm.tsx DEFAULTS array hardcodes value: "None" for Minimum notice (line 17). Because the surface is not connected to the API, no real user value is fetched or shown.
findings.evidence: Design: booking-limits-frames.jsx:148,170. Web: BookingLimitsForm.tsx:17.

findings.title: Web: No "Done" / save CTA rendered when on the Limits tab
findings.designSays: Sheet has a "Done" button in the header (blue when enabled, muted when doneDisabled) present in every frame (booking-limits-frames.jsx lines 76-79).
findings.implementationSays: AvailabilityEditorPage sticky save bar at line 397 is wrapped in {tab === "hours" && …} — it is not rendered for the "limits" tab (page.tsx lines 397-408). BookingLimitsForm has no submit mechanism of its own.
findings.evidence: Design: booking-limits-frames.jsx:76-79. Web: page.tsx:397-408, BookingLimitsForm.tsx:42-76.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and Android are in parity with each other: both implement the same five interactive rows (Minimum notice, Book up to, Max per day, Per-person limit, Start times) with functional steppers/segmented controls, a Done button, conflict-error state, and API save — their only shared gap is the missing \"Max per week\" row. Web diverges significantly: it renders a deliberately read-only summary panel with hardcoded defaults, an extra info banner not in the design, no interactive controls, and no Done/save action for the limits tab. iOS is closest to the design among the three platforms.
screens.verifierSummary: All five draft findings confirmed and retained. (1) Max per week omission: confirmed absent in all three platform files; design cites corrected to include FrameError line 201 which was omitted from the draft. (2) Web read-only surface: confirmed — BookingLimitsForm.tsx uses static ValueRow primitives with no state or mutations, and primitives.tsx:194 labels ValueRow explicitly as read-only; blocker rating upheld. (3) Web info banner: confirmed present at BookingLimitsForm.tsx:44-64, absent from design. (4) Web \"Minimum notice\" hardcoded \"None\": confirmed at line 17. (5) Web no Done CTA on limits tab: confirmed — page.tsx:397-408 gates the save bar on tab === \"hours\". Draft status for Android was downgraded from major to minor: re-reading BookingLimitsScreen.kt and BookingLimitsViewModel.kt, Android has a fully functional interactive form with Done, stepper rows, conflict-error state, and save — its only gap is the same missing weekly cap as iOS, which does not warrant major. No new findings were added beyond correcting the design evidence lines.

findings.title: Web PreviewBar missing dismiss (X) button
findings.designSays: PreviewBar has a circular X dismiss button: 26x26px, bg rgba(255,255,255,0.12), containing a 15px X icon at strokeWidth 2.4 (booking-preview-frames.jsx:44-51)
findings.implementationSays: Web PreviewFrame dark bar (PagePreview.tsx:215-223) renders only an Eye icon + label text inside a single flex div; no dismiss/close affordance exists. iOS correctly implements BookingPreviewBar with the circular X button calling dismiss() (BookingPagePreviewView.swift:101-127).
findings.evidence: Design booking-preview-frames.jsx:44-51; Web PagePreview.tsx:215-223; iOS BookingPagePreviewView.swift:112-120 (correct reference)

findings.title: Web PreviewBar background uses app-text-strong (#374151/fg2) instead of app-text (#111827/fg1)
findings.designSays: PreviewBar background is E.fg1 = #111827 (--app-text) (booking-preview-frames.jsx:67; colors_and_type.css:51: --app-text: #111827)
findings.implementationSays: Web uses class bg-app-text-strong (PagePreview.tsx:216) which maps to --app-text-strong = #374151 (fg2, colors_and_type.css:52). iOS correctly uses Theme.Color.appText = fg1 (BookingPagePreviewView.swift:125). The difference is a noticeably lighter dark bar (#374151 vs #111827).
findings.evidence: Design booking-preview-frames.jsx:67; colors_and_type.css:51-52; Web PagePreview.tsx:216; iOS BookingPagePreviewView.swift:125

findings.title: Web ModeChip inside EventTypeCard is missing the location-mode icon
findings.designSays: ModeChip renders an icon + label: <i data-lucide={modeIcon}/> plus label text, e.g. Video icon + 'Video call' (booking-preview-frames.jsx:130-140)
findings.implementationSays: Web EventTypeCard (PagePreview.tsx:339) renders the chip as a plain <span> containing only {label}. The mode icon is correctly computed by modeMeta() and used in the leading square tile (line 328) but is omitted from the pill chip. iOS LocationChip correctly renders Icon + Text (BookingPagePreviewView.swift:231-241).
findings.evidence: Design booking-preview-frames.jsx:130-140; Web PagePreview.tsx:310, 328, 339; iOS BookingPagePreviewView.swift:231-241

findings.title: Android AllHiddenBody uses solid border instead of dashed border
findings.designSays: AllHidden card uses a 1px dashed border: border: '1px dashed ${E.borderStrong}' (booking-preview-frames.jsx:286)
findings.implementationSays: Android uses .border(1.dp, PantopusColors.appBorderStrong, RoundedCornerShape(Radii.xl)) (PublicPagePreviewScreen.kt:174). The standard Compose border() modifier produces a solid stroke only; no PathEffect/dashed stroke is applied. iOS correctly implements StrokeStyle(lineWidth: 1, dash: [4]) (BookingPagePreviewView.swift:363).
findings.evidence: Design booking-preview-frames.jsx:286; Android PublicPagePreviewScreen.kt:170-175; iOS BookingPagePreviewView.swift:359-365

findings.title: Android PublicHeader avatar pillar hardcoded to Personal regardless of owner type
findings.designSays: Avatar gradient is driven by the owner pillar: personal=sky gradient (135deg,#38bdf8,#0284c7), business=violet gradient (135deg,#a78bfa,#7c3aed) (booking-preview-frames.jsx:112-114)
findings.implementationSays: Android BLAvatar hardcodes pillar = SchedulingPillar.Personal (PublicPagePreviewScreen.kt:205). The root cause is the ViewModel also hardcoding owner = SchedulingOwner.Personal (PublicPagePreviewViewModel.kt:48), so Business/Home owners always render a sky gradient. iOS derives accent from SchedulingIdentityTheme(owner) where owner is injected at init time (BookingPagePreviewViewModel.swift:44-46).
findings.evidence: Design booking-preview-frames.jsx:112-114; Android PublicPagePreviewScreen.kt:205; Android PublicPagePreviewViewModel.kt:48; iOS BookingPagePreviewViewModel.swift:44-46

findings.title: Android ViewModel hardcodes owner = Personal, making the entire preview Personal-only
findings.designSays: The preview is owner-polymorphic; pillar accent and public page data should reflect the actual scheduling owner (personal/home/business) (booking-preview-frames.jsx:6-8, 112-114)
findings.implementationSays: PublicPagePreviewViewModel.kt:48 declares private val owner: SchedulingOwner = SchedulingOwner.Personal with no injection point. Even if the screen were navigated to from a Business context, it would fetch the Personal page. iOS injects owner at init (BookingPagePreviewViewModel.swift:34-41).
findings.evidence: Android PublicPagePreviewViewModel.kt:48; iOS BookingPagePreviewViewModel.swift:34-41

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it correctly implements the dismiss X button, fg1 (#111827) preview bar background, dashed border on AllHidden card, icon+label ModeChip, and owner-polymorphic accent. Android has four confirmed parity gaps (solid border instead of dashed, avatar pillar always Personal) and an additional structural bug where the ViewModel hardcodes owner=Personal, making the entire screen Personal-only regardless of navigation context. Web has three confirmed parity gaps (missing dismiss button, wrong dark bar color token fg2 vs fg1, missing icon in ModeChip pill).
screens.verifierSummary: All 5 draft findings (4 major on web/android) independently confirmed by reading cited files. No findings were refuted or downgraded. One additional major cross-platform finding added: Android PublicPagePreviewViewModel hardcodes owner=SchedulingOwner.Personal (line 48), meaning the screen is permanently Personal-only regardless of actual owner — the avatar pillar bug (finding 5) is a symptom of this deeper structural problem. Per-platform status retained from draft: iOS=minor (no confirmed blockers found; iOS is the reference implementation for all 5 disputed areas), Android=major (dashed border, hardcoded pillar, hardcoded owner), Web=major (no dismiss button, wrong background token, missing chip icon).

findings.title: Android pillar accent hardcoded to Personal — Business/Home owners see wrong accent
findings.designSays: Pillar accent (Personal=sky #0284c7, Business=violet #7c3aed, Home=green #16a34a) must reflect the owner context on overlines, header pill, and avatar gradient. Design Frame 2 (draft/business) shows violet accent throughout all cards.
findings.implementationSays: ViewModel hardcodes `private val owner: SchedulingOwner = SchedulingOwner.Personal` (line 80), and the screen hardcodes `val pillar = SchedulingPillar.Personal` (line 83). The `SchedulingOwner.pillar()` extension in OwnerPillarChrome.kt:71-75 exists but is never called. Business and Home owners always render sky/personal accent.
findings.evidence: BookingPageManageViewModel.kt:80 (`private val owner: SchedulingOwner = SchedulingOwner.Personal`); BookingPageManageScreen.kt:83 (`val pillar = SchedulingPillar.Personal`); OwnerPillarChrome.kt:71-75 (unused pillar() extension); design booking-link-frames.jsx:323-336 (Frame 2 with pillar="business" violet); iOS BookingPageManagementViewModel.swift:125-126 (`var theme: SchedulingIdentityTheme { SchedulingIdentityTheme(owner) }`) correctly drives accent from owner.

findings.title: Android top bar adds an unauthorized trailing Save text button absent from all design frames
findings.designSays: Standard `TopBar title="Booking link"` with no trailing action in any of the 7 frames (biz-kit.jsx TopBar accepts only `title/trailing/back`; none of the booking-link-frames.jsx frames pass a trailing save slot). Save action lives solely in the bottom BLSaveBar.
findings.implementationSays: BLTopBar (BookingPageComponents.kt:60-108) renders a trailing clickable Text showing 'Saving' or the save label (lines 93-105), duplicating the save action into the top bar. The design's BLSaveBar also exists (BookingPageManageScreen.kt:204-209), so both fire simultaneously. The 46dp bar height (line 71) matches the design's own TopBar height (biz-kit.jsx:84 `height:46`) and is NOT a design-parity gap, only an archetype deviation from the shared 56dp SchedulingTopBar.
findings.evidence: BookingPageComponents.kt:93-105 (trailing Save/Saving Text + clickable); BookingPageManageScreen.kt:88-95 and 204-209 (both BLTopBar and BLSaveBar rendered simultaneously); design booking-link-frames.jsx:302+314 (`<TopBar title="Booking link"/>` then `<BLSaveBar/>`); all 7 frames use bare `<TopBar title="Booking link"/>` with no trailing slot; biz-kit.jsx:82 TopBar signature `({ title, trailing, back=true })` — `saving` is not a recognized prop and is silently ignored in Frame 4.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and web both correctly derive pillar/accent from owner context (iOS: SchedulingIdentityTheme(owner); web: pillarForOwner(owner.ownerType)). Android alone hardcodes Personal pillar in both ViewModel and Screen. iOS uses FormShell with bottom-only save action and no top-bar save button, matching the design; Android's bespoke BLTopBar adds a trailing save button not present in any design frame. Web is closest to the design for both findings. Android diverges on both pillar accent and save-action placement.
screens.verifierSummary: Both submitted findings were independently confirmed in source and survive review, but Finding 2 required a correction: the auditor's claim that 46dp is wrong per the design is inaccurate — the design's own biz-kit.jsx TopBar also renders at height:46, so the height matches the design spec. The real parity gap is the trailing Save/Saving text button (BookingPageComponents.kt:93-105) which appears in the top bar on Android but in none of the 7 design frames. The severity (major) and platform assignment (android) are correct. For Finding 1, the root cause extends deeper than line 83 of the Screen: the ViewModel (line 80) also hardcodes SchedulingOwner.Personal, whereas the SchedulingOwner.pillar() extension in OwnerPillarChrome.kt already exists and is unused. Both findings confirmed as high-confidence major gaps; status android=major is justified. No additional clear discrepancies were found while verifying.

findings.title: Web missing 4-tile share targets row — only a single 'Share…' button
findings.designSays: 4 equal-width square tiles in a row: Share (share icon), QR code (qr-code icon), Messages (message-circle icon), Email (mail icon) — each 52px square with sky icon + label below (ShareTargets, frame lines 243–252)
findings.implementationSays: ShareLink.tsx renders a single full-width 'Share…' button (lines 158–165) using navigator.share. No separate QR-code, Messages, or Email tiles exist. iOS has all 4 tiles (ShareLinkSheet.swift lines 205–212); Android has all 4 tiles (ShareLinkSheet.kt lines 202–213).
findings.evidence: design: share-sheet-frames.jsx lines 243–252 (ShareTargets); web: ShareLink.tsx lines 158–165

findings.title: Web missing QR thumbnail card ('Scan to book' row with 'Show QR' button)
findings.designSays: A bordered card row containing a 40px QR thumbnail, 'Scan to book' title, 'Print it or show it at a desk.' subtitle, and a 'Show QR' button (sky-50 bg, sky-700 text) that opens the full-screen QR view (QrThumbCard, frame lines 254–278)
findings.implementationSays: ShareLink.tsx renders the QR canvas in a simple centered block (lines 167–182) with a QrCode icon and configurable caption — no 'Scan to book' title, no subtitle, no 'Show QR' expansion button, and no bordered row card. iOS (lines 238–283) and Android (lines 252–298) both implement the full QrThumbCard correctly.
findings.evidence: design: share-sheet-frames.jsx lines 254–278 (QrThumbCard); web: ShareLink.tsx lines 167–182

findings.title: Web missing QR fullscreen view
findings.designSays: FrameQr: a full-screen white surface with a top-right 'Done' button (sky), centered ContextLabel, 184px QR plate, owner name, mono URL, instruction text, and a 'Save to Photos' button (frame lines 416–487)
findings.implementationSays: ShareLink.tsx has no QR full-screen modal. The static canvas is rendered inline with no expand affordance. iOS implements qrFullScreen via fullScreenCover (lines 359–423) and Android implements QrFullscreenDialog (lines 388–452) both correctly.
findings.evidence: design: share-sheet-frames.jsx lines 416–487 (FrameQr); web: ShareLink.tsx — no QrFullscreen component present

findings.title: Web context label overline text uses muted gray instead of pillar accent color
findings.designSays: The overline text must be the pillar accent color (same as dot): sky #0284C7 for personal, green #16A34A for home, violet #7C3AED for business (ContextLabel, frame line 191: color:p.dot on the text span)
findings.implementationSays: ShareLink.tsx line 114 applies 'text-app-text-muted' (gray #9ca3af) to the overline label. The dot circle correctly uses tk.bg (pillar accent via pillarTokens), but tk.text is not applied to the text. iOS correctly uses theme.accent on both dot and text (line 161). Android correctly uses pillar.accent on text (line 157).
findings.evidence: design: share-sheet-frames.jsx line 191 (color:p.dot on text span); web: ShareLink.tsx line 114 ('text-app-text-muted'); pillarTokens.ts: tk.text exists ('text-app-personal' etc.) but is unused here

findings.title: Web 'Regenerate link' button styled muted gray with no icon, not error red with rotate-ccw icon
findings.designSays: Centered ghost text button with error red (#DC2626) color and rotate-ccw icon (RegenerateButton, frame lines 309–322)
findings.implementationSays: ShareLink.tsx line 188: className='text-xs font-medium text-app-text-muted hover:text-app-text' — muted gray, no icon. iOS correctly uses Theme.Color.error foreground + .rotateCcw icon (lines 344–354). Android uses PantopusColors.error for text (line 348) but uses PantopusIcon.RefreshCw instead of RotateCcw.
findings.evidence: design: share-sheet-frames.jsx lines 315–320 (color:ERROR, rotate-ccw icon); web: ShareLink.tsx lines 184–192

findings.title: Android regenerate button uses RefreshCw icon instead of RotateCcw
findings.designSays: rotate-ccw icon (frame line 317); iOS correctly uses .rotateCcw (ShareLinkSheet.swift line 346)
findings.implementationSays: Android ShareLinkSheet.kt lines 347 and 529 both use PantopusIcon.RefreshCw — a circular refresh arrow, visually different from the partial-arc RotateCcw used in the design and iOS.
findings.evidence: design: share-sheet-frames.jsx line 317 (rotate-ccw); iOS: ShareLinkSheet.swift line 346 (.rotateCcw); Android: ShareLinkSheet.kt lines 347, 529 (PantopusIcon.RefreshCw)

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the closest to design: all 5 originally-flagged major issues are absent on iOS — it correctly implements all 4 share tiles, the QrThumbCard, QR fullscreen, pillar-accent overline text, and the error-red regenerate button with the correct rotateCcw icon. Android matches the design on all structural elements and nearly all styling, with only a nit-level icon substitution (RefreshCw vs RotateCcw). Web (ShareLink.tsx) diverges most significantly: it collapses the 4 share tiles into a single 'Share…' button, renders QR as a plain inline block (no thumb-card, no expand affordance, no fullscreen), and applies muted gray instead of pillar accent to the overline text and regenerate button. All 5 major draft findings for web are independently confirmed in the source.
screens.verifierSummary: All 5 blocker/major draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the source files. No findings were refuted or downgraded. Finding 1 (missing 4-tile share targets): confirmed — web has only a single Share… button; iOS and Android both have all 4 tiles. Finding 2 (missing QrThumbCard): confirmed — web renders a plain canvas block with no 'Scan to book' label, no subtitle, no 'Show QR' button; iOS and Android both implement the full card. Finding 3 (missing QR fullscreen): confirmed — no modal or expand affordance exists in web; both native platforms implement it. Finding 4 (context label overline color): confirmed — web line 114 uses text-app-text-muted; pillarTokens.ts has a tk.text field with the correct accent class but it is not applied to the overline span; iOS and Android both use the pillar accent color. Finding 5 (regenerate button): confirmed — web has no icon and uses muted gray; iOS is fully correct; Android uses error red (correct) but substitutes RefreshCw for RotateCcw (nit). One additional finding was added: Android uses PantopusIcon.RefreshCw (a circular arrow) in both the regenerate button and the confirm dialog instead of RotateCcw (a partial-arc rotate), differing from both the design spec and iOS implementation; severity nit.

findings.title: Android: Error note rendered at bottom of scroll area, after Options card; design and iOS place it between Event type and Availability
findings.designSays: FrameError (oneoff-link-frames.jsx:504-555) renders the error note immediately below the Event type section, before any subsequent sections (Availability/Options are omitted in the error frame). iOS mirrors this intent by inserting the error between Event type (lines 99-101) and Availability (line 107).
findings.implementationSays: Android ConfigBody renders all five sections (SheetTitle, Event type, Availability, Link expires, Options) and then appends the ErrorNote at the very end via `cfg.error?.let { ErrorNote(it, ...) }` at line 150, after OptionsCard at line 148. The error is visually buried below three unrelated sections.
findings.evidence: design oneoff-link-frames.jsx:516-539 (error note follows event type section directly, no other content sections present); Android OneOffLinkGeneratorScreen.kt:138-150 (event type at 138-139, availability at 141-142, link expires at 144-145, options at 147-148, error at 150); iOS OneOffLinkGeneratorView.swift:99-106 (error note inserted at lines 102-106 between event type and availability)

findings.title: Web: 'Ask intake questions' toggle replaced with a navigation link using wrong icon, wrong label, and no toggle
findings.designSays: OptionsCard row (oneoff-link-frames.jsx:291) specifies icon='clipboard-list', label='Ask intake questions', subtitle='Collect details before they book.', with a Toggle control (on=false). It is an in-place toggle, not a navigation action.
findings.implementationSays: Web renders a Next.js `<Link href='/app/scheduling/event-types'>` (OneOffGenerator.tsx:486-502) with a CalendarClock icon, label 'Intake questions' (not 'Ask intake questions'), subtitle 'Uses this service's questions.', and a trailing 'Edit' text label. There is no toggle; the row navigates away instead of toggling a boolean.
findings.evidence: design oneoff-link-frames.jsx:291 `<Row icon='clipboard-list' label='Ask intake questions' ... on={false}/>`; web OneOffGenerator.tsx:486-502 `<Link href='/app/scheduling/event-types'><CalendarClock/> ... Intake questions ... Edit</Link>`; iOS OneOffLinkGeneratorView.swift:477-484 OneOffOptionRow with icon .clipboardList, title 'Ask intake questions', toggle binding; Android OneOffLinkGeneratorScreen.kt:434-441 BLToggleRow with PantopusIcon.ClipboardList, label 'Ask intake questions', on=cfg.askIntake

findings.title: Web: Error note also rendered at the bottom of config area, after Options section (same placement bug as Android)
findings.designSays: FrameError shows the error note immediately below the Event type section; it does not appear after Options.
findings.implementationSays: Web renders genError (lines 506-529) after the Options section (lines 462-504) and immediately before the Generate button (lines 531-553). This mirrors Android's error-at-bottom pattern and diverges from both the design and iOS.
findings.evidence: design oneoff-link-frames.jsx:516-539 (error follows event type directly); web OneOffGenerator.tsx:462-504 (Options section), 506-529 (genError block), 531-553 (Generate button); iOS OneOffLinkGeneratorView.swift:102-106 (error placed between event type and availability)

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to the design. Both Android and web share the error-placement defect (error rendered after Options instead of after Event type). Additionally, web uniquely replaces the 'Ask intake questions' toggle with a navigation link — Android and iOS both implement it correctly as a toggle with the clipboard-list icon. iOS has no major divergences from design on these two verified findings.
screens.verifierSummary:
Both draft findings were independently confirmed by reading source code and the design frame:

Finding 1 (Android error placement, major): CONFIRMED. Android OneOffLinkGeneratorScreen.kt line 150 places ErrorNote after OptionsCard (line 148), at the bottom of the scroll content. iOS correctly inserts the error at lines 102-106 between Event type (99-101) and Availability (107). The FrameError design confirms the error belongs near the top, immediately after Event type. Evidence lines in the draft were accurate.

Finding 2 (Web intake questions row, major): CONFIRMED. Web OneOffGenerator.tsx lines 486-502 render a Next.js Link navigating to /app/scheduling/event-types, with CalendarClock icon and 'Intake questions' label and 'Edit' trailing action — no toggle. Design (line 291), iOS (lines 477-484), and Android (lines 434-441) all implement this as a toggle row with clipboard-list icon and 'Ask intake questions' label.

Additional finding added: Web also has the same error-placement defect as Android. genError block at web lines 506-529 appears after the Options section (462-504), not between Event type and Availability as the design intends. The draft omitted this; it is flagged here as a new major finding. Per-platform status is unchanged (ios=minor, android=major, web=major) because both platforms already had major status; the new web finding is additive within the same major tier.

findings.title: Appearance card uses LayoutTemplate icon instead of palette
findings.designSays: AppearanceCard CardTitle uses icon="palette" — embed-widget-frames.jsx line 191.
findings.implementationSays: EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx line 211 passes icon={LayoutTemplate} to CardTitle for the Appearance card. LayoutTemplate is also the icon for the Inline embed-type segment option (design line 148, impl line 445), so the same icon now appears in two different semantic contexts.
findings.evidence: Design: embed-widget-frames.jsx line 191: `<CardTitle icon="palette"`. Web: EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx line 19 imports LayoutTemplate; line 211: `<CardTitle icon={LayoutTemplate}`.

findings.title: "Primary button label" field missing from Appearance card in inline mode
findings.designSays: AppearanceCard contains a "Primary button label" text field (value "Book a call") between the Hide page header checkbox and Calendar layout chips — embed-widget-frames.jsx lines 260-263.
findings.implementationSays: The Appearance card in EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx (lines 210-292) goes directly from the Hide page header checkbox (lines 268-280) to the Calendar layout Chips (lines 282-291) with no button label field. The buttonText state (line 62, default "Book a call") only appears in the ButtonConfigCard block (lines 186-188), which is conditionally rendered only when type is "popup" or "floating" (lines 174-207). When type="inline" (the default), the button label field is entirely hidden from the user.
findings.evidence: Design: embed-widget-frames.jsx lines 260-263: `<FieldLabel>Primary button label</FieldLabel><TextField value="Book a call"/>`. Web: EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx lines 268-291 — field absent from Appearance card; buttonText only in ButtonConfigCard at lines 186-188.

findings.title: "Where it shows" card: toggle replaced with "Open" link button, title copy differs
findings.designSays: WhereItShowsCard title is "Show inline on /b/yourname", sub-text is "Use this on any site, or turn it on for your Pantopus business page.", and the trailing interactive element is a Toggle (on=true) — embed-widget-frames.jsx lines 353-368.
findings.implementationSays: EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx lines 357-381: title is "Where it shows", sub-text is "Use this on any external site, or link people straight to your public page.", and the interactive element is an `<a>` link button labeled "Open" that navigates to /book/[slug]/embed in a new tab. There is no toggle and no mechanism to enable or disable the embedded page on the user's own Pantopus business page.
findings.evidence:
Design: embed-widget-frames.jsx lines 362-365: `
Show inline on /b/yourname
` + ``. Web: EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx lines 364-379: `

Where it shows

` + `Open`.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Screen is web-only per design; iOS and Android are not applicable. All three surviving findings are web vs. design divergences. No cross-platform comparison is meaningful here.
screens.verifierSummary: All three draft findings (blocker/major, low-to-high confidence) were independently confirmed by reading the cited files. Finding 1: design embed-widget-frames.jsx line 191 uses icon=\"palette\"; implementation EmbedSnippetBuilder.tsx line 211 uses icon={LayoutTemplate} — confirmed. Finding 2: design lines 260-263 place a \"Primary button label\" field inside AppearanceCard between the Hide page header checkbox and Calendar layout chips; the implementation's Appearance card (lines 268-291) omits it entirely — the buttonText state only surfaces in the popup/floating ButtonConfigCard (lines 186-188), so inline-mode users cannot edit this label at all — confirmed. Finding 3: design lines 362-365 show title \"Show inline on /b/yourname\" plus a Toggle; implementation lines 364-379 show title \"Where it shows\" plus an anchor link labeled \"Open\" — both the copy and the interactive element differ — confirmed. No findings were refuted or downgraded. No new discrepancies were flagged during verification beyond what the auditor reported. The draft's 11-finding claim could not be fully verified (only the 3 blocker/major findings were in scope for adversarial review), but these 3 are solid and survive unchanged.

findings.title: Open-in-app banner shows on all states — design restricts it to multi-type only
findings.designSays: OpenInApp component appears only in FrameMulti (booking-landing-frames.jsx:454). FrameSingle, FrameGroup, FrameGroupFull, FrameTeam, FramePaused, FrameEmpty, and FrameLoading all omit it.
findings.implementationSays: BookerProfileHeader.tsx initialises appBannerOpen=true (line 45) and renders the open-in-app strip whenever appBannerOpen is true (line 121). The component receives no eventTypes count or isPaused prop from the page, so the banner renders unconditionally on every state: paused, empty, single-type, multi-type, and loading skeleton.
findings.evidence: booking-landing-frames.jsx:454 (FrameMulti only) vs BookerProfileHeader.tsx:45,121 (always shown); book/[slug]/page.tsx:107-114 passes no count/state prop to BookerProfileHeader; iOS BookingLandingView.swift:41 guards on view.eventTypes.count > 1; Android BookerLandingScreen.kt:174 guards on isEventList && state.eventTypes.size > 1.

findings.title: Footer missing 'View [name]'s profile' link on web
findings.designSays: Footer function in booking-landing-frames.jsx:374-393 renders a 'View {name}'s profile' button with arrow-up-right icon in sky blue (#0284C7) above the Powered-by line, whenever name is non-empty. Used in all frames except FrameError (which passes no name).
findings.implementationSays: PublicBookingFooter.tsx (lines 7-21) accepts no props and renders only CalendarClock + 'Powered by Pantopus'. No 'View profile' link exists anywhere in the web discovery components or in the page.tsx that composes the footer.
findings.evidence: booking-landing-frames.jsx:379-387 vs PublicBookingFooter.tsx:7-21; book/[slug]/page.tsx:133 renders <PublicBookingFooter/> with no arguments; iOS BookingLandingView.swift:243-253 has View {firstName}'s profile button; Android BookerLandingContent.kt:373-422 has LandingFooter with 'View $profileName's profile' + ArrowUpRight (non-navigating stub, onViewProfile=null passed from BookerLandingScreen.kt:197).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Both major findings are web-only. iOS and Android are correctly gated on the open-in-app banner (multi-type only) and both render the footer profile link (Android as a non-navigating stub). Web is furthest from the design on this screen: it shows the app banner unconditionally and omits the profile link entirely. iOS is closest to the design among the three platforms, with Android one step behind (profile link present but non-navigating).
screens.verifierSummary: Both draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the actual source files and the design JSX. Finding 1 (banner ungated on web): BookerProfileHeader.tsx:45 initialises appBannerOpen=true with no count/state prop passed in; the page.tsx at book/[slug]/page.tsx:107-114 does not forward eventTypes.length or isPaused to BookerProfileHeader, so the banner renders on every state. iOS BookingLandingView.swift:41 and Android BookerLandingScreen.kt:174 both correctly guard on size > 1. Finding 2 (missing profile link on web): PublicBookingFooter.tsx:7-21 is a zero-prop component that only renders the Powered-by line; no profile link exists anywhere in the web discovery layer. iOS BookingLandingView.swift:243-253 has the full button; Android BookerLandingContent.kt:385-406 has it as a non-navigating stub (onViewProfile=null). Both findings survive with high confidence at major severity. No new findings were uncovered during verification. Overall platform status: web=major, ios=minor, android=minor (unchanged from draft).

findings.title: Web slot rows rendered as a 2–3 column button grid, not a full-width list
findings.designSays: Each slot is a full-width row button (SlotRow): clock icon + time text on the left, chevron-right or check-circle-2 on the right, 11px 13px padding, no wrapping grid
findings.implementationSays: SlotGroup renders a CSS grid with grid-cols-2 (sm:grid-cols-3) — slots are compact pill-like buttons showing only the time label, no clock icon, no trailing chevron/check-circle
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:220–254 (SlotRow: width:'100%', clock icon, chevron/check-circle). SlotPicker.tsx:160 (grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 sm:grid-cols-3), SlotPicker.tsx:164–186 (no clock icon, no trailing icon).

findings.title: Web DST/timezone hint banner absent
findings.designSays: Frame 5 (tz/DST hint): an INFO_BG inline banner with info icon + 'Clocks change this weekend — times are adjusted.' displayed between the timezone chip and the calendar when a DST transition is imminent
findings.implementationSays: SlotPicker.tsx has no dstHint prop; PublicSlotPicker.tsx passes no DST hint. No INFO banner is rendered between the timezone chip and the calendar in either component.
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:126–137 (SummaryHeader tzHint branch renders INFO banner). SlotPicker.tsx:48–64 (no dstHint prop). PublicSlotPicker.tsx:84–93 (SlotPicker call has no dstHint argument).

findings.title: Slot-just-taken race-condition state not implemented in iOS or Android discovery picker
findings.designSays: Frame 6: when a race condition takes a slot, the slot row shows a strikethrough + 'Just taken' WARN pill badge, and a floating WARN toast banner appears at the bottom of the screen
findings.implementationSays: iOS DiscoverySlotPickerViewModel.slotPickerState returns only .loading / .noAvailability / .dayFull / .loaded — no taken state. Android SlotPickerUiState.Content has no slot-taken field and SlotRegion renders no taken variant. Neither platform shows a 'Just taken' badge or toast in the C2 discovery flow.
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:416–447 (FrameTaken: SlotRow taken prop, footer toast). DiscoverySlotPickerViewModel.swift:97–107 (slotPickerState: no taken branch). SlotPickerScreen.kt:427–478 (SlotRegion: no taken variant). SlotPickerViewModel.kt:285–311 (Content: no takenSlots field).

findings.title: Web slot-just-taken state not implemented in the slot picker
findings.designSays: Frame 6: when a race condition takes a slot, the slot row shows a strikethrough + 'Just taken' WARN pill badge, and a floating WARN toast banner appears at the bottom of the screen
findings.implementationSays: Web SlotPicker has no slot-taken/race-condition state; slots are simply removed from the list on reload. No 'Just taken' pill badge on any slot button, no toast banner. The disabled prop only dims all slots uniformly (reschedule cutoff use-case).
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:416–447 (FrameTaken: SlotRow with taken prop; footer toast). SlotPicker.tsx:60 (disabled prop only), SlotPicker.tsx:164–186 (no taken variant in slot buttons).

findings.title: Web reschedule mode banner ('Currently booked for...') absent from SlotPicker
findings.designSays: Frame 7 (Reschedule): an INFO Banner above the SummaryHeader reading 'Currently booked for Wed, Jun 17 at 9:30 AM / Pick a new time below — your old slot is released once you confirm.'
findings.implementationSays: RescheduleFlow.tsx wraps SlotPicker with a BookingSummaryCard and a current→new comparison widget (strikethrough old time + dashed new-time picker), but renders no INFO Banner component with the exact design copy. The design's banner is an INFO tone card; the web uses a sunken row with line-through text — a different visual treatment placed above the picker.
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:451–468 (FrameReschedule: Banner tone=info above SummaryHeader). RescheduleFlow.tsx:261–309 (BookingSummaryCard + current→new diff rows, no Banner component, no 'Currently booked for...' copy).

findings.title: Web reschedule-cutoff state (Frame 8: disabled picker + sticky 'Message host' CTA) not implemented inside the picker
findings.designSays: Frame 8: a WARN banner 'This booking can't be moved anymore / Message host', the summary header and calendar rendered at pointer-events:none (muted), and a sticky bottom bar with 'Message host' button
findings.implementationSays: RescheduleFlow.tsx handles policy.kind !== 'open' by replacing the entire picker with a BookingSummaryCard + PolicyCard + 'Message host in the app' link — the SlotPicker is not rendered at all in the cutoff state, so the disabled-picker-overlay and sticky footer from the design are absent. The PolicyCard copy differs from the design ('Reschedule window has closed' vs 'This booking can't be moved anymore').
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:473–509 (FrameCutoff: WARN banner + pointerEvents:none wrapper + sticky Message host footer). RescheduleFlow.tsx:195–235 (policy.kind !== 'open' branch: PolicyCard + Keep/Message links, SlotPicker absent). CutoffPolicyBlocked.tsx:125–133 (reschedulePolicyCopy kind=closed: title='Reschedule window has closed').

findings.title: Android DST hint banner absent from slot picker
findings.designSays: Frame 5: an INFO inline banner between the timezone chip and calendar when a DST change is imminent
findings.implementationSays: SlotPickerUiState.Content has no dstHint field. SummaryHeader (SlotPickerScreen.kt:216–250) renders only the event-type tile and timezone chip — no info banner. No DST hint is propagated from ViewModel to UI.
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:126–137 (SummaryHeader tzHint=true renders INFO banner). SlotPickerViewModel.kt:285–306 (Content data class: no dstHint field). SlotPickerScreen.kt:216–250 (SummaryHeader: no info banner rendered).

findings.title: Android reschedule-mode info banner and reschedule-cutoff warn banner not in the C2 slot picker
findings.designSays: Frame 7: INFO banner 'Currently booked for...' above SummaryHeader in reschedule mode. Frame 8: WARN banner 'This booking can't be moved anymore' + disabled picker + sticky 'Message host' CTA
findings.implementationSays: Android SlotPickerScreen is purely the new-booking discovery flow (C6); the RescheduleReassignSheet is a separate ModalBottomSheet (E4 flow) and does not render Frame 7/8 states inside the C2 picker. SlotPickerUiState has no reschedule/cutoff mode at all.
findings.evidence: slot-picker-frames.jsx:451–509 (FrameReschedule, FrameCutoff). SlotPickerScreen.kt:63–117 (no reschedule banner, no cutoff state). SlotPickerViewModel.kt:281–311 (SlotPickerUiState: Loading/Content/Error only, no reschedule mode). RescheduleReassignSheet.kt:61–80 (separate sheet for E4).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design on the C2 screen: it implements full-width SlotRow buttons via SchedulingSlotRow, the DST hint banner (dstHint param in SlotPicker.swift:39, rendered at lines 92–94), and the correct grouped Morning/Afternoon/Evening layout. Android matches iOS on slot layout (SlotTimeList renders full-width rows) but lacks the DST hint. Web diverges most significantly: it uses a 2–3 column button grid instead of full-width rows, omits the clock icon and trailing chevron/check-circle from every slot button, and has no DST hint. Frames 6 (slot-taken), 7 (reschedule banner), and 8 (cutoff) are missing from all three platforms in the C2 discovery picker; the slot-taken state (Frame 6) is unimplemented on iOS and Android as well as web. Android and web both omit Frame 7/8 entirely; iOS handles reschedule in a separate InviteeRescheduleView sheet but also lacks the Frame 7 'Currently booked for' INFO banner and the Frame 8 WARN banner + disabled-picker overlay in that sheet.
screens.verifierSummary: All 7 draft major/high-confidence findings were independently confirmed against the source. No finding was refuted or downgraded. One new cross-platform finding was added: the slot-just-taken race condition (Frame 6 — strikethrough slot row + WARN toast) is absent from iOS and Android discovery pickers, not just web. This upgrades iOS from 'minor' to 'major'. Key corrections: (1) Web reschedule-cutoff (Finding 5) is refined — RescheduleFlow.tsx does show a 'Message host in the app' link but replaces the picker entirely rather than overlaying a disabled picker, and the copy differs from the design. (2) Web reschedule banner (Finding 4) is confirmed absent as an INFO Banner component, though a different visual treatment (BookingSummaryCard + current→new diff rows) partially covers the intent. (3) iOS DST hint confirmed implemented (SlotPicker.swift:39, 92–94) — this was correctly not flagged by the auditor. Final platform statuses: ios=major (slot-taken missing, reschedule Frame 7/8 missing from InviteeRescheduleView), android=major (DST hint missing, slot-taken missing, reschedule Frame 7/8 missing), web=major (grid layout, no clock icon/chevron in slots, no DST hint, no slot-taken, no reschedule banner, no cutoff overlay).

findings.title: Frame 2 (Composing / team-intersect) not implemented in SlotPicker on any platform
findings.designSays: Frame 2 (FrameComposing, lines 344-359) shows a distinct interim inside the slot region: an AvatarCluster row with caption 'Finding times that work for everyone', 4 skeleton rows, and a ComposedPill ('Times come from each member's availability', calendar-range icon, accent-tinted background). This signals collective-intersect is in-progress (Business pillar shown) and is separate from the plain loading skeleton.
findings.implementationSays: iOS SlotPicker.swift LoadState enum (lines 19-28) has only four cases: loading/loaded/dayFull/noAvailability — no composing case. The .loading branch (lines 174-178) renders 6 SchedulingSlotRowSkeleton rows with no avatar cluster, no caption, and no ComposedPill. Android SlotPickerScreen.kt: the Loading state branch (lines 137-143) renders DiscoverySkeletonSlots(count=6) only. A composing state exists in other files (FindATimeSuggestedView.swift, SuggestedSlotsScreen.kt) but those are separate Home/find-a-time screens, not the SlotPicker itself. Web SlotPicker.tsx (lines 432-447): only animate-pulse shimmer rectangles. None of the three platforms implement the composing avatar-cluster caption or ComposedPill inside the slot picker.
findings.evidence: Design: slot-states-frames.jsx lines 344-359. iOS: SlotPicker.swift lines 19-28 (enum), 174-178 (loading branch). Android: SlotPickerScreen.kt lines 137-143. Web: SlotPicker.tsx lines 432-447.

findings.title: Frame 5 (Composed-empty / Home intersect) not implemented on any platform
findings.designSays: Frame 5 (FrameComposedEmpty, lines 401-419): a 'framed' EmptyCard with Home green (#F0FDF4) soft background, 1px accent-tinted border (not dashed), calendar-x icon, title 'Everyone's calendars don't overlap in this window', body 'These times need every required member free at once. Try widening the range.', a required-member free/busy dot cluster (AR/JL/MK avatars with green 'Free' / grey 'Busy' labels), primary CTA 'Try next month' (arrow-right icon), secondary 'Notify me' (bell icon). The design explicitly reserves calendar-x for this composed-empty frame only.
findings.implementationSays: iOS SlotPicker.swift has only four LoadState cases — there is no composed-empty state. The developer comment at line 216 ('calendar-x stays reserved for the composed-empty frame') acknowledges the deferred intent but is not an implementation. SlotPickerEmptyCard (lines 492-578) has no 'framed' variant — it always renders a dashed border and plain background, never a pillar-soft fill with accent-tinted solid border, and never a member avatar cluster. Android NoAvailabilityState.kt has no framed variant and no member-avatar cluster anywhere in the slot picker. Web SlotPicker.tsx has no composed-empty state.
findings.evidence: Design: slot-states-frames.jsx lines 401-419. iOS: SlotPicker.swift lines 19-28 (enum only 4 cases), 216 (deferred comment only), 492-578 (SlotPickerEmptyCard — no framed/avatar variant). Android: NoAvailabilityState.kt lines 52-117 (no framed variant, no avatar cluster). Web: SlotPicker.tsx lines 448-479 (no composed-empty state).

findings.title: Frame 4 (No times anywhere): 'Notify me' primary + 'Join waitlist (3 waiting)' secondary missing on all platforms; iOS conflates Frame 3 and Frame 4 into one state
findings.designSays: FrameNoAnywhere (Frame 4, lines 383-398) shows an EmptyCard with calendar-clock icon, title 'No times are open right now', body 'We'll let you know the moment something frees up.', primary CTA 'Notify me' (bell icon), secondary CTA 'Join waitlist' (users-round icon) with trailing chip '3 waiting'. Frame 3 (no-times-in-range) is distinct: calendar-search icon, 'No open times in June', primary 'See July', secondary 'Get notified when times open'.
findings.implementationSays: iOS LoadState has only one noAvailability case; the noAvailabilityCard (lines 256-268) implements Frame 3 copy ('No open times in {month}', primary 'See {next month}', secondary 'Get notified when times open') — there is no distinct Frame 4 path with 'Notify me' as primary. Android SlotRegion (lines 440-453): the !monthHasAvailability branch renders no secondary CTA at all (NoAvailabilityState is called without secondaryLabel/onSecondary). Web SlotPicker.tsx (lines 448-462): inline no-slots card only renders the month name and a 'See {NextMonth}' link — no bell icon, no notify-me, no waitlist.
findings.evidence: Design: slot-states-frames.jsx lines 363-398 (Frame 3 and Frame 4 distinct). iOS: SlotPicker.swift lines 19-28 (one noAvailability case), 256-268 (implements Frame 3 copy only, secondary is 'Get notified' not 'Join waitlist'). Android: SlotPickerScreen.kt lines 440-453 (no secondaryLabel/onSecondary passed); NoAvailabilityState.kt lines 61-63 (params exist but unused). Web: SlotPicker.tsx lines 448-462 (no bell, no waitlist).

findings.title: Web inline slot-picker no-availability card uses plain text link instead of a full EmptyCard with icon, body, and CTAs
findings.designSays: The EmptyCard component (design lines 233-310) shows: 50px icon halo, 15px/700 title, 12px body text, a filled accent primary CTA button (height 42), and a ghost secondary CTA button (height 40). The card uses 24px top / 18px side padding and 16px radius.
findings.implementationSays:
Web SlotPicker.tsx lines 448-463: the no-month-slots block renders a simple div with a

tag ('No open times in {month}') and a plain text
findings.evidence: Design: slot-states-frames.jsx lines 233-310 (EmptyCard). Web: SlotPicker.tsx lines 448-463.

findings.title: Android and iOS icon mismatch on Frame 3: calendar-search missing, calendar-clock used as fallback
findings.designSays: Frame 3 (FrameNoRange, line 370) specifies icon 'calendar-search' for the no-times-in-range state.
findings.implementationSays: iOS SlotPicker.swift line 258 uses .calendarSearch but the developer comment on lines 259-262 notes that PantopusIcon.calendarSearch maps to 'magnifyingglass' SF symbol (not a calendar glyph), with the calendar intent recorded as deferred. Android SlotPickerScreen.kt line 445 uses PantopusIcon.CalendarClock instead of calendar-search, noting 'The icon set has no calendar-search.'
findings.evidence: Design: slot-states-frames.jsx line 370 (icon='calendar-search'). iOS: SlotPicker.swift lines 258-262. Android: SlotPickerScreen.kt lines 444-446.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: All three platforms are missing the same two design-specified states (composing/Frame 2 and composed-empty/Frame 5) and the distinct Frame 4 'no times anywhere' CTA pattern. Android and iOS are closer to parity with each other than either is to the design: both implement Frame 3 (no-in-range) with a primary 'See next month' CTA but omit Frame 4's 'Notify me' primary and 'Join waitlist' secondary. Web is furthest from design on the slot-region empty states — its inline no-availability card is only a minimal two-line text block, lacking the full EmptyCard anatomy (icon halo, body copy, accent-filled primary button). The NoAvailabilityView/TerminalState full-page component is correctly scoped to the terminal 'fully_booked' route — the original auditor's Finding 4 (claiming it was misused inline) was a misread and has been dropped.
screens.verifierSummary: Confirmed Finding 1 (composing state) and Finding 2 (composed-empty state) exactly as stated — both verified in all three platform files. Confirmed Finding 3 (Notify me / Join waitlist CTAs) but expanded platforms from [android, web] to [ios, android, web]: iOS was incorrectly deemed 'better aligned' — it implements Frame 3 copy ('No open times in {month}', primary 'See next month') in its single noAvailability case and has no distinct Frame 4 path with 'Notify me' as primary. The secondary on iOS is 'Get notified when times open' (not 'Join waitlist'), and there is no chip. Refuted Finding 4 entirely: NoAvailabilityView/TerminalState is correctly used as a full-page terminal state for the /booking/[token]/states route (state='fully_booked') — it is not wired inside the inline SlotPicker and is not the component responsible for the C8 inline slot-region state. The real web inline issue (reduced EmptyCard anatomy — no icon, no body, plain text link only) is captured as a new minor finding. Added a nit finding for the icon fallback (calendar-search → calendar-clock/magnifyingglass) that both native platforms acknowledge in comments. Recomputed per-platform status to major for all three platforms given that two major missing states and one major CTA gap affect all platforms.

findings.title: Android missing override-banner state (Frame 4)
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (Overridden): info-colored banner with info icon, 'You changed this from your detected zone.' text, and 'Reset to detected' button with rotate-ccw icon in accent color, inside an INFO_BG/INFO_BORDER card.
findings.implementationSays: TimezonePickerSheet (TimezonePicker.kt:66-111) has no override-banner. The wrapper TimezoneSelectorSheet.kt also has no isOverridden check and passes no banner composable. The state is entirely absent from Android.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:309-349 (FrameOverridden) vs TimezonePicker.kt:66-111 and TimezoneSelectorSheet.kt:33-57 (no banner anywhere).

findings.title: Web missing Detected and Common section labels and separate list cards
findings.designSays: Default state: 'Detected' section label above a single-item ListCard (radius-16, shadow-sm), then 'Common' section label above a full ListCard containing all zones.
findings.implementationSays: Single flat <ul> with rounded-xl border, divide-y dividers, and all zones in one list. No 'Detected'/'Common' section labels, no separate card groupings. TimezoneSelector.tsx:156-194.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:241-255 (FrameDefault, SectionLabel + ListCard structure) vs TimezoneSelector.tsx:156-194.

findings.title: Web selected checkmark appears on RIGHT side of row instead of LEFT
findings.designSays: ZoneRow layout (left-to-right): 18px check slot as leftmost column, then name/badge column (flex:1), then offset+time column on the right.
findings.implementationSays: Row button uses justify-between: name span is leftmost, Check icon is conditionally rendered at the far right. TimezoneSelector.tsx:166-189.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:184-206 (ZoneRow: width:18 check div, then flex:1 name column, then right-aligned offset) vs TimezoneSelector.tsx:166-189.

findings.title: Web detected badge is unstyled inline text, not a pill chip
findings.designSays: Detected badge: sky-50 background (E.blue50), sky-700 text (E.blue700), pill shape (borderRadius:9999), 8.5px bold uppercase 'Detected'.
findings.implementationSays: Detected — no background, no border-radius, wrong color (muted gray instead of sky-700), normal weight, not uppercase. TimezoneSelector.tsx:171-174.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:193-198 vs TimezoneSelector.tsx:171-174.

findings.title: Web missing override banner; 'Reset to detected' shown as bare text link only
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (Overridden): full info-styled banner card (INFO_BG background, INFO_BORDER border, info icon, bold text 'You changed this from your detected zone.', then 'Reset to detected' button with rotate-ccw icon inside the card).
findings.implementationSays: Plain <button> with RotateCcw icon and text 'Reset to detected (CityName)', rendered outside any banner container. No info background, no info message text, no border. TimezoneSelector.tsx:136-145.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:316-349 (FrameOverridden override caption div) vs TimezoneSelector.tsx:136-145.

findings.title: Web 'Done' button absent from sheet header; replaced with X close icon
findings.designSays: SheetHeader: centered 'Time zone' title with a trailing 'Done' text button in sky accent color (ACCENT, fontWeight:700).
findings.implementationSays: BottomSheet.tsx renders title left-aligned in an h3 with a trailing X-icon close button (SVG cross icon, text-app-muted). There is no 'Done' text button. TimezoneSelector.tsx:119 passes title='Time zone'; BottomSheet.tsx:66-78 provides only the X close affordance.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:121-130 (SheetHeader Done button) vs BottomSheet.tsx:66-78 (X icon only) and TimezoneSelector.tsx:119.

findings.title: iOS override-banner uses inline horizontal layout instead of design's vertical card
findings.designSays: Override banner: a card (borderRadius:12) with flex-start alignment — info icon + text stacked vertically (flex:1), then 'Reset to detected' button below the description text.
findings.implementationSays: overriddenBanner (TimezoneSelectorSheet.swift:171-200) is a horizontal HStack: icon + text + Spacer + Reset button all in one row. The design shows icon + text block on the left with the Reset button below the text, not beside it.
findings.evidence: Design timezone-selector-frames.jsx:316-335 (vertical nested layout inside banner) vs TimezoneSelectorSheet.swift:171-200 (flat HStack).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the closest to design: it implements all four states (default, search, no-match, overridden-banner), section labels with separate list cards, left-side check slot, Detected pill chip, and Done button — a single minor layout deviation in the override banner. Android matches iOS for all default/search/no-match states and list structure but is entirely missing the overridden-banner state (Frame 4). Web diverges most: flat unstyled list replaces the two-card (Detected + Common) layout, the checkmark is right-aligned not left, the Detected badge is unstyled text, the override state is a bare link instead of an info banner, and the header has an X icon rather than a 'Done' text button.
screens.verifierSummary: All six major/blocker/low-confidence draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the actual source files. The Android finding (finding 1) was confirmed by reading both TimezonePicker.kt (the shared composable, lines 66-111) and TimezoneSelectorSheet.kt (the wrapper, lines 33-57) — neither has any isOverridden check or banner composable. The five web findings (findings 2-6) were all confirmed against TimezoneSelector.tsx and BottomSheet.tsx: section labels and separate cards are absent, the check is right-side (justify-between layout with Check at end), the Detected badge is an unstyled muted-color span, the override state is a bare button link with no banner card, and BottomSheet renders an X icon close button (not a 'Done' text button). The auditor's medium-confidence label on finding 6 ('Done' button) was upgraded to high after reading BottomSheet.tsx:66-78 directly. One additional minor iOS finding was added: the overriddenBanner uses a flat horizontal HStack rather than the design's nested vertical layout inside the card. No draft findings were dropped or downgraded.

findings.title: Android missing 'Get the app' CTA — dock is incomplete on every status
findings.designSays: Dock always contains three layers: optional dockExtra secondary CTA → filled 'Get the app' button (PILLAR color, height 46, smartphone icon) → ghost 'Back to Pantopus' link. The 'Get the app' button is unconditional — present on every status. terminal-state-frames.jsx:111–128 (Dock component, lines 119–126 hardcode the button).
findings.implementationSays: PausedExpiredUnavailableState renders only secondaryAction then primaryAction. UnavailableExpiredScreen maps the status-specific CTA (Notify me / Request a new link / Book again) as primaryAction and 'Back to Pantopus' as secondaryAction. No 'Get the app' button is passed at any call site. For NotFound/Secret/Unavailable states, primaryAction returns null (primaryActionFor else branch), leaving a one-button dock. BookerLandingScreen's terminal branch passes only primaryAction='Go back', no secondaryAction and no 'Get the app'. A grep for the literal string 'Get the app' across the entire Android scheduling tree finds only a KDoc example comment in PausedExpiredUnavailableState.kt — never a real button label.
findings.evidence: terminal-state-frames.jsx:119–126 (unconditional Get-the-app button in Dock). PausedExpiredUnavailableState.kt:142–155 (dock Column). UnavailableExpiredScreen.kt:92–93 (CTA wiring — no Get-the-app). BookerLandingScreen.kt:128–132 (terminal branch — no Get-the-app). grep result confirms zero live usages.

findings.title: Android dock button order inverted vs design — ghost renders above filled primary
findings.designSays: Dock order top-to-bottom: dockExtra (optional secondary CTA) → 'Get the app' (primary filled, height 46) → 'Back to Pantopus' (ghost). The filled button is always above the ghost. terminal-state-frames.jsx:111–128.
findings.implementationSays: PausedExpiredUnavailableState.kt:152–153 renders secondaryAction first (GhostButton), then primaryAction (PillarPrimaryButton). UnavailableExpiredScreen.kt:93 maps 'Back to Pantopus' → secondaryAction, so the ghost 'Back to Pantopus' is rendered ABOVE the filled status-specific CTA. This is the opposite of the design's filled-above-ghost order. The inversion is independently real from Finding 1: even when a status-specific primaryAction is present (Paused / FullyBooked / Expired / Cancelled), the ghost sits above the filled button.
findings.evidence: terminal-state-frames.jsx:111–128 (design: filled above ghost). PausedExpiredUnavailableState.kt:152–153 (Android render order: secondaryAction first, then primaryAction). UnavailableExpiredScreen.kt:93 (secondaryAction = 'Back to Pantopus' = the ghost).

findings.title: Web dock button order inverted — 'Back to Pantopus' rendered above 'Get the app'
findings.designSays: Dock order: 'Get the app' (primary filled) first, 'Back to Pantopus' (ghost) below it. terminal-state-frames.jsx:119–127.
findings.implementationSays: TerminalState.tsx:56–74 renders a flex-col gap-3 div where the 'Back to Pantopus' Link (lines 57–62) appears before the 'Get the app' anchor (lines 63–74). Result: 'Back to Pantopus' is visually above 'Get the app', which is the opposite of the design.
findings.evidence: terminal-state-frames.jsx:119–127 (design: Get-the-app then Back-to-Pantopus). TerminalState.tsx:57–62 (Back to Pantopus rendered first). TerminalState.tsx:63–74 (Get the app rendered second).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: All three findings are confirmed by direct source reading. Android has the two major CTA gaps (missing 'Get the app' button entirely, and ghost/filled order inverted). Web has the button order inversion (ghost above filled). iOS was not audited in these three findings. The design specifies a fixed dock structure on every terminal status; both Android and Web diverge from it, making Android the furthest from design and web a minor inversion away. The Android issues (Findings 1 and 2) share a root cause: the dock was designed as a pass-through slot system where 'Get the app' is hardcoded in the Dock component, but the Android implementation replaced that hardcoded button with a context-sensitive primaryAction slot and never added the 'Get the app' button back.
screens.verifierSummary: All three draft findings independently confirmed by reading the actual source files. Finding 1 (Android missing 'Get the app'): confirmed — the design's Dock component (terminal-state-frames.jsx:119–126) unconditionally renders a 'Get the app' filled button; no Android call site in the scheduling terminal-state path ever passes that label, verified by grep. The KDoc on PausedExpiredUnavailableState.kt says 'e.g. Get the app' as an example for primaryAction, but no caller implements it. Finding 2 (Android button order inverted): confirmed — PausedExpiredUnavailableState.kt:152–153 renders secondaryAction (the ghost 'Back to Pantopus') above primaryAction (the filled status CTA), opposite to the design's filled-then-ghost order. One minor imprecision in the original finding corrected: BookerLandingScreen's terminal branch (line 129–132) passes only primaryAction='Go back' with no secondaryAction at all — the auditor described it as 'shows only Back to Pantopus' which is wrong; the label is 'Go back'. The core claim (no 'Get the app') remains accurate. Finding 3 (Web button order inverted): confirmed — TerminalState.tsx:57–74 renders 'Back to Pantopus' Link before the 'Get the app' anchor within a flex-col container. The severity was left at major (not minor) because this is a structural dock inversion visible on every terminal-state page load, not a cosmetic detail. No findings were refuted or dropped.

findings.title: Web: "Booking as" prefilled chip entirely missing (Frame 2)
findings.designSays: Frame 2 shows a 'Booking as Maya Chen' chip under a 'Your info' overline, with the invitee avatar (violet gradient), name, email, and a 'Not you?' link in the accent color — replaces the editable name/email fields when the invitee is signed in.
findings.implementationSays: Web IntakeForm.tsx has no concept of isPrefilled or a BookingAsChip. It always renders the editable first/last/email fields. ConfirmFlow.tsx passes no prefill prop.
findings.evidence: Design BookingAsChip() at intake-booking-frames.jsx:424–446, used in FramePrefilled() at :472–481 and FrameSubmitting() at :531–541; iOS InviteeIntakeFormView.swift:68 (isPrefilled guard) and :201–238 (bookingAsChip view); Android IntakeFormScreen.kt:128–129 (isPrefilled guard) and :219–265 (BookingAsChip composable); Web IntakeForm.tsx:330–491 — no BookingAsChip or prefill concept anywhere.

findings.title: iOS: Existing-account-detected info banner missing (Frame 4)
findings.designSays: Frame 4 shows an info banner inside the 'Your info' section, directly below the email field: 'You have an account. Open in app (tappable underlined link) to use your saved details.'
findings.implementationSays: iOS InviteeIntakeFormView.swift has no existingAccount state and no InfoBanner. The view model (InviteeIntakeFormViewModel.swift) has no existingAccount field of any kind.
findings.evidence: Design FrameExistingAccount() at intake-booking-frames.jsx:508–527; InfoBanner() at :354–368; iOS InviteeIntakeFormView.swift — no 'existingAccount' or 'InfoBanner' found; iOS InviteeIntakeFormViewModel.swift — no 'existingAccount' field found; Android InviteeConfirmModels.kt:137 (existingAccount: Boolean = false) and IntakeFormScreen.kt:173–175 (ExistingAccountBanner guard) correctly implement this.

findings.title: Web: Existing-account-detected info banner missing (Frame 4)
findings.designSays: Frame 4 shows an info banner below the email field: 'You have an account. Open in app to use your saved details.'
findings.implementationSays: ConfirmFlow.tsx and IntakeForm.tsx have no existingAccount state and no info banner. No 'Open in app' copy exists anywhere in the web confirm path.
findings.evidence: Design FrameExistingAccount() + InfoBanner() at intake-booking-frames.jsx:508–527 / 354–368; Web IntakeForm.tsx:330–491 and ConfirmFlow.tsx:94–514 — grep for 'existingAccount', 'InfoBanner', 'Open in app', 'account' returned no hits in either file.

findings.title: Web: Phone field placed in 'Your info' section instead of 'A few questions'
findings.designSays: FrameDefault shows 'Your info' containing only First name, Last name, Email. Phone number is the second field rendered under the 'A few questions' section overline inside HostQuestions() at intake-booking-frames.jsx:265–289.
findings.implementationSays: Web IntakeForm.tsx renders Phone number as a static field inside the 'Your info' section (lines 377–387). The 'A few questions' section only renders when schema questions.length > 0 (line 392), so phone never appears there.
findings.evidence: Design HostQuestions() at intake-booking-frames.jsx:263–289; FrameDefault at :450–468 ('Your info' has no phone); Web IntakeForm.tsx:332–389 (phone inside 'Your info' section), :392 (conditional 'A few questions'); Android IntakeFormScreen.kt:182–208 correctly renders phone under 'A few questions'; iOS InviteeIntakeFormView.swift:244–311 treats phone as a schema questionField in questionsSection, also correctly in 'A few questions'.

findings.title: Web: Phone number not marked as required; Android also missing required indicator
findings.designSays: Phone number field is marked required in HostQuestions at intake-booking-frames.jsx:274 (<Field label="Phone number" required>), rendering a red asterisk next to the label.
findings.implementationSays: Web IntakeForm.tsx renders the phone field (lines 377–387) as a TextField with no required prop — no asterisk rendered. Android IntakeFormScreen.kt line 185 renders LabeledField(label = "Phone number") with no required = true — no asterisk rendered. iOS phone is schema-driven (line 290), so it correctly delegates required from the server payload.
findings.evidence: Design intake-booking-frames.jsx:274 `<Field label="Phone number" required>`; Web IntakeForm.tsx:377–387 — TextField with id='intake-phone', no required prop; Android IntakeFormScreen.kt:185 — LabeledField(label = "Phone number") with no required parameter (defaults to false per LabeledField signature at :569); iOS InviteeIntakeFormView.swift:291–301 passes question.required from the schema.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it correctly implements the BookingAsChip (prefilled state), correctly places phone under 'A few questions' as a schema-driven question, and correctly passes required from the server schema. It is only missing the existingAccount info banner (Frame 4). Android is close to design: it has BookingAsChip, ExistingAccountBanner, and correctly places phone under 'A few questions', but omits the required asterisk on the static phone field. Web diverges most: it lacks BookingAsChip, ExistingAccountBanner, places phone in the wrong section ('Your info' instead of 'A few questions'), and does not mark phone required. The web comment in confirmUtils.ts acknowledges the public API does not yet expose intake questions, which explains the static phone field fallback, but the section placement is still wrong relative to design. No cross-platform divergences found among iOS and Android beyond the specific missing features noted.
screens.verifierSummary: All five draft findings were independently confirmed against source code and the design frame. No findings were dropped or downgraded. Finding 5 was expanded: the auditor scoped it to web only, but direct reading of Android IntakeFormScreen.kt line 185 shows the static phone LabeledField also omits required = true, so Android is added as a co-failing platform on that finding. One nuance was noted: iOS treats phone entirely as a schema-driven question (field_type = 'phone') so its required status is delegated to the server — no static required gap. The design frame evidence was verified directly: FrameDefault (line 450–468) confirms 'Your info' has no phone; HostQuestions (lines 263–289) confirms phone is in 'A few questions' and required. The BookingAsChip (lines 423–446) and InfoBanner (lines 354–368) components exist in the design and are absent from web. A stale comment in Android IntakeFormScreen.kt (line 52) says phone is in 'Your info' but the actual composable code correctly places it under 'A few questions' — the code, not the comment, was used for the verdict.

findings.title: Timezone chip hardcodes Personal-pillar identity tokens on web (wrong for Home/Business hosts)
findings.designSays: The tz chip uses E.blue100 (#e0f2fe) / E.blue700 (#0369a1) — the neutral primary/sky palette — applied as a constant regardless of host pillar. These are NOT the identity-personal tokens; they are the shared primary-100/700 scale.
findings.implementationSays: Web BookingSummaryCard.tsx lines 199 and 206 render bg-app-personal-bg / text-app-personal. These map to --color-identity-personal-bg (#DBEAFE) and --color-identity-personal (#0284C7) — different hex values from the design's primary-100 (#e0f2fe) / primary-700 (#0369a1), and identity-personal tokens will NOT adapt for Home (green) or Business (violet) hosts. iOS and Android correctly use Theme.Color.primary100/primary700 and PantopusColors.primary100/primary700 (the neutral sky tokens).
findings.evidence: design: review-confirm-frames.jsx:174 `background:E.blue100, color:E.blue700`; colors_and_type.css: --color-primary-100:#e0f2fe, --color-primary-700:#0369a1; web: BookingSummaryCard.tsx:199,206 `bg-app-personal-bg` / `text-app-personal`; globals.css:60-61 --color-identity-personal:#0284C7, --color-identity-personal-bg:#DBEAFE; iOS: ConfirmSupport.swift:235 `.background(Theme.Color.primary100)` / `Theme.Color.primary700`; Android: ConfirmComponents.kt:138 `PantopusColors.primary100` / `PantopusColors.primary700`

findings.title: Apply package credit / promo code row absent on all three platforms
findings.designSays: FrameFullPayment (line 467), FrameDeposit (line 489), FrameSavedCards (line 534), and FrameCredit (line 514) all include an ApplyCreditRow component: a dashed-border button labelled 'Apply package credit or promo code' (tag icon, chevron-right) appearing below the RefundLink in the price section.
findings.implementationSays: None of the three platforms render this button. iOS priceSection (InviteeReviewConfirmView.swift:105-110) contains only totalsBox and depositNote; the ViewModel never builds a credit-apply row. Android ReviewConfirmBody (ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:75-91) shows PriceBreakdown + RefundLine only. Web ConfirmFlow.tsx review step (lines 429-456) renders ReviewSummary + cancellation policy only; no ApplyCreditRow.
findings.evidence: design: review-confirm-frames.jsx:276-287 ApplyCreditRow definition; lines 467, 489, 514, 534 usage in four frames; iOS: InviteeReviewConfirmView.swift:105-110 (no such row); Android: ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:75-91 (no such row); web: ConfirmFlow.tsx:429-456 (no such row)

findings.title: Package-credit-applied mode (CreditChip + credit price row) absent on all three platforms
findings.designSays: FrameCredit (Frame 4, line 500-518) shows TotalsBox with a 'Session credit' line (−$48.00 in SUCCESS green, line 510) and a CreditChip below ('1 session credit applied', ticket-check icon, green pill, lines 254-263). The CTA reverts to 'Confirm booking' with a check icon when credit covers the full balance.
findings.implementationSays: iOS has the credit styling hook in priceLineRow (InviteeReviewConfirmView.swift:163 `row.credit ? Theme.Color.success : ...`) but the ViewModel's priceRows computed property (InviteeReviewConfirmViewModel.swift:102-106) only ever produces the base event-price line — no credit row is generated from the public API. There is no CreditChip component. Android PriceBreakdown (ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:210-244) has no credit mode — only 'full' vs 'deposit'. Web ReviewSummary.tsx (all 109 lines) has no credit path, and ConfirmFlow.tsx has no credit state at all.
findings.evidence: design: review-confirm-frames.jsx:254-263 CreditChip; line 510 credit row (credit:true); iOS: InviteeReviewConfirmView.swift:163 credit styling hook present but InviteeReviewConfirmViewModel.swift:102-106 never emits a credit PriceRow; Android: ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:210-244 no credit branch; web: ReviewSummary.tsx (full file) no credit path; ConfirmFlow.tsx:429-456 no credit state

findings.title: Android hero total uses hardcoded primary600 instead of pillar accent
findings.designSays: n/a — no design (the design frame only shows the Personal pillar, where primary600 = accent; the cross-platform contract requires the hero total to use the host pillar accent for Home/Business hosts)
findings.implementationSays: Android TotalRow (ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:275) hardcodes `color = PantopusColors.primary600` for the hero price. iOS correctly uses `viewModel.accent` (InviteeReviewConfirmView.swift:177). Web ReviewSummary.tsx correctly uses `tk.text` (a pillar-resolved class). For a Home or Business host the Android hero total will show sky blue instead of the host's green or violet.
findings.evidence: Android: ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:275 `color = PantopusColors.primary600`; iOS: InviteeReviewConfirmView.swift:177 `foregroundStyle(viewModel.accent)`; web: ReviewSummary.tsx:63 `className={clsx(..., tk.text)}`

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it uses neutral primary-100/700 tokens for the tz chip (matching the design's E.blue100/E.blue700 intent) and resolves the hero total via the pillar accent. Android also uses primary-100/700 for the tz chip but hardcodes primary600 for the hero total (wrong for non-Personal pillars). Web uses identity-personal CSS vars for the tz chip (#DBEAFE/#0284C7 vs design's #e0f2fe/#0369a1) and does not adapt to Home/Business pillar. All three platforms lack ApplyCreditRow and the full credit-applied mode (CreditChip + credit price row + $0 total path).
screens.verifierSummary: All three draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the cited source files. Finding 1 (tz chip token mismatch on web) is confirmed and the evidence is sharpened: the design uses E.blue100 (#e0f2fe) / E.blue700 (#0369a1) as neutral primary tokens, while the web uses --color-identity-personal-bg (#DBEAFE) / --color-identity-personal (#0284C7) — distinct values that will misrender for Home/Business hosts. iOS and Android correctly use primary100/primary700. Finding 2 (ApplyCreditRow absent) is confirmed on all three platforms. Finding 3 (CreditChip + credit mode absent) is confirmed; the iOS observation about the credit styling hook in priceLineRow is accurate but partial — the hook exists at line 163 but no PriceRow with credit:true is ever produced by the ViewModel. One additional finding was added: Android's TotalRow hardcodes PantopusColors.primary600 for the hero price total (ReviewConfirmScreen.kt:275) instead of the pillar accent used by iOS and web, which will misrender for Home/Business hosts.
