Android ShareFab uses activeAccent(scope, scopes) (BookingsInboxScreen.kt:167-170), scope-tinted. Web FAB (page.tsx:362-372) uses tk.bg when activePillar is non-null (scope ≠ 'all'), pillar-tinted; only falls back to bg-primary-600 for the all scope.

findings.title: Android AssignedChip shows hardcoded 'Assigned' text, not the assignee name
findings.designSays: AssignedChip receives a name prop and renders it — e.g. 'Priya', 'Devon', 'Unassigned' (bookings-inbox-frames.jsx:170-176).
findings.implementationSays: Android AssignedChip composable (BookingsInboxScreen.kt:622-645) is parameterless and hardcodes text to 'Assigned', losing the actual assignee name or Unassigned distinction. Web primitives.tsx:76-83 correctly accepts and renders a label prop.
findings.evidence: Design bookings-inbox-frames.jsx:172 ({name} rendered). Android BookingsInboxScreen.kt:638-645 (Text(text = "Assigned") hardcoded). Web primitives.tsx:76-83 (label prop — correct).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design on the Approve CTA (uses primary600 correctly) and segmented accent (uses viewModel.accent which matches design intent per pillar). Android has the most divergences — 5 of 6 findings affect it. Web unexpectedly deviates on the Approve button accent and FAB accent, which the first-pass auditor did not flag (auditor treated web as 'minor'). All three platforms share the same two structural omissions: no member-gated banner and no hidePending logic. Android is the weakest platform due to the additional hardcoded primary700 in the segmented control and the hardcoded AssignedChip label.
screens.verifierSummary: All five draft major/high-confidence findings were independently confirmed by reading the actual source files and design frame. Two corrections were made: (1) Finding 4 (Approve CTA): the auditor cited Android only but Web BookingRow.tsx lines 111-115 uses tk.bg (pillar-tinted) instead of a fixed primary-600, making web a second wrongly-coloured platform — added to platforms list. (2) Finding 5 (FAB accent): the auditor cited iOS and Android only, but web page.tsx lines 362-372 also uses tk.bg when activePillar is non-null (any scope other than 'all'), making FAB pillar-tinted on Business/Home scopes — web added to platforms list. One new finding was added: Android AssignedChip (BookingsInboxScreen.kt:638-645) hardcodes the text 'Assigned' regardless of the actual assignee name, whereas the design passes the name via a prop and web renders it correctly. The draft's overall status field (ios:minor, android:major, web:minor) understated iOS (has major gaps: member-gated banner, hidePending, FAB accent) and understated web (has 4 major gaps); all three platforms are now rated 'major'.

findings.title: Web dock primary button uses pillar accent color instead of design's brand blue (#0284c7)
findings.designSays: BtnPrimary always uses PRIMARY = E.blue600 (#0284c7) — never the owner accent (booking-detail-frames.jsx:27, 193). iOS and Android both confirm: iOS dockPrimary uses Theme.Color.primary600; Android DockPrimaryButton comment reads 'always the brand blue primary600, never the owner accent'.
findings.implementationSays: Web dockClass 'primary' resolves to pillarTokens(pillar).bg: personal=bg-app-personal (#0284c7, accidentally matches), home=bg-app-home (#16a34a green), business=bg-app-business (#7c3aed violet). Home and Business bookings render a green or violet primary button instead of brand blue.
findings.evidence: booking-detail-frames.jsx:27,193 vs page.tsx:403-407 (dockClass returns tk.bg from pillarTokens); pillarTokens.ts:33-59; globals.css:60-65 confirms token color values; BookingDetailView.swift:298 (primary600); BookingDetailScreen.kt:1086-1101 (primary600, comment explicit)

findings.title: Web dock missing CTAs for completed, no-show, and cancelled states
findings.designSays: Frame 3 (completed): ghost 'Rebook' (rotate-ccw) + primary 'Follow up' (send). Frame 5 (no-show/status=noshow): ghost 'Message' (message-circle) + primary 'Send rebook link' (link). Frame 4 (cancelled): single primary 'Rebook this time' (rotate-ccw). (booking-detail-frames.jsx:292-297, 323-326, 353-356)
findings.implementationSays: Web completed state (status==='completed') renders at most a 'Message invitee' DockLink if messageHref is set (page.tsx:348-353) — no Rebook or Follow up. When booking status is 'no_show', web renders NO dock at all (the 'Mark no-show' branch fires for confirmed+elapsed, before no-show is recorded). Cancelled status has no dock branch. iOS handles all three states (BookingDetailView.swift:218-235). Android handles all three states (BookingDetailScreen.kt:1007-1043).
findings.evidence: booking-detail-frames.jsx:292-297,323-326,353-356 vs page.tsx:327-353 (no no_show branch, completed only shows message); BookingDetailView.swift:218-235 (completed/noShow/cancelled all have docks); BookingDetailScreen.kt:1007-1043 (same)

findings.title: Web missing conflict banner (design frame 6)
findings.designSays: Design frame 6 shows an amber 'This overlaps another booking' banner with a 'View' action button above the dock when hasConflict is true (booking-detail-frames.jsx:379, 196-203, ConflictBanner component).
findings.implementationSays: Web page.tsx and BookingDetailView.tsx contain no conflict banner UI — confirmed by grep returning no results for 'conflict' in either file. iOS renders conflictBanner (BookingDetailView.swift:118-154) and Android renders ConflictBanner (BookingDetailScreen.kt:770-807). Caveat: Android hardcodes hasConflict=false (BookingDetailModels.kt:153-155), so the banner never triggers at runtime on Android either — but the UI scaffolding is present for both native platforms while web has no scaffolding at all.
findings.evidence: booking-detail-frames.jsx:196-203, 379 vs page.tsx (no conflict banner); BookingDetailView.swift:118-154; BookingDetailScreen.kt:77,182-183,770-807; BookingDetailModels.kt:153-155

findings.title: Web dock backdrop-blur radius is 8px instead of design's 12px (opacity also 95% vs 97%)
findings.designSays: Dock: background 'rgba(255,255,255,0.97)' + backdropFilter blur(12px) (booking-detail-frames.jsx:185).
findings.implementationSays: Web Dock uses bg-app-surface/95 (95% opacity, delta=2%) with Tailwind class 'backdrop-blur' which resolves to blur(8px) by default — not blur-md (12px). The blur radius is 4px short of the design spec. The opacity gap (2%) is imperceptible; the blur gap is minor but measurable.
findings.evidence: booking-detail-frames.jsx:185 vs page.tsx:395 ('bg-app-surface/95 backdrop-blur'); tailwind.config.js has no custom backdropBlur override so Tailwind default blur(8px) applies

findings.title: Web missing dedicated Refund section card for cancelled bookings
findings.designSays: Design frame 4 (cancelled) shows a distinct 'Refund' SectionCard with receipt icon, 'Refunded to card' text, dollar amount, and refund policy note (booking-detail-frames.jsx:312-318).
findings.implementationSays: Web collapses refund information into the cancellation status banner as inline text '· refund issued' (BookingDetailView.tsx:99-101). No separate Refund card is rendered. iOS shows a dedicated refundLine card when refundIssued==true (BookingDetailSections.swift:80-90). Android shows a RefundLine composable (BookingDetailScreen.kt:497-518).
findings.evidence: booking-detail-frames.jsx:312-318 vs BookingDetailView.tsx:96-101 (inline text only); BookingDetailSections.swift:23-24,80-90 (refundLine card); BookingDetailScreen.kt:429,497-518 (RefundLine composable)

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and Android are closely aligned with each other: both use brand blue (primary600) for all dock primary buttons regardless of pillar, implement all five status-specific dock states (pending/confirmed/completed/no-show/cancelled), render a conflict banner scaffold (though Android hardcodes hasConflict=false so the banner never fires at runtime), and show a dedicated Refund section card for cancelled bookings. Web diverges on all four of these dimensions. iOS is closest to design; Android matches iOS for dock states and color but the hardcoded hasConflict=false means neither Android nor web surfaces the conflict banner in practice. The 3-way backdrop-blur mismatch exists only on web (native apps use native system APIs, not Tailwind).
screens.verifierSummary: All four draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the cited files. Finding 1 (pillar-colored primary button) is confirmed: iOS BookingDetailView.swift:298 and Android BookingDetailScreen.kt:1086-1101 explicitly document 'always primary600, never the owner accent,' while web pillarTokens.ts maps home/business to green/violet. Finding 2 (missing dock CTAs) is confirmed — web has no dock branch for status==='no_show' and the completed dock only shows 'Message invitee', missing 'Rebook' and 'Follow up'; iOS (lines 218-235) and Android (lines 1007-1043) handle all three states. A minor correction was applied: the auditor's claim that web's 'Mark no-show' fires AFTER the no-show is recorded is backwards — it fires for confirmed+elapsed (before recording); the actual gap is that after status transitions to 'no_show', web shows no dock. Finding 3 (conflict banner) is confirmed; additionally noted that Android hardcodes hasConflict=false (BookingDetailModels.kt:153-155), making the banner dead code on Android at runtime, while web lacks the scaffolding entirely. Finding 4 (backdrop blur nit) is confirmed: Tailwind 'backdrop-blur' = blur(8px) vs design's blur(12px); confidence upgraded to medium since the code path is unambiguous. One new finding was added: web shows refund information as inline banner text rather than a dedicated Refund SectionCard as shown in design frame 4 and implemented in both iOS (BookingDetailSections.swift:80-90) and Android (BookingDetailScreen.kt:497-518).

findings.title: Conflict-warning banner absent inside the ApproveDeclineSheet on Android and Web
findings.designSays: Frame 3 (FrameConflict) renders an amber ConflictBanner ('This slot overlaps a confirmed booking' + 'View conflict' link) inside the approve/decline sheet, between SlotLine and NoteInput, when a slot conflict exists. WARN_BG=#FFFBEB, triangle-alert icon.
findings.implementationSays: Android: ApproveDeclineSheet.kt has no ConflictBanner composable and ApproveDeclineSheetState (BookingDetailModels.kt:70-83) has no hasConflict field. A ConflictBanner composable does exist in BookingDetailScreen.kt (lines 770-807) and is wired at line 182-184, but it renders on the underlying detail screen — it is hidden behind the modal scrim when the sheet is open, so it is effectively invisible to the user during the approve/decline flow. Web: ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx has no conflict banner code at all.
findings.evidence: Design: approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 113-120 (ConflictBanner component), lines 198-209 (FrameConflict using it). Android: ApproveDeclineSheet.kt full file — no conflict refs; BookingDetailModels.kt:70-83 ApproveDeclineSheetState — no hasConflict field; BookingDetailModels.kt:153-155 hasConflict hard-coded false in BookingDetailData; BookingDetailScreen.kt:182-184 ConflictBanner() placed above dock, outside the sheet; BookingDetailScreen.kt:770-807 ConflictBanner composable. Web: ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx full file — no conflict-related code.

findings.title: Android conflict banner copy differs from design: 'This overlaps another booking' vs design 'This slot overlaps a confirmed booking'
findings.designSays: ConflictBanner text: 'This slot overlaps a confirmed booking' (approve-decline-frames.jsx line 117).
findings.implementationSays: Android BookingDetailScreen.kt:793 renders 'This overlaps another booking' — different phrasing, misses 'slot' and 'confirmed'.

findings.title: 'Propose another time' link missing in decline mode on Android and Web
findings.designSays: Frame 2 (FrameDeclineExpanded) includes a 'Propose another time' text+icon link (calendar-plus icon, PRIMARY blue) rendered inside ReasonChips, below the chip row (approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 153-155).
findings.implementationSays: Android: ReasonChips composable (ApproveDeclineSheet.kt lines 253-306) renders only the chip FlowRow with no link below, and ApproveDeclineSheet's parameter list has no onProposeTime callback. Web: decline branch (ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx lines 191-239) renders chips + textarea + error + buttons with no propose-time link and no onProposeTime prop.
findings.evidence: Design: approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 153-155. iOS: ApproveDeclineSheet.swift lines 174-185 — onProposeTime implemented and shown when non-nil. Android: ApproveDeclineSheet.kt lines 51-60 (no onProposeTime param), lines 253-306 (no link in ReasonChips). Web: ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx lines 191-239 — absent.

findings.title: Approve button uses pillar accent color instead of design-specified primary blue on Android and Web
findings.designSays: Actions component (approve-decline-frames.jsx line 126) uses PRIMARY = E.blue600 (#0284c7) for the Approve button background and its shadow ('0 6px 16px rgba(2,132,199,0.28)'), regardless of the booking's pillar.
findings.implementationSays: Android: PillarFilledButton(accent = state.pillar.accent) — violet for Business, green for Home, sky-blue for Personal (ApproveDeclineSheet.kt lines 120-122). Web: className uses ${tk.bg} ${tk.textOn} — resolves to bg-app-personal (#0284C7) / bg-app-home (#16A34A) / bg-app-business (#7C3AED) per pillarTokens.ts and tailwind.config.js. Only the Personal pillar accidentally matches design. iOS uses SheetCTAButton(tone: .primary) → Theme.Color.primary600 (fixed blue), which matches design.
findings.evidence: Design: approve-decline-frames.jsx line 20 (PRIMARY = E.blue600) and line 126 (background:PRIMARY). Android: ApproveDeclineSheet.kt lines 120-122 PillarFilledButton(accent = state.pillar.accent). Web: ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx line 172 ${tk.bg} ${tk.textOn}; pillarTokens.ts lines defining home/business bg; tailwind.config.js lines 55-60; globals.css lines 60-65 (--color-identity-home: #16A34A, --color-identity-business: #7C3AED). iOS: BookingSheetComponents.swift lines 275-277 (case .primary: Theme.Color.primary600).

findings.title: Intake answers row is non-interactive on Android and Web; iOS has expandable disclosure
findings.designSays: approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 96-101: IntakePreview renders as a button element with a chevron-down icon, implying it is tappable.
findings.implementationSays: Android: IntakePreview composable (ApproveDeclineSheet.kt lines 224-251) is a static Row with no clickable modifier — it shows the count but tapping does nothing. Web: the intake row (ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx lines 141-154) is a static div with no onClick. iOS: IntakeAnswersDisclosure (BookingSheetComponents.swift lines 108-170) is a properly expandable disclosure widget with animated chevron toggle.
findings.evidence: Design: approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 94-102. Android: ApproveDeclineSheet.kt lines 224-251 — no clickable, no chevron. Web: ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx lines 141-154 — static div. iOS: BookingSheetComponents.swift lines 108-170 — @State private var expanded, Button with chevron toggle.

findings.title: Web adds a 'Back' button in decline mode absent on Android and not in design
findings.designSays: FrameDeclineExpanded (approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 181-193) shows only a single 'Decline request' red button. No 'Back' or 'Cancel' button is specified.
findings.implementationSays: Web ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx lines 230-237 renders a 'Back' button below 'Decline request' that returns the user to review mode. Android and iOS have no such button (users dismiss via drag handle).
findings.evidence: Design: approve-decline-frames.jsx lines 188-193. Web: ApproveDeclineSheet.tsx lines 230-237. Android: ApproveDeclineSheet.kt — no back button in decline branch (lines 111-118). iOS: ApproveDeclineSheet.swift — no back button.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the closest platform to design: it has the correct primary-blue Approve CTA, the conflict banner with showConflictWarning parameter, the 'Propose another time' link, and an expandable intake disclosure. Android and Web share three major gaps: (1) conflict banner inside the sheet (Android misplaces it on the detail screen; Web omits it entirely), (2) missing 'Propose another time' link in decline mode, and (3) pillar-colored Approve button instead of the design-specified fixed primary blue. Web adds one extra nit not on Android: a 'Back' button in decline mode that is absent from the design frame.
screens.verifierSummary: All three draft blocker/major findings were independently confirmed by reading the actual source files. Finding 1 (conflict banner) is confirmed real but the original evidence statement was corrected: Android does have a ConflictBanner composable in BookingDetailScreen.kt (lines 770-807), but it is rendered on the detail screen above the dock — not inside ApproveDeclineSheet.kt — so it is hidden behind the modal scrim when the sheet is open. ApproveDeclineSheetState has no hasConflict field, and ApproveDeclineSheet.kt has no banner. The finding survives at major severity. Additionally, the copy in the Android banner ('This overlaps another booking', BookingDetailScreen.kt:793) differs from the design spec ('This slot overlaps a confirmed booking'); added as a nit. Finding 2 (propose-time link) is fully confirmed: zero references in both Android and Web sheet files. Finding 3 (approve button color) is confirmed: Android uses PillarFilledButton(accent = state.pillar.accent); Web uses pillar token ${tk.bg} resolving to green (#16A34A) for Home and violet (#7C3AED) for Business; iOS correctly uses SheetCTAButton(tone: .primary) → Theme.Color.primary600. Two additional findings were added from code observed during verification: (4) the intake-answers row is non-interactive on Android and Web while iOS has a proper expandable disclosure (confirmed minor cross-platform gap); and (5) the Web adds an extra 'Back' button in decline mode not in the design frame (nit).

findings.title: Web: Notify-invitee toggle entirely absent
findings.designSays: All four form frames (1–4, and frame 5) include NotifySwitch: bell icon + 'Notify invitee' label + pill toggle, placed last before the CTA footer (cancel-refund-frames.jsx:139-147, used at lines 157, 170, 183, 196, 209, 222).
findings.implementationSays: Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx has no notify state variable, no bell icon, no toggle UI element, and sends no notify flag in the cancelBooking API call. Only the file header comment (line 4) mentions 'notify'.
findings.evidence: Design cancel-refund-frames.jsx:139-147 (NotifySwitch function); iOS CancelRefundSheet.swift:27 (notifyInvitee state), 372-387 (notifyRow view); Android CancelRefundSheet.kt:572-604 (NotifySwitch composable). Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx: no notify reference anywhere in the component body.

findings.title: Web: Refund section is a static paragraph stub, not the full segmented-preset + money-rows UI
findings.designSays: Frames 2–4: RefundSection with (a) 'Refund' overline + receipt icon, (b) Full/Partial/Per-policy inset segmented control, (c) 'Paid' and 'Refund to card' money rows with divider, (d) per-preset policy copy, (e) entire section dims at opacity 0.55 when non-refundable (frame 4). cancel-refund-frames.jsx:105-124.
findings.implementationSays: Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx:183-199 renders only a rounded box containing the Receipt icon, 'REFUND' overline label, and one static sentence: 'This booking was paid. A refund is issued per the cancellation policy when you cancel.' No segmented control, no money rows, no policy copy variants, no non-refundable disabled/dimmed state.
findings.evidence: Design cancel-refund-frames.jsx:105-124 (full RefundSection); iOS CancelRefundSheet.swift:250-317 (refundCard + refundPresetPicker), 321-333 (refundMoneyRow); Android CancelRefundSheet.kt:264-323 (RefundCard + RefundPresetPicker + RefundMoneyRow); Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx:183-199 (stub).

findings.title: Web: Credit-redeemed restore-credit switch (Frame 5) not implemented
findings.designSays: Frame 5: CreditSwitch — ticket icon tile + 'Restore 1 session credit' title + 'Paid with a 5-session package' subtitle + pill toggle, replacing the money refund section when the booking was paid with a package credit. cancel-refund-frames.jsx:126-137.
findings.implementationSays: Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx has no credit-redeemed branch, no credit restore switch, and no package_credit_id detection. The `refundable` flag at line 59 only checks `booking.payment_id` — there is no `creditRedeemed` path.
findings.evidence: Design cancel-refund-frames.jsx:126-137 (CreditSwitch function), 208-209 (used in FrameCredit); iOS CancelRefundSheet.swift:56-58 (creditRedeemed computed var), 339-370 (restoreCreditCard view); Android CancelRefundSheet.kt:153-159 (creditRedeemed branch), 400-449 (RestoreCreditCard composable); Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx: no credit or package_credit_id reference.

findings.title: Web: Post-cancel success dismisses via toast+close instead of an in-place terminal state
findings.designSays: After a successful cancel, iOS and Android re-render the sheet in-place to a terminal confirmation scaffold (64pt circle-slash disc, 'Booking cancelled' title, outcome body copy, optional 'Refunded to card' row, ghost 'Done' button) that mirrors the FrameAlready read-only frame (cancel-refund-frames.jsx:244-262). The design's 8-frame set does not include a separate FrameSuccess, but the same terminal scaffold reuse in iOS/Android (and the standard Pantopus destructive pattern) makes the in-place confirmation the design intent.
findings.implementationSays: Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx:85-96: on success, toast.success() fires and onDone()+onClose() are immediately called — the sheet closes with no in-place succeeded state. There is no `succeeded` state variable. The alreadyCancelled branch (lines 123-146) does render a terminal view, but the post-submit success path does not.
findings.evidence: Web CancelRefundSheet.tsx:85-96 (submit success path: toast then onDone+onClose), 57 (no succeeded state); iOS CancelRefundSheet.swift:39 (succeeded state), 134 (succeeded = true), 181-182 (succeeded branch → cancelledView), 411-420 (cancelledView → terminalScaffold); Android CancelRefundSheet.kt:79-85 (state.succeeded → CancelTerminal), 482-549 (CancelTerminal composable).

findings.title: iOS + Android: Credit card copy omits the quantity specifiers from design
findings.designSays: CreditSwitch title: 'Restore 1 session credit'; subtitle: 'Paid with a 5-session package' (cancel-refund-frames.jsx:131-132). The '1' and '5' are specific to the design mock's sample data.
findings.implementationSays: iOS CancelRefundSheet.swift:348,351 uses 'Restore session credit' / 'Paid with a session package'. Android CancelRefundSheet.kt:435,441 uses the same generic strings. Both drop the count specifiers, which is arguably correct (the real count should be dynamic), but diverges from the design mock's text.
findings.evidence: Design cancel-refund-frames.jsx:131-132; iOS CancelRefundSheet.swift:348,351; Android CancelRefundSheet.kt:435,441.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and Android are closely aligned to each other and to the design on all major structural elements: both implement the full RefundSection (segmented preset + money rows + policy copy), the CreditSwitch/RestoreCreditCard, the NotifySwitch, the REFUND_FAILED error banner + retry CTA, and the in-place terminal state for both success and already-cancelled paths. The only cross-platform deviation found is the generic credit card copy strings vs. the design's count-specific text ('Restore session credit' vs 'Restore 1 session credit') — present on both iOS and Android. Web is the most divergent platform, missing 4 major features: the notify toggle, the full refund section UI, the credit switch, and the post-cancel terminal state. iOS is closest to design.
screens.verifierSummary: All four draft major findings independently confirmed by reading source files line by line. (1) Notify toggle: confirmed absent in web — no state, no UI, no API flag; design line 139, iOS line 372, Android line 572 all verified. (2) Refund section stub: confirmed — web lines 183-199 render only a static sentence; no segmented control, no money rows, no disabled dimming; iOS refundCard (250-317) and Android RefundCard (264-323) both implement the full design. (3) Credit switch: confirmed entirely absent in web — no creditRedeemed branch, no package_credit_id check; iOS (339-370) and Android (400-449) both implement it. (4) Post-cancel terminal state: confirmed — web immediately calls toast+onDone+onClose on success with no succeeded state variable; iOS has succeeded state at line 39→181 and Android state.succeeded at line 79, both rendering CancelTerminal. No draft findings were refuted or downgraded. One new nit finding added: iOS and Android both use generic copy strings for the credit card ('Restore session credit' / 'Paid with a session package') while the design mock specifies 'Restore 1 session credit' / 'Paid with a 5-session package' — likely intentional genericization but a visible divergence. The auditor's iOS/Android line citations were verified accurate to within 1-2 lines. Per-platform status unchanged: web=major, ios=minor, android=minor.

InitialsAvatar default is accent: Color = PantopusColors.business (BookingsExtrasUi.kt:404), so personal-owner bookings get the wrong accent. NoShowSheetState has no owner or accent field.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: correct 16.5sp title, owner.theme.accent avatar, full group roster + note field + 'Who didn't show?' title, correct user-x icon, and spec-exact font sizes. Android diverges on title size (20sp vs 16.5sp), avatar accent (hardcoded business default, no owner context), icon glyph (user-minus vs user-x), and body font sizes (14sp vs 13/12.5sp). Web diverges most severely: BottomSheet instead of centered modal, missing group checkbox roster + note field + dynamic title, neutral avatar background instead of identity accent. All three platforms agree on the dismiss-during-submit guard and the error inline display pattern.
screens.verifierSummary: All three scrutinized major findings CONFIRMED after reading source files. Finding 1 (Android h3 typography): confirmed — NoShowSheet.kt:85 uses PantopusTextStyle.h3 (20sp, Typography.kt:44) while ExtrasType.header at 16.5sp exists unused in BookingsExtrasUi.kt:62. Finding 2 (Android avatar accent): confirmed — NoShowSheet.kt:157 passes no accent param, falling to PantopusColors.business default (BookingsExtrasUi.kt:404); NoShowSheetState has no owner field; iOS correctly uses viewModel.owner.theme.accent (MarkNoShowDialog.swift:114). Finding 3 (Web group variant): confirmed and slightly broadened — web also lacks the 'Who didn't show?' title switch for group (NoShowSheet.tsx:132 always renders 'Mark as no-show?'). Added two new findings: Android icon glyph mismatch (UserMinus vs design's user-x); and web uses BottomSheet instead of centered dialog archetype. Also added a nit for Android body/subtitle font sizes (14sp via small vs 13/12.5sp spec). No findings were refuted or downgraded. Per-platform status retained: iOS minor (avatar accent on 1:1 is technically neutral background, iOS correct), Android major (three confirmed deviations), Web major (three confirmed deviations including missing group UI).

findings.title: Web: Private note section entirely absent
findings.designSays: PrivateNote section (lock-icon overline, textarea placeholder 'Outcome notes, next steps…', eye-off 'Only you can see this' caption) is a required structural block between the composer and the push toggle — followup-frames.jsx lines 99–110. Present in Frames 1, 2, and 3.
findings.implementationSays: FollowUpSheet.tsx has no private-note textarea, no lock icon row, and no 'Only you can see this' label. A comment at the top of the file (line 5–6) acknowledges the backend gap ('no private note persistence endpoint') and the sheet was scoped down to omit the element entirely.
findings.evidence: followup-frames.jsx:99–110 (PrivateNote component); FollowUpSheet.tsx:1–163 (no private-note block); iOS BookingFollowUpSheet.swift:111–132 (privateNote implemented); Android PostMeetingFollowupScreen.kt:165–181 (privateNote implemented)

findings.title: Web: Push-toggle row ('Send via push + message') absent
findings.designSays: PushToggle row with bell icon, 'Send via push + message' label, and a blue-primary on/off toggle is required — followup-frames.jsx lines 112–120. Present in Frames 1, 2, 3, and 5 (error).
findings.implementationSays: FollowUpSheet.tsx renders no push-toggle row at all. No Bell icon, no 'Send via push + message' text, and no toggle control exist anywhere in the component.
findings.evidence: followup-frames.jsx:112–120 (PushToggle component); FollowUpSheet.tsx:85–163 (no push toggle); iOS BookingFollowUpSheet.swift:134–144 (ExtrasChannelRow implemented); Android PostMeetingFollowupScreen.kt:183–190 (ExtrasChannelRow implemented)

findings.title: Web: 'Save note only' ghost CTA never rendered
findings.designSays: Frame 1 (Default, no outcome selected) shows a ghost lock CTA labeled 'Save note only' (followup-frames.jsx:126: cta='Save note only' ctaIcon='lock' ctaGhost). The solid blue CTA only appears when an outcome is selected.
findings.implementationSays: FollowUpSheet.tsx always renders a single solid blue button (bg-primary-600) regardless of state — it shows 'Sending…', 'Try again', or 'Send follow-up' but never the ghost 'Save note only' variant. Private note state is not tracked at all.
findings.evidence: followup-frames.jsx:126 (Sheet prop ctaGhost for default frame); FollowUpSheet.tsx:92–105 (single button, always bg-primary-600, no ghost variant); iOS BookingFollowUpSheet.swift:146–168 (primaryIsGhost branch implemented); Android PostMeetingFollowupScreen.kt:204–211 (isSaveNoteOnly ghost path implemented)

findings.title: Web: Active outcome chip uses info-blue tint instead of owner/pillar accent
findings.designSays: Selected chip background uses ID.home.bg (#dcfce7, green-100) and text uses ACCENT (#16a34a, green-600) — followup-frames.jsx lines 10–13, 82. Accent follows owner/pillar context.
findings.implementationSays: FilterChip in ui.tsx line 69 maps tone='neutral' to 'bg-app-info-bg text-app-info' (sky/blue tokens). FollowUpSheet.tsx passes tone='neutral' to every FilterChip (line 124), so selected chips render blue regardless of pillar. The pillar prop is explicitly voided at line 51 (`void pillar`), so no accent resolution occurs.
findings.evidence: followup-frames.jsx:10–13, 82 (ID.home.bg / ACCENT); FollowUpSheet.tsx:51 (void pillar), line 124 (tone='neutral'); ui.tsx:69 (neutral maps to info-bg/info); iOS BookingFollowUpSheet.swift:82–83 (selectedForeground: theme.accent, selectedBackground: theme.accentBg); Android PostMeetingFollowupScreen.kt:114 (accent = state.pillar.accent)

findings.title: Web: Success state (Frame 4) not implemented — sheet closes immediately
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (Sent): full-screen scrim over the sheet with a centered 72pt successBg/successLight-ringed disc + 'Follow-up sent' white headline + dark pinned toast 'Follow-up sent to Mara' — followup-frames.jsx:43–49.
findings.implementationSays: FollowUpSheet.tsx submit() on success calls toast.success(), onSent(), onClose(), and resets state (lines 73–77). The sheet closes immediately; there is no in-sheet success overlay at any point.
findings.evidence: followup-frames.jsx:43–49 (toast overlay with disc, headline, dark toast row); FollowUpSheet.tsx:73–77 (toast.success + onClose, no overlay); iOS BookingFollowUpSheet.swift:27 (if viewModel.didSend { successOverlay }), lines 172–218 (successOverlay/successDisc/successToast); Android PostMeetingFollowupScreen.kt:86 (state.didSend -> FollowUpSuccess), lines 235–276 (FollowUpSuccess composable)

findings.title: iOS/Android: Ghost 'Save note only' CTA shown only after private note is typed, not on blank open
findings.designSays: Frame 1 shows the ghost 'Save note only' CTA in the initial blank state (no outcome, no message, no private note typed yet) — followup-frames.jsx:126.
findings.implementationSays: Both platforms gate the ghost CTA on a non-empty private note: iOS BookingFollowUpViewModel line 80 `isSaveNoteOnly = trimmedMessage.isEmpty && outcome == nil && !trimmedNote.isEmpty`; Android line 47 `isSaveNoteOnly = outcome == null && message.isBlank() && privateNote.isNotBlank()`. On blank open, both show the disabled solid 'Send follow-up' CTA instead of the ghost lock CTA.
findings.evidence: followup-frames.jsx:126 (ghost CTA on FrameDefault with empty note); iOS BookingFollowUpViewModel.swift:79–81 (isSaveNoteOnly requires !trimmedNote.isEmpty); Android PostMeetingFollowupViewModel.kt:47 (isSaveNoteOnly requires privateNote.isNotBlank())

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Android is implemented as a full Scaffold screen (not a bottom sheet), while iOS uses a SwiftUI .sheet — both deliver the same content parity. iOS and Android are closely aligned: both implement all five design elements (private note, push toggle, ghost CTA, pillar-tinted chips, success overlay). The main cross-platform divergence is the subtle ghost-CTA gating logic (both platforms agree with each other but may diverge from the literal Frame 1 design intent). Web is the outlier, missing four structural elements: private note section, push toggle, ghost CTA variant, and success overlay — all omitted by deliberate implementation scope decisions documented in comments. iOS is the closest platform to the design frame.
screens.verifierSummary: REFUTED finding 6 (iOS drag indicator nit, low-confidence): .presentationDragIndicator(.hidden) combined with a custom ExtrasSheetGrabber() (36pt × 5pt Capsule, appBorderStrong fill) is an exact match to the design spec. Suppressing the system indicator while rendering a custom grabber is correct practice; the finding was based on a misread of the pattern. CONFIRMED findings 1–5 (all web major): independently verified by reading FollowUpSheet.tsx:1–163, ui.tsx:66–74, followup-frames.jsx:43–49/99–120/126, iOS BookingFollowUpSheet.swift:111–144/146–218, Android PostMeetingFollowupScreen.kt:165–276. Line numbers are accurate (minor off-by-one on ui.tsx:69 cited as 69 — correct — and FollowUpSheet.tsx line 124 cited as 123 — off by one, not material). ADDED new minor cross-platform finding: both iOS and Android gate the ghost 'Save note only' CTA on a non-empty private note, whereas Frame 1 of the design shows the ghost CTA in the completely blank initial state. Both platforms are internally consistent with each other on this behavior; confidence is low because the design intent is ambiguous. Per-platform status recomputed: web=major (4 confirmed major gaps), ios=minor (one minor behavioral quirk on ghost CTA gating), android=minor (same ghost CTA gating quirk).
