findings.title: Web error state: wrong icon, wrong disc shape, wrong headline, wrong CTA casing
findings.designSays: Error state (frame 6): 72px circular errorBg disc + cloud-off icon (32px, 1.8 stroke), headline 'Couldn't load the roster', secondary body copy, ghost 'Try again' CTA — roster-frames.jsx:251-256.
findings.implementationSays: Web uses generic ErrorState component: AlertTriangle icon (not cloud-off) in a 56px rounded-2xl (not circle) red-50 disc, hardcoded headline 'Something went wrong' (not 'Couldn't load the roster'), the passed message 'We couldn't load the roster.' appears only as secondary text, CTA says 'Try Again' (capital A, not lowercase). ErrorState.tsx:3,13-21; page.tsx:174.
findings.evidence: roster-frames.jsx:251-256 vs ErrorState.tsx:3-21 vs page.tsx:174

findings.title: iOS and Web: Confirmed stat uses success (#059669) not darker SUCCESS_DK (#047857)
findings.designSays: Confirmed stat value color is SUCCESS_DK = '#047857' (darker emerald) — roster-frames.jsx:16,73.
findings.implementationSays: iOS uses Theme.Color.success (CapacityRoster.swift:61) which maps to #059669. Web Stat component uses tone='success' → text-app-success → --color-success: #059669 (globals.css:46). Both are green (correct intent) but use the lighter shade, not SUCCESS_DK.
findings.evidence: roster-frames.jsx:16 vs CapacityRoster.swift:61 vs RosterSeats.tsx:78 vs globals.css:46

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it correctly uses cloud-off disc in the error state, correct headline, correct 'Try again' CTA, and renders Add or invite attendee in the host controls section. Android matches iOS on error state and host controls layout, but diverges on the Confirmed stat color (appText near-black vs green). Web diverges most: layout order inverted (host controls above attendees), add-attendee prop never wired, and generic ErrorState replaces the screen-specific error treatment. All three platforms share the FAB color issue (pillar accent vs fixed blue600) and a nit-level Confirmed stat shade (#059669 vs #047857).
screens.verifierSummary: All five draft blocker/major findings independently confirmed by reading source files. No findings were refuted or downgraded. Finding 1 (FAB color) confirmed in GroupRosterView.swift:294 and GroupRosterScreen.kt:141 — the JSX header comment 'accent follows owner context' was verified to refer to the capacity bar and avatar badge, not the FAB, so the auditor's reading of PRIMARY at line 52 is correct. Finding 2 (Android Confirmed stat appText) confirmed in CapacityRoster.kt:117; auditor's 'Color.kt line 133' citation for the token value is unnecessary but the core evidence stands. Finding 3 (web layout order) confirmed by tracing RosterSeats.tsx line by line: host controls div at line 85 precedes the Seated div at line 140. Finding 4 (onAddAttendee not passed) confirmed in page.tsx:200-209; additionally found a secondary issue that the web button label reads 'Add attendee' rather than 'Add or invite attendee'. Finding 5 (web error state) confirmed in ErrorState.tsx: AlertTriangle icon, rounded-2xl disc, hardcoded 'Something went wrong' headline, 'Try Again' CTA with capital A. One new finding added: iOS and Web both use the lighter success token (#059669) rather than the design's SUCCESS_DK (#047857) for the Confirmed stat value — nit severity, high confidence.

findings.title: Web filter sheet missing 'Owner context' facet section entirely
findings.designSays: filter-sheet-frames.jsx lines 105-108 (FrameDefault) and 133-134 (FrameActive): Section label='Owner context' with chips All / Personal / Home / Business; Business pre-selected in FrameDefault, indicating the sheet opens pre-scoped to the active owner pillar.
findings.implementationSays: BookingSearchFilter.tsx renders only Status (line 163), Event type (line 179), and Date range (line 195). No 'Owner context' section exists. The BookingFilters interface in filters.ts (lines 20-28) has no scope field at all. iOS BookingFilterSheet.swift lines 139-154 and Android BookingFilterSheet.kt lines 172-182 both implement this section correctly.
findings.evidence: filter-sheet-frames.jsx:105-108 and :133-134 vs BookingSearchFilter.tsx:162-234 (no owner-context block); filters.ts:20-28 (no scope field). iOS: BookingFilterSheet.swift:139-154 (ownerContextSection). Android: BookingFilterSheet.kt:172-182.

findings.title: Web no-results block appended after facets instead of replacing them
findings.designSays: filter-sheet-frames.jsx FrameNoResults (lines 145-163): when count=0 the scrollable body contains SearchField + ActiveSummary + the empty disc/headline/ClearAll block only. The four facet sections (Status / Owner context / Event type / Date range) are absent — they are replaced, not augmented.
findings.implementationSays: BookingSearchFilter.tsx: Status (line 163), Event type (line 179), and Date range (line 195) sections render unconditionally with no guard on noMatches. The noMatches empty block (line 236) is appended at the bottom, leaving all facets visible below it. iOS (BookingFilterSheet.swift:39-46) and Android (BookingFilterSheet.kt:155-158) both gate facets behind an else-branch so they disappear when count=0.
findings.evidence: filter-sheet-frames.jsx:145-163 (FrameNoResults body has no Section elements) vs BookingSearchFilter.tsx:162-250 (facets rendered unconditionally, noMatches block at line 236 appended after). iOS: BookingFilterSheet.swift:39-46 (if resultCount==0 { noResults } else { statusSection; ... }). Android: BookingFilterSheet.kt:155-158 (if count==0 { NoResultsBlock; return }).

findings.title: Web active-filter summary chips hardcode info-blue instead of per-semantic tint
findings.designSays: filter-sheet-frames.jsx ActiveSummary (lines 81-93) and FrameActive (lines 121-141): removable summary chips carry per-semantic color — Pending → amber (#B45309 bg #FFFBEB), No-show → error red (#DC2626 bg #FEF2F2), Business scope → violet (#7C3AED bg #F3E8FF), date → default blue.
findings.implementationSays: BookingSearchFilter.tsx activeChips array (lines 59-82) stores only { label, onRemove } — tone/color is never captured. Line 152 renders all summary chips with hardcoded className 'bg-app-info-bg text-app-info' (flat info-blue) regardless of status or scope. Note: the FilterChip facet buttons themselves DO carry per-tone color via ui.tsx CHIP_ACTIVE map — the defect is isolated to the ActiveSummary removable chips only. iOS correctly maps tint per chip via BookingFilterViewModel.ChipTint (BookingFilterViewModel.swift:178-195). Android ActiveFiltersSummary (BookingFilterSheet.kt:243-253) passes statusAccent()/bookingScopeAccent() per chip.
findings.evidence: filter-sheet-frames.jsx:86-90 (per-chip bg/color props) vs BookingSearchFilter.tsx:59-82 (no tone field) and :152 (hardcoded bg-app-info-bg text-app-info). ui.tsx:68-74 (CHIP_ACTIVE does support tones, but is not used here). BookingFilterViewModel.swift:154-170 (activeSummary with ChipTint). BookingFilterSheet.kt:243 (statusAccent per chip).

findings.title: Web search field placeholder copy diverges from design
findings.designSays: filter-sheet-frames.jsx line 59: placeholder text is 'Search invitee or intake text'.
findings.implementationSays: BookingSearchFilter.tsx line 126: placeholder is 'Search invitee name' — drops the 'or intake text' clause. iOS BookingFilterSheet.swift line 86 and Android BookingFilterSheet.kt line 140 both use the full design string.
findings.evidence: filter-sheet-frames.jsx:59 vs BookingSearchFilter.tsx:126 vs BookingFilterSheet.swift:86 vs BookingFilterSheet.kt:140.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and Android are closely aligned with each other and with the design: both implement the Owner context facet, both replace facets with the no-results block when count=0, and both apply per-semantic tints to active-filter summary chips. Web diverges on all three major areas (missing Owner context facet, appends rather than replaces facets on no-results, hardcodes info-blue on summary chips) and on the nit placeholder copy. iOS is the closest platform to design for this screen.
screens.verifierSummary: All three major findings were independently confirmed by reading the cited files. Finding 1 (missing Owner context on web): confirmed — no scope field in BookingFilters type and no owner-context section in BookingSearchFilter.tsx; iOS and Android both have it. Finding 2 (no-results replaces vs appends facets): confirmed — web renders all three facet sections unconditionally and appends the noMatches block at the end; iOS uses an if/else guard and Android uses an early return, both correctly hiding facets. Finding 3 (summary chip tinting): confirmed as stated — the activeChips array in BookingSearchFilter.tsx discards tone and the inline className hardcodes bg-app-info-bg/text-app-info for all chips; however the finding was refined: the FilterChip facet buttons themselves DO apply semantic tones via ui.tsx CHIP_ACTIVE, so the defect is scoped to the ActiveSummary removable chips only (the original finding title already says 'active-filter summary chips', so no correction needed). One additional nit was added: the web placeholder copy ('Search invitee name') truncates the design string ('Search invitee or intake text'), which iOS and Android reproduce correctly. No findings were refuted or downgraded. Per-platform status is unchanged: ios=minor, android=minor, web=major.

findings.title: Web: Hard-conflict frame entirely absent — only soft-overlap state exists
findings.designSays: Two frames: Frame 1 = soft overlap (amber disc + ConflictCard + Cancel / Book anyway); Frame 2 = hard conflict (error disc + green member note + disabled lock button + Cancel / Pick another member). double-book-frames.jsx lines 76–112.
findings.implementationSays: Web DoubleBookWarning.tsx (74 lines) renders a single hard-coded 'This time overlaps' title and CalendarClock disc feeding into SlotConflictAlternatives. No severity discriminator exists in the SlotConflict type (packages/types/src/scheduling.ts:435–439 — no severity field) and none in the component. There is no hard-conflict branch, no disabled lock button, no 'Member is unavailable' heading, no green member-availability note, and no 'Pick another member' CTA.
findings.evidence: Design: double-book-frames.jsx:93–112 (FrameHardConflict). Web: DoubleBookWarning.tsx:17–74 — single render path, no severity discriminator. SlotConflict type at packages/types/src/scheduling.ts:435–439 has no severity field at all.

findings.title: Web: ConflictCard (tappable linked event) missing even in soft-overlap state
findings.designSays: Soft-overlap frame shows a tappable ConflictCard row with wrench icon, event title, detail (time + calendar name), and chevron-right. double-book-frames.jsx:61–72 (ConflictCard component), used at line 82.
findings.implementationSays: Web DoubleBookWarning.tsx replaces the conflict card entirely with SlotConflictAlternatives (a slot-picker list showing nearest open times). No wrench card, no chevron, no clickable event row.
findings.evidence: Design: double-book-frames.jsx:61–72 (ConflictCard), :82 (usage in FrameSoftOverlap). Web: DoubleBookWarning.tsx:13 (SlotConflictAlternatives import), :62–69 (rendered in place of ConflictCard).

findings.title: Web: Primary CTA 'Book anyway' absent; replaced with slot-picker paradigm
findings.designSays: Soft-overlap CTA row: Cancel (ghost) + 'Book anyway' (solid primary blue, height 44). double-book-frames.jsx:84–86.
findings.implementationSays: Web omits 'Book anyway' entirely. The footer contains only a 'Cancel' button (DoubleBookWarning.tsx:41–48). File comment at lines 3–8 explicitly documents that the backend has no force-book endpoint, making this an intentional product divergence rather than a gap.
findings.evidence: Design: double-book-frames.jsx:85 (`<button>Book anyway</button>`). Web: DoubleBookWarning.tsx:41–48 — footer has Cancel only; no 'Book anyway' anywhere in file.

findings.title: Cross-platform: Web entirely lacks the hard-conflict variant present on iOS and Android
findings.designSays: n/a — no design
findings.implementationSays: iOS (DoubleBookWarningDialog.swift:44, 78–82, 144–175) and Android (DoubleBookWarning.kt:79, 113–165) both implement soft and hard severity branches with full UI including the member conflict pill, disabled lock button, and 'Pick another member' CTA. Web DoubleBookWarning.tsx has no severity parameter and no hard-conflict branch; the SlotConflict type (packages/types/src/scheduling.ts:435–439) itself carries no severity field.
findings.evidence: iOS: DoubleBookWarningDialog.swift:44 (`isHard`), :78–82 (hard branch), :144–175 (footer with hard/soft split). Android: DoubleBookWarning.kt:79 (`val hard = …`), :113–165 (hard UI). Web: DoubleBookWarning.tsx — no severity param; types/src/scheduling.ts:435–439 — SlotConflict has no severity field.

findings.title: Cross-platform: Web replaces ConflictCard + 'Book anyway' with a slot-picker alternative flow absent on iOS/Android
findings.designSays: n/a — no design
findings.implementationSays: Web introduces SlotConflictAlternatives (a slot-picker list with shimmer rows, nearest-open slot buttons, waitlist CTA, and 'Pick another time' ghost) as its conflict resolution UX — a fundamentally different interaction model. iOS and Android use a simple conflict card row + 'Book anyway' CTA without any slot-picker list. The web file comment acknowledges this divergence is intentional ('backend has no force-book').
findings.evidence: Web: DoubleBookWarning.tsx:13 (SlotConflictAlternatives import), :62–69 (rendered). SlotConflictAlternatives.tsx:49–167 (full slot-picker component). iOS: DoubleBookWarningDialog.swift:73–76 (ConflictCard) + :171 ('Book anyway'). Android: DoubleBookWarning.kt:110–111 (ConflictCard) + :169 ('Book anyway').

findings.title: Android: ConflictCard missing 1px border present in design and iOS
findings.designSays: n/a — no design
findings.implementationSays: iOS ConflictCard uses .strokeBorder(Theme.Color.appBorder, lineWidth: 1) (DoubleBookWarningDialog.swift:116–118). Android ConflictCard applies only background(PantopusColors.appSurfaceSunken) with no border stroke, omitting the 1px outline that separates the card from the background.
findings.evidence: iOS: DoubleBookWarningDialog.swift:115–119 (strokeBorder overlay). Android: DoubleBookWarning.kt:181–204 (ConflictCard composable) — no border modifier present.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: implements both soft and hard variants, ConflictCard with border, correct CTAs, member conflict pill. Android matches iOS except the ConflictCard lacks the 1px border stroke. Web diverges significantly — no hard-conflict variant at all, no ConflictCard, no 'Book anyway' CTA, and uses a SlotConflictAlternatives slot-picker paradigm instead; this is partially intentional (documented backend constraint: no force-book endpoint) but still leaves the hard-conflict member-unavailable state completely unhandled.
screens.verifierSummary: All five blocker/major findings from the draft were independently confirmed by reading the source files. No finding was refuted or downgraded. Key confirmations: (1) Web DoubleBookWarning.tsx (74 lines) is a single-path component with no severity discriminator; the SlotConflict type in packages/types/src/scheduling.ts:435–439 has no severity field, confirming the hard-conflict variant is structurally absent from the web stack, not merely hidden. (2) The ConflictCard component with wrench icon exists on both iOS (lines 90–123) and Android (lines 177–204) but has no web equivalent — SlotConflictAlternatives is an entirely different pattern. (3) 'Book anyway' is confirmed absent on web; the file comment at lines 3–8 acknowledges this as an intentional product decision due to a missing backend force-book endpoint — severity is kept major because the design specifies it and the hard-conflict branch is also absent. (4) iOS and Android hard-conflict branches are both fully implemented and verified. One new finding was added: Android ConflictCard omits the 1px border stroke that iOS implements and the design specifies (nit severity, medium confidence — the visual impact is subtle since the sunken background already separates it).

findings.title: Web: channel rows show static 'Auto' label instead of interactive Push/Email toggles
findings.designSays: Push row toggle ON, Email row toggle OFF — both rendered as toggle-switch shapes. Design file comment explicitly names 'push toggle' as part of the reused form archetype. iOS and Android both implement interactive bound toggles with state callbacks. (nudge-frames.jsx:106-114)
findings.implementationSays: Web renders both channel rows with a static 'Auto' uppercase label (NudgeSheet.tsx:197-198) — no toggle control, no user state. iOS uses ExtrasChannelRow with $viewModel.pushOn binding (SendNudgeSheet.swift:153-154). Android uses onPushChange/onEmailChange callbacks with checked state (NudgeSheet.kt:204-217).
findings.evidence: nudge-frames.jsx:106-114 vs NudgeSheet.tsx:184-201 vs SendNudgeSheet.swift:150-155 vs NudgeSheet.kt:203-218

findings.title: Web: success state replaced by ephemeral toast — full overlay not rendered
findings.designSays: Frame 3 (Nudge · Sent): full overlay inside the sheet — 72px green check disc (successBg background, successLight border), 'Update sent' headline, dark bottom toast bar 'Update sent to 12 attendees' with checkCircle2 icon. (nudge-frames.jsx:41-49, Sheet label='Nudge · Sent')
findings.implementationSays: Web calls toast.success('Reminder sent to N.') then immediately calls onClose() — the BottomSheet closes and no overlay is shown at all (NudgeSheet.tsx:83-90). iOS shows successOverlay for 1.3s before closing (SendNudgeSheet.swift:27, 158-199). Android shows NudgeSuccess composable with 72dp disc + dark toast bar (NudgeSheet.kt:126-129, 251-292).
findings.evidence: nudge-frames.jsx:41-49 vs NudgeSheet.tsx:78-94 vs SendNudgeSheet.swift:27,33-37,158-199 vs NudgeSheet.kt:126-129,251-292

findings.title: Web: audience section and header title conditional on group booking; iOS/Android always show group UI
findings.designSays: All four frames show 'Message attendees' header and the Audience chip row unconditionally. No single-invitee frame exists in the design. (nudge-frames.jsx:65-100)
findings.implementationSays: Web wraps audience chips in {isGroup && ...} guard (NudgeSheet.tsx:161) and conditionally shows 'Send a nudge' header for non-group (NudgeSheet.tsx:117). Web is called from bookings search with no attendees array, making isGroup=false for 1:1 bookings (search/page.tsx:255-260). iOS always shows 'Message attendees' (SendNudgeSheet.swift:73) and renders NudgeAudience.allCases unconditionally (SendNudgeSheet.swift:118). Android always shows 'Message attendees' (NudgeSheet.kt:135) and renders NudgeAudience.entries unconditionally (NudgeSheet.kt:166). Both native platforms are only called from the group roster.
findings.evidence: nudge-frames.jsx:65-100 vs NudgeSheet.tsx:117,161 vs SendNudgeSheet.swift:73,118 vs NudgeSheet.kt:135,166

findings.title: Web: 'Use a template' inserts hardcoded string instead of fetching API templates
findings.designSays: Template chip button labeled 'Use a template' with file-text icon (nudge-frames.jsx:77). Design does not show a picker UI but iOS and Android both implement an API-backed picker, establishing the intent.
findings.implementationSays: Web onClick directly sets text to hardcoded 'Quick reminder about your upcoming booking — see you then.' (NudgeSheet.tsx:129-133). iOS fetches GET /message-templates on demand and presents a confirmationDialog (SendNudgeViewModel.swift:109-121). Android uses a DropdownMenu populated from state.templates loaded from the same endpoint (NudgeSheet.kt:139-149).
findings.evidence: NudgeSheet.tsx:127-133 vs SendNudgeViewModel.swift:109-121 vs NudgeSheet.kt:139-149

findings.title: Web: no-recipients empty state missing 'users-round' icon; uses plain paragraph instead of icon+text row
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (No recipients): icon (users-round) + 'No one to message in this group' in a sunken rounded box. (nudge-frames.jsx:157-160)
findings.implementationSays:
Web renders a plain

tag without any icon (NudgeSheet.tsx:176-180). iOS renders UsersRound icon + text in a sunken border box (SendNudgeSheet.swift:131-146). Android renders PantopusIcon.UsersRound + text in a clipped sunken border row (NudgeSheet.kt:176-199).
findings.evidence: nudge-frames.jsx:157-160 vs NudgeSheet.tsx:176-180 vs SendNudgeSheet.swift:131-146 vs NudgeSheet.kt:176-199

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS and Android are closely matched to each other and to the design: both show interactive Push/Email toggles, a full-screen success overlay with 72dp check disc and dark bottom count toast, the audience chip row unconditionally, and an API-backed template picker. The main iOS/Android minor gap is that both only expose the nudge sheet from the group roster (never from 1:1 bookings search), so the single-invitee scenario is untested on native. Web diverges on all four major dimensions: static Auto labels instead of toggles, ephemeral toast instead of full overlay, conditional audience/header for 1:1 bookings, and hardcoded template text. iOS is closest to design.
screens.verifierSummary: All four draft major/high-confidence findings independently confirmed by reading the actual source files. Finding 1 (static 'Auto' label vs toggles): confirmed — NudgeSheet.tsx:197-198 is a static span; iOS SendNudgeSheet.swift:153-154 and Android NudgeSheet.kt:204-217 both use interactive bound channel rows. Finding 2 (ephemeral toast vs full overlay): confirmed — NudgeSheet.tsx:83-90 calls toast.success then onClose with no overlay; iOS successOverlay (SendNudgeSheet.swift:158-199) and Android NudgeSuccess (NudgeSheet.kt:251-292) both faithfully implement the design's Frame 3. Finding 3 (audience section/header conditional on web): confirmed — NudgeSheet.tsx:117 and :161 are guarded by isGroup; search/page.tsx:255-260 creates NudgeTarget without attendees for 1:1 bookings. Finding 4 (hardcoded template string): confirmed — NudgeSheet.tsx:129-133 hardcodes the string; SendNudgeViewModel.swift:109-121 fetches from API; NudgeSheet.kt:139-149 uses DropdownMenu from state.templates. One finding ADDED: web's no-recipients empty state omits the users-round icon (NudgeSheet.tsx:176-180 vs nudge-frames.jsx:157-160 and both native implementations). No findings were refuted or downgraded; all cited line numbers were verified accurate.

findings.title: Step 3 details: web shows flat simultaneous form — 'Invite by' channel selector entirely absent
findings.designSays: onbehalf-frames.jsx FrameStep3Invite (lines 210–235, Frame 4): when the invitee is not on Pantopus, step 3 shows a search field, a blue 'Not on Pantopus yet' banner, then an 'Invite by' section with Phone (recommended) and Email card-rows. The verified-invitee branch (Frame 3, lines 185–206) also uses a search field, not a flat multi-field form.
findings.implementationSays: ManualBooking.tsx lines 331–375: step 3 renders a flat form with Invitee name, Email, Phone (optional), and Note — all fields simultaneously, no search field, no channel selector, no directory lookup of any kind.
findings.evidence: Design: onbehalf-frames.jsx:210–235 (invite branch), 185–206 (verified branch). Web: ManualBooking.tsx:331–375.

findings.title: Step 3 details: Android 'Invite by' selector uses pill chips instead of design's card rows
findings.designSays: onbehalf-frames.jsx lines 222–231 (Frame 4): Phone and Email are full-width bordered card rows with icon, label, 'Recommended' subtitle, and trailing check-circle — same card-row pattern as event-type tiles.
findings.implementationSays: ManualBookingScreen.kt lines 320–324: uses ExtrasPillChip inside ExtrasChipFlow for the Phone/Email channel selector — pill chips, not card rows.
findings.evidence: Design: onbehalf-frames.jsx:222–231. Android: ManualBookingScreen.kt:320–324. iOS uses inviteByRow() card rows (ManualBookingView.swift:311–345) which match the design.

findings.title: Step 3 details: verified-invitee result card and 'not-on-Pantopus' invite banner missing on all platforms
findings.designSays: onbehalf-frames.jsx Frame 3 (lines 192–201): verified invitee card (avatar with initials, name, 'Verified neighbor · Riverside', badge-check badge, accent border) appears after a directory search. Frame 4 (lines 217–220): 'Not on Pantopus yet — invite them to book' blue info banner for unregistered invitees.
findings.implementationSays: iOS defers both elements explicitly (ManualBookingView.swift:288–291 comment: 'depends on an invitee directory the model lacks; deferred'). Android and web have no search-result card or invite banner — step 3 proceeds directly to channel/field entry without any directory resolution step.
findings.evidence: Design: onbehalf-frames.jsx:192–220. iOS: ManualBookingView.swift:288–291 (deferred). Android: ManualBookingScreen.kt:317–334 (no card/banner). Web: ManualBooking.tsx:331–375 (no card/banner).

findings.title: Step 4 review: web is missing 'Skip approval' and 'Skip notifications' toggles entirely
findings.designSays: onbehalf-frames.jsx lines 252–253 (Frame 5 / FrameStep4): both 'Skip approval' (toggle on, subtitle 'Confirm the booking now') and 'Skip notifications' (toggle off, subtitle 'Don't notify the invitee') are present as toggle rows below the summary card.
findings.implementationSays: ManualBooking.tsx lines 379–420: step 4 renders only the summary dl table and a conditional approval-required info banner when requires_approval is true. No toggle controls for skip-approval or skip-notifications exist.
findings.evidence: Design: onbehalf-frames.jsx:252–253. Web: ManualBooking.tsx:379–420 (no toggles). iOS has both toggles: ManualBookingView.swift:367–368. Android has both: ManualBookingScreen.kt:356–357.

findings.title: Created state: web primary CTA is 'Back to bookings' instead of 'View booking'
findings.designSays: onbehalf-frames.jsx line 270 (FrameCreated): primary CTA button (accent fill, full-width) is labelled 'View booking'. A secondary ghost button below it is 'Book another'.
findings.implementationSays: ManualBooking.tsx lines 192–201: the primary accent-styled button is labelled 'Back to bookings' and calls onBackToBookings(). A 'View this booking' text-link is rendered at lines 219–227 only when both createdId is set AND onCreated callback is provided — making it absent if the callback is not wired.
findings.evidence: Design: onbehalf-frames.jsx:270. Web: ManualBooking.tsx:200 ('Back to bookings'), 224 ('View this booking' conditional text-link). iOS: ManualBookingView.swift:432 ('View booking' as ExtrasSolidButton). Android: ManualBookingScreen.kt:386 ('View booking' as PrimaryButton).

findings.title: Step-rail circle size: Android and web use 24px circles vs design's 22px
findings.designSays: onbehalf-frames.jsx line 44: step-rail circles are 22×22px.
findings.implementationSays: Android ManualBookingScreen.kt line 145: Modifier.size(24.dp). Web ManualBooking.tsx line 69: h-6 w-6 (24px). iOS ManualBookingView.swift line 87: width:22, height:22 — matches design.
findings.evidence: Design: onbehalf-frames.jsx:44. Android: ManualBookingScreen.kt:145. Web: ManualBooking.tsx:69. iOS: ManualBookingView.swift:87.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to the design: it has the search field, 'Invite by' card-row selector (Phone/Email), channel-conditional input field, both Skip toggles in review, and 'View booking' as the primary CTA on the created screen. The only iOS gap is the deferred verified-invitee directory card and not-on-Pantopus banner (explicitly noted in code). Android has the channel selector but renders it as pill chips rather than design's card rows, and is otherwise on-par with iOS. Web diverges most severely: step 3 is a flat simultaneous form (no search, no channel mode), step 4 review lacks both Skip toggles, and the created screen uses 'Back to bookings' as the primary CTA instead of 'View booking'.
screens.verifierSummary:
All four flagged draft findings were independently confirmed in the source.

Finding 1 (web step 3 flat form / no Invite-by selector): CONFIRMED. Web ManualBooking.tsx:331–375 collects name + email + phone + note simultaneously with no search field or channel-mode selector. The design has two step-3 frames — both use a search-field paradigm, not a flat multi-field form. iOS and Android correctly implement the Invite-by channel pattern.

Finding 2 (verified-invitee card and not-on-Pantopus banner missing on all platforms): CONFIRMED. iOS ManualBookingView.swift:288–291 explicitly defers both elements. Android and web have neither element.

Finding 3 (web step 4 missing Skip approval / Skip notifications toggles): CONFIRMED. Web ManualBooking.tsx:379–420 has only a summary dl and a conditional info banner. iOS ManualBookingView.swift:367–368 and Android ManualBookingScreen.kt:356–357 both implement the toggles correctly.

Finding 4 (web created screen CTA mismatch): CONFIRMED. Primary button at ManualBooking.tsx:200 is 'Back to bookings'; 'View this booking' is a conditionally-rendered text-link at line 224, not a primary button. iOS (line 432) and Android (line 386) both use 'View booking' as the primary button matching the design.

Two additional findings added during verification: (a) Android uses ExtrasPillChip for the Invite-by selector instead of the full-width card rows specified in the design (iOS card-row implementation is correct); (b) Android and web use 24px step-rail circles vs the design's 22px (nit, iOS is correct at 22px).

findings.title: Join form: contact field is Email/email-keyboard instead of Mobile/phone on iOS and Android (web is correct)
findings.designSays: Frame 1 (FrameJoin): second field label is 'Mobile', placeholder 'For a text when a spot opens', icon 'phone'. The backend notifies by text/SMS.
findings.implementationSays: iOS: field label 'Email', icon .mail, keyboardType .emailAddress (WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:59-65). Android: field label 'Email', placeholder 'For an alert when a spot opens', leadingIcon PantopusIcon.Mail, keyboardType KeyboardType.Email (WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:110-118). Web correctly uses label 'Mobile', hint 'For a text when a spot opens', inputMode='tel' with a phone state variable (WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:179-187) — web should be dropped from this finding.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx:141 (Field label='Mobile' icon='phone'). iOS WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:59-65. Android WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:110-118. Web WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:179-187 (correct).

findings.title: Join form and confirmation: iOS uses 'email' in body copy where design says 'text'
findings.designSays: Frame 1 intro copy: 'we'll text you the moment a spot opens.' Frame 2 confirmation subtitle: 'we'll text you if a spot frees up.'
findings.implementationSays: iOS WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:51 reads 'we'll email you the moment a spot opens.' and the joinedConfirmation at line 163 reads 'We'll email you the moment a spot opens.' Android (WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:92) correctly says 'we'll text you the moment a spot frees up.' Web (WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:109) says 'We'll text you the moment a spot opens.' — also correct.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx:138 ('text you'). iOS WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:51 ('email you'), line 163 ('email you'). Android WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:92 ('text you' — correct). Web WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:109 ('text you' — correct).

findings.title: Joined confirmation: queue-position badge (#N in line) is absent on iOS and Android; web has conditional logic but backend never populates position
findings.designSays: Frame 2 (FrameJoined) and Frame 3 (FrameAlready): a pill badge showing '#3' (bold accent color) + 'in line' (fg2) on accentBg background, borderRadius:11. The position is the invitee's queue rank.
findings.implementationSays: iOS: joinedConfirmation block (WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:150-175) shows icon disc + title + subtitle + Done button only; no position badge. Android: JoinedConfirmation composable (WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:188-223) shows icon + title + subtitle + Done button only; no position badge. Web: position badge rendered conditionally when result.position is a positive number (WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:112-123), but the join API request (WaitlistJoinViewModel.kt, WaitlistJoinViewModel.swift) sends only email+name and the response type carries no position field, so the badge never renders in practice.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx:161-162 (position pill). iOS WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:150-175 (no badge). Android WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:188-223 (no badge). Web WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:112-123 (conditional — never fires). iOS WaitlistJoinViewModel.swift:59-68 (response carries no position). Android WaitlistJoinViewModel.kt:88-93 (response carries no position).

findings.title: Joined confirmation: CTA is 'Done' instead of 'Leave waitlist' (ghost style) on all platforms
findings.designSays: Frame 2 (FrameJoined): footer CTA label is 'Leave waitlist', ctaTone='ghost' (outlined/surface button). Allows the invitee to remove themselves after joining.
findings.implementationSays: iOS: ExtrasGhostButton title='Done' (WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:169). Android: GhostButton title='Done' (WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:221). Web: button reads 'Done' (WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:80-87). No platform provides a 'Leave waitlist' action. WaitlistJoinViewModel.swift header comment (lines 8-9) acknowledges 'no leave endpoint' as a backend gap.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx:154 (cta='Leave waitlist' ctaTone='ghost'). iOS WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:169. Android WaitlistJoinSheet.kt:221. Web WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:80-87. iOS WaitlistJoinViewModel.swift:8-9 (backend gap comment).

findings.title: 'Already on waitlist' state (Frame 3) missing on iOS and Android; web has the branch but subtitle omits join date
findings.designSays: Frame 3 (FrameAlready): when the invitee is already on the waitlist, show a distinct state with clock icon on accentBg disc, title 'You're already waiting', subtitle with join date ('You joined this waitlist on Jun 11. We'll text you the moment a seat opens.'), and the #N position badge.
findings.implementationSays: iOS: no alreadyJoined branch; WaitlistJoinUiState has only didJoin (WaitlistJoinViewModel.swift, WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:28). Android: WaitlistJoinUiState only has didJoin: Boolean (WaitlistJoinViewModel.kt:35); no alreadyJoined distinction. Web: alreadyJoined branch exists with Clock icon and 'You're already waiting' title (WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:74, 98-107), but subtitle at line 108-110 is generic 'We'll text you the moment a spot opens.' — no join date.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx:171-185. iOS WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:28 (only didJoin). iOS WaitlistJoinViewModel.swift (no alreadyJoined field). Android WaitlistJoinViewModel.kt:27-39 (only didJoin). Web WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:74-110 (alreadyJoined branch present, subtitle missing join date).

findings.title: Host management screen: CapacityHeaderCard missing entirely from web
findings.designSays: Frames 4 and 5 (host views): a CapacityHeader card at the top showing 'N of M seats filled · K waiting', a fill bar with accent color (grayed when full), inside a surfaced card with border and rounded corners.
findings.implementationSays: WaitlistManager.tsx (lines 54-97) renders only a SectionOverline ('N waiting') and a waitlist row list; there is no capacity card. The page.tsx does not pass filled/total counts into WaitlistManager (props at WaitlistManager.tsx:15-26: pillar, eventTypeName, waitlist, promotingId, onPromote only). iOS WaitlistManagementView.swift:57-65 and Android WaitlistScreen.kt:119-125 both implement CapacityHeaderCard correctly.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx:98-108 (CapacityHeader component), 192-193 (usage in FrameHostOpen). WaitlistManager.tsx:15-26 (props — no filled/total), 54-97 (rendered JSX — no capacity card). iOS WaitlistManagementView.swift:57-65. Android WaitlistScreen.kt:119-125.

findings.title: Android empty-state for 'no event types' uses wrong icon and misleading copy (not a design frame — minor issue)
findings.designSays: The design provides no frame for a 'no event types' empty state. The only host-view empty state shown is 'No one's waiting' (implicit in Frame 4 when entries list is empty), with a clock icon.
findings.implementationSays: Android WaitlistScreen.kt:70-75: WaitlistUiState.Empty branch (triggered when event types list is empty) uses PantopusIcon.Users and headline 'No event types yet'. The 'No one's waiting' state is correctly implemented inside WaitlistContent (WaitlistScreen.kt:127-134) with PantopusIcon.Clock when entries are empty. The outer Empty state is a functionally distinct condition the design doesn't cover, but the Users icon and copy are inconsistent with the waitlist context. iOS takes an eventTypeId argument and never reaches a 'no event types' screen-level state.
findings.evidence: Design waitlist-frames.jsx (no 'no event types' frame). Android WaitlistScreen.kt:70-75 (outer empty — Users icon, 'No event types yet'). Android WaitlistScreen.kt:127-134 (inner empty — Clock icon, 'No one's waiting' — correct). iOS WaitlistManagementView.swift:38-44 (only one empty state: clock + 'No one's waiting').

findings.title: Android WaitlistViewModel hard-codes pillar and owner to Personal regardless of owner context
findings.designSays: The design uses the host's owner pillar accent throughout (Business violet #7c3aed in the demo frames). The accent must be owner-polymorphic (Personal/Home/Business).
findings.implementationSays: Android WaitlistViewModel.kt:63: private val owner: SchedulingOwner = SchedulingOwner.Personal is hardcoded. WaitlistViewModel.kt:136: pillar = SchedulingPillar.Personal is hardcoded in WaitlistData construction, regardless of resolved owner or nav args. iOS resolves the pillar correctly via viewModel.owner.theme (WaitlistManagementView.swift:20). Web resolves it via pillarForOwner(owner.ownerType) from the URL query (page.tsx:42).
findings.evidence: Android WaitlistViewModel.kt:63 (owner hardcoded to Personal), 136 (pillar hardcoded to Personal). iOS WaitlistManagementView.swift:20 (correct). Web page.tsx:42 (correct).

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Web is closest to design on the join form: it correctly uses a Mobile/phone field (label 'Mobile', inputMode='tel') and collects phone not email, matching the design's intent that notification is by SMS. iOS and Android both collect email instead, which contradicts both the design label and the SMS-notification promise in the copy. On the host view, iOS and Android both implement the CapacityHeaderCard correctly; web omits it entirely. The 'already on waitlist' state (Frame 3) is most complete on web (has the branch and Clock icon) but all three platforms miss the join date in the subtitle and the position badge is effectively absent everywhere due to the backend not returning a queue position. The Android WaitlistViewModel hardcodes Personal pillar, making it the worst offender on owner-polymorphism; iOS and web resolve the pillar from context correctly.
screens.verifierSummary:
Confirmed 7 of the 7 draft findings with corrections; added 1 new finding; downgraded 1 draft finding's severity.

CONFIRMED: Findings 2, 3, 4, 5, 7 are confirmed as described with minor evidence corrections.

CORRECTED: Finding 1 — web was incorrectly listed as a platform with the Email/phone bug. Web correctly implements the Mobile field with inputMode='tel' (WaitlistJoinSheet.tsx:179-187). Platforms corrected to iOS and Android only.

DOWNGRADED: Finding 6 (Android empty-state headline) — downgraded from major to minor. The 'No event types yet' outer empty state is a functionally distinct condition not covered by any design frame. The inner 'No one's waiting' state is correctly implemented within WaitlistContent. This is an unspecified edge case, not a design-parity violation against an existing frame.

ADDED: New finding — iOS uses 'email' in body copy (join form intro and confirmation subtitle) where the design says 'text'. Both the form intro (WaitlistJoinSheet.swift:51) and the joined confirmation subtitle (line 163) say 'email you' while design frame 1 and frame 2 say 'text you'. Android and web correctly say 'text you'. This is a minor copy parity issue unique to iOS and consistent with the iOS VM's decision to key on email instead of phone.

All file:line citations were verified against actual source. No draft findings were refuted outright.

findings.title: Web: Offline frame not implemented — no banner, no row dimming
findings.designSays: FrameOffline (home-calendar-frames.jsx:122-139) shows an amber 'You're offline' Banner above the MonthStrip, all event rows dimmed via a dim prop, no FAB, and the 'Who's free' button muted.
findings.implementationSays: HomeAgenda.tsx has no network monitoring, no offline state variable, no amber banner, and no row dimming. The calendar/page.tsx wrapper also contains no isOnline/NetworkMonitor logic. The component simply shows an error state if fetch rejects, with no distinction between a network outage and a server error, and no cached-data rendering path.
findings.evidence: Design: home-calendar-frames.jsx:122-139. Web: frontend/apps/web/src/components/scheduling/home/HomeAgenda.tsx — no 'offline', 'online', 'navigator', or 'NetworkMonitor' imports at all. Web: frontend/apps/web/src/app/(app)/app/homes/[id]/calendar/page.tsx — no offline state. Android reference (correct): HomeCalendarScreen.kt:79-80 (val online by viewModel.isOnline) plus OfflineCalendarBanner composable.

findings.title: iOS: 'Who's free' top-bar action renders icon-only; design specifies icon + label
findings.designSays: All frames: TopBar right={{ icon:'users', label:"Who's free" }} — icon and text label shown side-by-side in the trailing position (home-calendar-frames.jsx:42, 63, 89, 107, 125, 146).
findings.implementationSays: iOS renders only Icon(.users, size:20) inside the ToolbarItem; there is no accompanying Text('Who\'s free') in the button label. Android correctly renders both icon and text (HomeCalendarScreen.kt:201-212).
findings.evidence: Design: home-calendar-frames.jsx:42. iOS: HomeCalendarView.swift:40-48 (Icon-only inside ToolbarItem label, no Text sibling). Android: HomeCalendarScreen.kt:201-212 (PantopusIconImage + Text 'Who\'s free' in a Row).

findings.title: Cross-platform: iOS 'Who's free' muted color uses appTextMuted; Android uses appTextMuted too — both correct
findings.designSays: FrameOffline: right={{ icon:'users', label:"Who's free", muted:true }} — the trailing action is visually muted when offline.
findings.implementationSays: iOS correctly switches tint to Theme.Color.appTextMuted when offline and disables the button. Android also switches PantopusColors.appTextMuted. Both match the design intent.
findings.evidence: Design: home-calendar-frames.jsx:125. iOS: HomeCalendarView.swift:39-46. Android: HomeCalendarScreen.kt:205-206.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: Android is the closest to the full design spec: it renders both icon and text in the 'Who's free' button, has the offline banner with correct amber styling, and has event row dimming on offline. iOS is functionally complete but shows icon-only in the trailing top-bar action (missing the 'Who's free' label text). Web has a structural route split: the admin-facing PermissionGate hub (/scheduling) is a separate F15 screen, not F1; the actual F1 route (/calendar) renders the agenda correctly with title and 'Who's free', but lacks the entire offline state (no banner, no stale data rendering, no row dimming).
screens.verifierSummary: Three of the four submitted major findings were refuted entirely. Findings 1, 2, and 3 all resulted from the auditor comparing the wrong web route to the design: they cited /homes/[id]/scheduling/page.tsx (which is the F15 permission-gated hub — a separate screen) instead of /homes/[id]/calendar/page.tsx (the true F1 implementation). The /calendar route correctly renders HomeAgenda with title 'Calendar' and a 'Who's free' icon+button in the header. Finding 4 (offline state missing on web) was confirmed independently: HomeAgenda.tsx has no network monitoring code whatsoever, no amber banner, and no row dimming — the offline frame from the design is fully absent on web. Evidence was corrected to cite the right files. One new finding was added: iOS renders the 'Who's free' toolbar action as icon-only, while the design specifies icon+label and Android correctly implements both. Android passes all checks and is upgraded to 'match'. The web 'major' status is retained solely for the missing offline frame.

findings.title: CategoryPill: iOS uses icon glyph instead of design's 7 dp dot
findings.designSays: CatChip (home-shell.jsx:156-157) renders a 7×7 px colored dot (`c.c`) + label text in N.fg2 on N.sunken background — no icon glyph
findings.implementationSays: iOS `CategoryPill` renders `Icon(category.icon, size: 10, color: category.foreground)` + label; the icon replaces the dot entirely
findings.evidence: home-shell.jsx:156-157; EventDetailView.swift:562-571

findings.title: DetailGrid: iOS missing row separators specified by design's DetailRow component
findings.designSays: home-shell.jsx:413 DetailRow sets `borderBottom: last ? 'none' : '1px solid N.border'` between all rows except the last

findings.implementationSays:
Web `

` uses fg3 (#6b7280). iOS uses `Theme.Color.appTextStrong` and Android uses `PantopusColors.appTextStrong` — both correct

findings.title: Web: RSVP offline state entirely missing
findings.designSays: event-detail-frames.jsx Frame 5 (FrameOffline) shows an amber wifi-off banner above content, 'Your RSVP' overline in N.fg4, and the segmented control at opacity 0.5 with a 'RSVP buttons are disabled until you reconnect.' note
findings.implementationSays: Web `EventDetailRsvp.tsx` has no `navigator.onLine` / network check. The RSVP segmented control is only disabled during `rsvpSaving`, not when offline. No offline banner shown. iOS applies `.offlineBanner(isOffline:)` modifier and passes `rsvpEnabled: NetworkMonitor.shared.isOnline`; Android collects `viewModel.isOnline` and passes it as `rsvpEnabled`

findings.title: Cross-platform: 'Repeats' row always shown on web ('Does not repeat' fallback) vs conditionally hidden on iOS/Android
findings.designSays: n/a — no design
findings.implementationSays: Web always renders the Repeats row via `recurrenceLabel()` which returns 'Does not repeat' when no rule is set (helpers.ts:308-318). iOS hides the row when `recurrenceRule` is nil/empty (EventDetailView.swift:617-619); Android likewise skips it (EventDetailScreen.kt:375). Web is closer to design intent since the design always shows 4 rows
findings.evidence: helpers.ts:308-318; EventDetailView.swift:617-619; EventDetailScreen.kt:373-376

findings.title: Cross-platform: RSVP 'maybe' recorded copy diverges (web 'You replied Maybe' vs iOS/Android 'You might go')
findings.designSays: n/a — no design (design only shows 'going' variant)
findings.implementationSays: Web returns 'You replied Maybe' for the maybe branch (EventDetailRsvp.tsx:300-301). iOS `recordedTitle(.maybe)` returns 'You might go' (EventDetailView.swift:900); Android's `recordedTitle` likewise returns 'You might go' (EventDetailScreen.kt:642). Web also shows 'You replied Maybe' while native platforms are self-consistent
findings.evidence: EventDetailRsvp.tsx:300-301; EventDetailView.swift:900; EventDetailScreen.kt:641-642

findings.title: Web: Recorded RSVP state always shows Check icon in successBg regardless of choice
findings.designSays: n/a — no design (design only shows 'going' variant with successBg/check icon)
findings.implementationSays: Web hardcodes `bg-app-success-bg` + `<Check>` icon for all recorded states, even 'maybe' or 'can't' (EventDetailRsvp.tsx:290-293). iOS and Android use `choice.background` and `choice.icon` which adapt per-choice (e.g. warningBg/help-circle for maybe, errorBg/x for can't)
findings.evidence: EventDetailRsvp.tsx:290-293; EventDetailView.swift:866-870; EventDetailScreen.kt:531-536

findings.title: Web: No 'Edit' action in top-bar area (only in footer, gated by canEdit)
findings.designSays: event-detail-frames.jsx:101 TopBar carries `right={{ text:'Edit' }}` visible to all viewers in the loaded state
findings.implementationSays: Web page.tsx has a back button + 'Event' title h1 only; the Edit button exists solely in the sticky footer and is hidden when `canEdit` is false. This means non-editors see no edit affordance at all, matching access control intent, but the layout differs from the design's dual edit placements
findings.evidence: event-detail-frames.jsx:101; page.tsx:29-41; EventDetailRsvp.tsx:344-358

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is the most aligned on RSVP pill icons, recorded-state adaptive colors, and offline handling, but diverges on CategoryPill (uses lucide icon instead of dot) and lacks DetailGrid row separators. Android matches the design on CategoryPill (dot), row separators, and offline state, but the GhostButton footer lacks a pencil icon. Web diverges most severely: it is the only platform missing the offline RSVP state, has icon-less RSVP pills in the attendee list, uses the wrong notes text color (fg3 vs fg2), and hard-codes successBg/Check for all recorded RSVP states. iOS is closest to design on typography and color tokens; Android is closest on structural layout (dot chip, dividers). Web is furthest from design on three distinct areas.
screens.verifierSummary: Verified all 11 total findings by reading the authoritative design frame (event-detail-frames.jsx + home-shell.jsx for shared primitives) and every cited source file. No blocker/major/low-confidence findings were present in the draft; all analysis was done from scratch. Key confirms: (1) The design's DetailRow component DOES specify `borderBottom` separators between rows — iOS's own comment claiming 'no inner dividers' is incorrect; Android and web are correct to show them. (2) CatChip in the design uses a 7 px dot, not a lucide icon — confirmed iOS is the divergent platform. (3) The offline RSVP state (Frame 5) is a real design requirement; web has zero offline detection, making this the only major finding. (4) Web notes text uses `text-app-text-secondary` (fg3) while the design specifies N.fg2 (appTextStrong) — real color mismatch. (5) Web RSVP pills are text-only (no icon) while design and iOS/Android all include a lucide icon. Added two new confirmed minor findings not in the draft: Android footer Edit button missing pencil icon (GhostButton has no icon param), and web's recorded RSVP state hardcoding successBg/Check regardless of the chosen status. Downgraded the web top-bar Edit finding to medium confidence since it is partly an access-control difference rather than a pure design divergence.

findings.title: Save/action button color uses sky primary600 instead of Home green on iOS and Android
findings.designSays: SheetBar action button (Save) renders in H.accent700 (#15803d, Home dark green) when enabled — home-shell.jsx:378
findings.implementationSays: iOS FormShell renders the right-action label in Theme.Color.primary600 (sky #0284c7) when enabled — FormShell.swift:228. Android FormShell renders it in PantopusColors.primary600 (0xFF0284C7) — FormShell.kt:323-325. Web correctly uses text-app-home (Home green) on the Save button — AddEditEventForm.tsx:229.

findings.title: Offline banner: web has no offline detection or banner
findings.designSays: FrameOffline (add-event-frames.jsx:156-169): amber Banner with wifi-off icon + 'You're offline / This event saves when you reconnect.' appears above the Title section when offline.
findings.implementationSays: Web AddEditEventForm has no offline detection (no navigator.onLine, no network event listener, no wifi/offline reference) and renders no offline banner. iOS correctly renders AddEventOfflineBanner when !NetworkMonitor.shared.isOnline — AddEventFormView.swift:55-56. Android correctly renders OfflineFormBanner when offline — AddEventFormScreen.kt:129,171.
findings.evidence: /tmp/calendarly_design/calendarly feature/Calendarly/add-event-frames.jsx:156-169; frontend/apps/web/src/components/scheduling/home/AddEditEventForm.tsx (no offline detection found); frontend/apps/ios/Pantopus/Features/Homes/Calendar/AddEventFormView.swift:55-56; frontend/apps/android/app/src/main/java/app/pantopus/android/ui/screens/homes/calendar/AddEventFormScreen.kt:109,129,171,200-218

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it correctly implements the Home-green save button (via FormShell which uses primary600 — this is a shared component gap), the full saving overlay with dimmed content and centered card, and the inline amber offline banner. Android matches iOS on offline detection/banner but shares the same FormShell primary600 save-button color bug and lacks the saving overlay entirely (FormShell.kt only swaps the save button for a spinner). Web correctly uses Home green for the enabled Save button, but is missing both the saving overlay and the offline banner, making it the furthest from design on state management. The save-button color gap on iOS/Android is a FormShell-level issue affecting all form screens, not just F3.
screens.verifierSummary: All three major/high-confidence draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the actual source files and design frame. (1) Save button color: home-shell.jsx:378 unambiguously uses H.accent700 (#15803d); iOS FormShell.swift:228 and Android FormShell.kt:323-325 both use primary600 (#0284c7/0xFF0284C7, confirmed via Color.kt:31); web AddEditEventForm.tsx:229 correctly uses text-app-home. Finding confirmed, evidence line numbers corrected to 323-325 for Android. (2) Saving overlay: add-event-frames.jsx:111-131 shows the dimmed-content + centered card pattern; iOS AddEventFormView.swift has SavingOverlay struct at lines 178-201 applied via .overlay at line 71 — confirmed correct; Android FormShell.kt:313-317 only shows a CircularProgressIndicator replacing the save button with no dim/overlay (a misleading comment at AddEventFormScreen.kt:100 claims 'saving overlay' but none exists); web AddEditEventForm.tsx:222-223 only shows inline Loader2. Finding confirmed for Android and Web. (3) Offline banner: FrameOffline at add-event-frames.jsx:156-169 specifies an amber Banner; iOS AddEventFormView.swift:55-56 correctly gates on !NetworkMonitor.shared.isOnline; Android AddEventFormScreen.kt:109,129,171 correctly gates on isOnline StateFlow with OfflineFormBanner composable at lines 200-218; web AddEditEventForm.tsx has zero offline detection or banner. Finding confirmed. Per-platform status upgraded from minor to major on all platforms given the confirmed major gaps. No additional clear discrepancies were observed beyond the three verified findings while reading the code.

findings.title: Android: no-overlap warning banner and quick-fix buttons are absent
findings.designSays: Frame 5 (FrameNoOverlap) shows a warning Banner (tone=warning, icon=triangle-alert, title='No time works for all 3') at the top of SheetBody, plus an inline 'Make Dad optional' button inside Who's needed and a 'Widen to two weeks' button inside Date window.
findings.implementationSays: FindATimeSetupScreen.kt SetupFormBody (lines 127–241) has no reference to a no-overlap state, FtBanner, or any suggestion buttons. FtBanner is fully implemented in FindATimeComponents.kt (lines 109–159) but never called. The VM has makeSomeoneOptional() (line 151) and widenWindow() (line 170) but SetupForm has no noOverlapMessage field and SetupFormBody never calls FtBanner or renders those buttons. iOS (FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 83–85, 214–228, 443–461) does implement the full no-overlap state.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx lines 207–223 (FrameNoOverlap). Android: FindATimeSetupScreen.kt lines 127–241 (no banner call); FindATimeSetupViewModel.kt lines 34–58 (SetupForm has no noOverlap field); FindATimeComponents.kt lines 109–159 (FtBanner exists but unused). iOS: FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 83–85, 139–159, 214–228, 443–461 (fully implemented).

findings.title: Web: primary CTA is 'Find times' + Search icon instead of 'Next' text action in top bar
findings.designSays: SheetBar carries a trailing text button 'Next' (SheetBar action='Next') in every frame. No bottom submit button is shown.
findings.implementationSays: FindATimeSetup.tsx renders a bottom full-width green button (lines 467–479) labelled 'Find times' with a Search icon. page.tsx header (lines 186–207) has a back arrow link but no 'Next' action. The setup form submits directly via the bottom CTA rather than a top-bar action.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx line 122 (SheetBar title='Find a time' action='Next'). Web: FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 467–479; page.tsx lines 186–207.

findings.title: Web: computing state uses a Loader2 spinner only — no 64dp arc, no users icon, no shimmer rows
findings.designSays: Frame 4 (FrameComputing) shows a 64×64 spinning arc with a users icon at centre, a heading 'Checking everyone's availability', a subtitle, and 3 shimmer rows (avatar shimmer + text shimmer + badge shimmer).
findings.implementationSays: page.tsx computing step (lines 222–239) renders only a Loader2 spinner (h-12 w-12, no arc/users icon) + heading + subtitle text. No shimmer rows are present. iOS (FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 484–519) fully implements the arc + users icon + 3 shimmer rows matching the design.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx lines 183–204. Web: page.tsx lines 222–239 (animate-spin Loader2 only, no shimmer). iOS: FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 484–519 (arc + shimmer rows match design).

findings.title: Web: no-overlap state is not implemented
findings.designSays: Frame 5 shows a warning banner and inline quick-fix buttons ('Make Dad optional' inside Who's needed, 'Widen to two weeks' inside Date window) when no overlap exists.
findings.implementationSays: Neither FindATimeSetup.tsx nor page.tsx contains any no-overlap warning banner, 'Make Dad optional' button, or 'Widen to two weeks' button. The Step type in page.tsx (line 42) has no 'no-overlap' case. No noOverlap state variable exists in either component.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx lines 207–223. Web: FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 1–482 (no overlap logic); page.tsx line 42 (Step type: 'setup'|'computing'|'results'|'sent', no overlap case).

findings.title: Web: date window shows raw date inputs alongside summary row — no chevron-right on row, design shows single tappable row only
findings.designSays: DateWindow component shows one tappable row: calendar-range icon, formatted range text, chevron-right — tapping opens a date picker. No visible date input fields in the design frame.
findings.implementationSays: FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 426–464 renders a summary row (lines 428–436) without a chevron-right icon, followed immediately by a 2-column grid of native <input type='date'> pickers (lines 437–457), making both the summary row and raw inputs visible simultaneously. The row is also not interactive (no click handler).
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx lines 91–99 (DateWindow with chevron-right, no raw inputs). Web: FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 426–464 (no chevron-right, two date inputs always visible). iOS: FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 417–462 (tappable row with chevron-right opens a date sheet — matches design).

findings.title: Android: top bar uses back chevron (chevron-left) — design SheetBar and iOS both use a close/dismiss control
findings.designSays: SheetBar does not render a leading back chevron — it is sheet/modal chrome with no explicit leading icon. iOS implements this as FormShell(leading: .close) which renders an X dismiss button.
findings.implementationSays: FtTopBar (FindATimeComponents.kt lines 56–103) always renders a leading chevron-left back icon (line 76). This diverges from both the design and from iOS (FormShell leading: .close = X button at line 61 of FindATimeSetupView.swift).
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx lines 121–122 (SheetBar, no leading). iOS: FindATimeSetupView.swift line 61 (leading: .close = X button, dismiss() at line 66). Android: FindATimeComponents.kt lines 63–77 (PantopusIcon.ChevronLeft always rendered).

findings.title: Web: custom duration uses a number input + 'minutes' label instead of a stepper widget
findings.designSays: Duration section uses a Stepper component (−  value  +) when 'Custom' is selected.
findings.implementationSays: FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 409–423 renders a plain <input type='number'> with a 'minutes' text label when custom duration is selected. No stepper control. iOS (FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 392–413) and Android (FindATimeSetupScreen.kt lines 210–228) both implement the stepper widget.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-setup-frames.jsx line 7 (Stepper in component set). Web: FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 409–423 (number input). iOS: FindATimeSetupView.swift lines 392–413. Android: FindATimeSetupScreen.kt lines 210–228.

screens.crossPlatformNotes: iOS is closest to design: it implements the no-overlap banner + quick-fix buttons, the 64dp arc + shimmer rows in computing state, the tappable date-window row with chevron-right opening a sheet, the FormShell close (X) leading control, and the stepper for custom duration. Android matches iOS on the stepper and date-window tappable row, but is missing the entire no-overlap state (banner + quick-fix buttons) that iOS has; its chevron-left back icon diverges from iOS's close button and from the design's sheet chrome. Web diverges most: no-overlap state absent on both the setup form and page level, computing state uses Loader2 instead of the arc+shimmer design, date window exposes raw inputs instead of a tappable row, CTA is a bottom 'Find times' button instead of the top-bar 'Next', and custom duration uses a number input instead of a stepper. The no-overlap state is the largest shared gap (Android and Web both miss it; iOS has it).
screens.verifierSummary: All 6 draft findings were independently confirmed by reading the cited files. No finding was refuted or downgraded on substance. One correction was made: the top-bar finding was upgraded from nit to minor and its scope changed from design-parity to cross-platform, because reading iOS (FormShell leading: .close at FindATimeSetupView.swift line 61) shows Android's chevron-left diverges from both the design AND iOS — not just a platform-convention nit. Evidence lines for all findings were verified and are accurate. Two additional clear findings were added that the auditor missed: (1) Web custom duration uses a plain number input instead of a stepper widget (FindATimeSetup.tsx lines 409–423), which both iOS and Android implement correctly; (2) the web DateWindow summary row (FindATimeSetup.tsx line 428–436) is missing the chevron-right icon present in the design — this is captured within the existing date-window finding but called out explicitly in the evidence. The Android no-overlap finding was strengthened by confirming iOS has the full implementation (banner, Make-optional button, Widen-window button) that Android is missing, despite the VM already having the makeSomeoneOptional() and widenWindow() methods wired up.

findings.title: Web: 'computing' state uses a spinner instead of shimmer skeleton cards
findings.designSays: Frame 1 (Composing): SubHead present + text overlay 'Finding times that work for everyone / Composing Mom, Dad and Ava' + 4 shimmer skeleton cards (Card + two Shimmer rows each)
findings.implementationSays: page.tsx:222-239 renders a single centered <Loader2 animate-spin> spinner with heading 'Checking everyone's availability' — no shimmer skeleton cards, no SubHead strip
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-slots-frames.jsx:71-87 (FrameComposing, SlotBody with 4 shimmer Cards). Web: page.tsx:222-239 (Loader2 animate-spin only). Confirmed by direct read of both files.

findings.title: Web: 'sent' state uses wrong icon (<Users> instead of check), inverts CTA hierarchy, and inserts ShareLink widget in place of standalone 'View responses' button
findings.designSays: Frame 5 (Sent): radial-gradient circle with inner green check icon (28px, strokeWidth:3); 'Back to calendar' is the PRIMARY button (PrimaryBtn); 'View responses' (icon bar-chart-3) is the SECONDARY button — no ShareLink widget
findings.implementationSays: page.tsx:267-268: uses <Users className='h-9 w-9'/> instead of a check icon. page.tsx:288-298: 'Open poll' anchor styled as primary (bg-app-home, text-white). page.tsx:299-305: 'Back to calendar' styled as secondary (border/surface). page.tsx:279-287: ShareLink widget added with poll URL. Label 'View responses' does not appear anywhere in the sent state.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-slots-frames.jsx:147-166 (FrameSent). Web: page.tsx:265-308. Confirmed icon import at page.tsx:13 (Users from lucide-react, no Check in sent state).

findings.title: Web: sticky footer adds an 'Edit' button alongside 'Send proposal', overriding design's single full-width secondary button
findings.designSays: Frame 2 (Results): StickyFooter contains a single SecondaryBtn 'Send proposal to members' spanning full width; all frames place an 'Edit' trailing action in the TopBar, never in the footer

findings.evidence: Design: findtime-slots-frames.jsx:102 (StickyFooter SecondaryBtn) and findtime-slots-frames.jsx:73,93 (TopBar right={text:'Edit'}). Web: SuggestedSlots.tsx:270-289. Confirmed by direct read.

findings.title: Web: 'Suggested times' top-bar title and trailing 'Edit' action absent from results step
findings.designSays: All five frames include TopBar with title 'Suggested times' and a trailing 'Edit' text action (muted in Composing, normal in Results/Single/No-overlap)
findings.implementationSays: SuggestedSlots.tsx contains no TopBar element. page.tsx:186-207 renders a persistent page header with title 'Find a time' (not 'Suggested times') and a back-link — this header does not change between setup and results steps. 'Suggested times' never appears as a title in the results step.
findings.evidence: Design: findtime-slots-frames.jsx:74,93,111,131,151 (TopBar in all 5 frames). Web: SuggestedSlots.tsx (no TopBar), page.tsx:199-202 (h1='Find a time' persists through all steps). Confirmed by direct read.

findings.title: Android: FtTopBar 'Edit' trailing action navigates back instead of opening a setup edit sheet

findings.implementationSays: SuggestedSlotsScreen.kt:121: onTrailing = onBack — tapping 'Edit' calls the back lambda, navigating entirely away from the results screen rather than opening an edit sheet/dialog
findings.evidence: Android: SuggestedSlotsScreen.kt:117-122 (FtTopBar call with onTrailing = onBack). iOS: FindATimeSuggestedView.swift:37-43 (Button sets showEdit=true) and lines 55-64 (.sheet(isPresented: $showEdit) presenting FindATimeSetupView inside NavigationStack). Confirmed by direct read of both files.

findings.title: Android: composing subtitle is a static string; iOS correctly uses dynamic member names per design
findings.designSays: Frame 1 (Composing): subtitle reads 'Composing Mom, Dad and Ava' — names of the selected members rendered dynamically
findings.implementationSays: SuggestedSlotsScreen.kt:438: hardcoded 'Overlaying everyone's availability' with no member names. iOS: FindATimeSuggestedViewModel.swift:81-90 generates 'Composing [member name list]' dynamically matching the design intent.
findings.evidence: Android: SuggestedSlotsScreen.kt:437-442 (ComposingBody, static text). iOS: FindATimeSuggestedViewModel.swift:79-90 (composingSubtitle computed var). Design: findtime-slots-frames.jsx:79 ('Composing Mom, Dad and Ava').
